Business justification: Each service enrollment is governed by a specific SLA definition determining response times, missed pickup credits, and escalation thresholds. WM operations and customer service teams need this link t']
  sla_term_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.sla_term. Business justification: Each service enrollment is subject to specific SLA terms governing response times and service frequency guarantees. Real-time SLA breach monitoring and penalty calculation require direct enrollment-to']
  stop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to collection.stop. Business justification: Service enrollments define ongoing service commitments that map to specific recurring stops on routes. This link enables operational execution, capacity planning, and route optimization by connecting ']
  training_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.training_requirement. Business justification: Hazardous waste service enrollments require customer personnel to meet specific training requirements (RCRA personnel training, DOT hazmat training). Linking service_enrollment to training_requirement']
  waste_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.waste_profile. Business justification: Recurring hazmat service enrollments specify approved waste profiles defining authorized waste codes, treatment requirements, TSDF destinations, and handling procedures. Essential for service authoriz']
  access_restriction_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service address for this enrollment has access restrictions (e.g., gated community, locked enclosure, restricted hours, weight-limited road). True = driver must follow special access procedures documented in special_instructions. Used for route planning and driver briefing.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service enrollment is configured to automatically renew upon reaching the enrollment end date. True = auto-renews per contract terms; False = requires explicit renewal action. Relevant for commercial and municipal contract management.']
  cancellation_date date [note: 'The date on which the service enrollment was formally cancelled. Distinct from enrollment_end_date, which may reflect a scheduled end; cancellation_date records the actual date the cancellation was processed. Used for churn reporting and final billing cutoff.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'The stated reason for cancellation or termination of the service enrollment. Populated when enrollment_status transitions to \'cancelled\'. Common reasons include customer relocation, competitor switch, non-payment, service dissatisfaction, or contract expiry. Used for churn analysis and customer retention reporting.']
  container_count varchar [note: 'The number of containers of the specified type and size assigned to this service enrollment. Commercial accounts may have multiple dumpsters at a single service address. Used for billing (per-container charges) and route capacity planning.']
  container_size_gallons varchar [note: 'The volumetric capacity of the assigned container in US gallons. Standard residential cart sizes include 32, 64, and 96 gallons. Commercial dumpsters range from 96 gallons to several cubic yards (converted). Used for capacity planning, route load calculations, and billing rate determination.']
  container_type varchar [note: 'The type of waste container assigned to this service enrollment. Cart = residential wheeled bin; dumpster = commercial front-end loader (FEL) or rear-end loader (REL) container; roll_off = open-top roll-off box; compactor = stationary or portable compactor unit; toter = smaller wheeled bin; bag = bag-based service. Aligns with Container Identification (CID) in AMCS Platform.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this service enrollment record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Represents the business event of enrollment initiation. Used for audit trail, SLA measurement, and data lineage tracking per SOX compliance requirements.']
  diversion_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The contractually or operationally agreed target diversion rate (percentage of waste diverted from landfill) for this service enrollment. Applicable primarily to recycling and organics enrollments. Used for sustainability reporting and customer-facing environmental impact dashboards.']
  enrollment_end_date date [note: 'The date on which the service enrollment is scheduled to end or was terminated. Null for open-ended recurring service agreements. Populated upon cancellation, contract expiry, or service completion.']
  enrollment_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for the service enrollment, used in customer communications, Waste Logics portal, and Salesforce CRM service records. Format: SE- followed by 8–12 digits.']
  enrollment_start_date date [note: 'The date on which the service enrollment becomes effective and service delivery begins. Used for billing cycle initiation, route assignment, and SLA tracking.']
  enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the service enrollment. \'active\' = service is being delivered; \'pending\' = enrollment initiated but not yet started; \'suspended\' = temporarily halted (e.g., customer request, non-payment); \'cancelled\' = terminated before or during service; \'completed\' = service period ended normally.']
  hazardous_waste_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service enrollment involves the handling, collection, or disposal of RCRA-regulated hazardous waste materials. True = hazardous waste service requiring EPA manifest tracking, TSDF routing, and DOT compliance. Triggers additional regulatory workflows in Enviance EHS.']
  last_service_date date [note: 'The date on which the most recent service event (pickup, collection, or delivery) was completed for this enrollment. Updated after each confirmed service completion in AMCS Platform or Locus Dispatch. Used for SLA compliance monitoring and missed pickup detection.']
  next_scheduled_service_date date [note: 'The date on which the next service event is scheduled for this enrollment based on the service frequency and route schedule. Derived from the route schedule but stored here for operational convenience and customer-facing portal display in Waste Logics.']
  origination_channel varchar [note: 'The channel through which this service enrollment was initiated. web_portal = customer self-service via Waste Logics portal; call_center = inbound customer service call; field_sales = direct sales representative; crm = Salesforce CRM workflow; edi = electronic data interchange from commercial account; municipal_contract = government contract award.']
  permit_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a regulatory permit (e.g., EPA, state environmental agency, local solid waste authority) is required to perform the service under this enrollment. True = permit must be on file and valid before service commences. Tracked in Enviance EHS permit management module.']
  salesforce_enrollment_ref varchar [note: 'The Salesforce CRM record ID corresponding to this service enrollment, enabling cross-system traceability between the Databricks lakehouse silver layer and the Salesforce CRM system of record. Standard Salesforce IDs are 15 or 18 alphanumeric characters.']
  service_day_of_week varchar [note: 'The scheduled day(s) of the week on which service is performed for this enrollment. For multi-day schedules, stores the primary service day; additional days captured in the route schedule. Used by AMCS Platform and Locus Dispatch for route planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Monday|Tuesday|Wednesday|Thursday|Friday|Saturday|Sunday — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  service_level varchar [note: 'The tier of service provided under this enrollment, defining the Service Level Agreement (SLA) commitments such as response time for missed pickups, customer support priority, and on-call availability. Standard = baseline SLA; Premium = enhanced SLA with guaranteed response windows; Basic = minimal SLA.']
  sla_response_hours varchar [note: 'The maximum number of hours within which a missed pickup or service failure must be remediated per the Service Level Agreement (SLA) for this enrollment. Drives customer service escalation workflows and compliance reporting.']
  special_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing any special handling, access, or placement instructions for the service address associated with this enrollment. Examples: gate codes, locked enclosure access, placement at curb vs. alley, fragile materials, or driver safety notes. Displayed to drivers in Locus Dispatch.']
  suspension_end_date date [note: 'The date on which a suspended service enrollment is scheduled to resume or was reactivated. Null when enrollment is not suspended or suspension is indefinite. Used for route reinstatement and billing resumption.']
  suspension_start_date date [note: 'The date on which the service enrollment was placed in a suspended state (e.g., seasonal hold, customer-requested pause, non-payment hold). Null when enrollment is not suspended. Used for billing adjustments and route management.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp of the most recent modification to this service enrollment record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Updated on any status change, rate adjustment, container change, or schedule modification. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks lakehouse pipeline and audit compliance.']
  waste_logics_enrollment_ref varchar [note: 'The enrollment or service record identifier from the Waste Logics customer portal and service scheduling system. Enables cross-system reconciliation between the lakehouse and the Waste Logics operational system of record for container management and service scheduling.']

  Note: 'Records the active enrollment of a customer account at a specific service address for a defined waste management service (e.g., residential MSW cart pickup, commercial FEL dumpster service, roll-off container rental, recycling curbside). Captures enrollment start and end dates, service frequency (weekly, bi-weekly, on-call), container type and size, service day(s), enrollment status, and the originating contract or service agreement reference. This is the operational link between a customer account and the services they receive — distinct from the contract (owned by the contract domain).'
}

Table customer.service_request {
  service_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each customer service request record in the Waste Management platform. Primary key for the service_request data product.']
  asset_container_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific waste container (bin, dumpster, roll-off, cart) associated with this service request. Relevant for container swap, repair, delivery, or removal requests. Sourced from Waste Logics Container Management or AMCS Container Tracking.']
  assigned_route_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the collection route to which this service request has been assigned for fulfillment. Populated when the request requires a field dispatch (e.g., bulk pickup, extra pickup, missed pickup). Sourced from AMCS Platform or Locus Dispatch.']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: A service request is submitted by a specific contact person (e.g., the billing contact or on-site contact for a commercial account). service_request currently has customer_account_id but no contact_id']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the active service contract or service agreement under which this request is being processed. Nullable for one-time or non-contracted service requests.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account (residential, commercial, industrial, or municipal) who initiated this service request. Links to the customer master record.']
  driver_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.driver. Business justification: Dispatch operations assign a specific driver to fulfill each service request. Driver accountability for missed pickups, service quality, and regulatory compliance requires knowing which driver execute']
  ehs_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.ehs_incident. Business justification: Service requests for spill cleanup, emergency waste removal, or incident response are generated from EHS incidents. Linking service_request to ehs_incident enables incident response time tracking, reg']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.fixed_asset. Business justification: Service requests for compactor malfunctions, baler breakdowns, or installed equipment repairs must reference the specific fixed_asset. Existing asset_container_id covers portable containers only. WM d']
  hazmat_service_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.service_order. Business justification: A customer hazmat service_request is fulfilled by creating a hazmat service_order. Linking request to fulfilling order enables end-to-end request-to-fulfillment tracking, SLA measurement, and billing ']
  inbound_load_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to recycling.inbound_load. Essential for root cause an']

Linking to the regu']

Business justification: Service requests for missed pickups, service complaints, or quality issues reference specific service dates.   segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_segment. Business justification: Normalize denormalized customer_segment STRING field to FK reference.   violation_notice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.violation_notice.   waste_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.waste_profile.   waste_stream_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.service_waste_stream. Business justification: Service requests specify waste stream to determine proper handling procedures, required equipment, routing to appropriate facility, manifest requirements, and pricing. Essential for work order generat']
  billing_status varchar [note: 'Indicates the billing lifecycle status for any additional charges associated with this service request. Not applicable for requests with no additional charges. Pending when charges are identified but not yet invoiced. Sourced from Oracle Revenue Management.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Standardized reason code explaining why the service request was cancelled. Populated only when request_status is \'cancelled\'. Used for operational analysis and customer experience improvement.']
  cancelled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the service request was cancelled, either by the customer or by operations. Nullable for non-cancelled requests. Used for cancellation rate analysis and customer churn indicators.']
  case_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable case number assigned to the service request, used in customer communications and cross-system tracking. Sourced from Salesforce CRM Case Number or Waste Logics ticket reference.']
  channel_source varchar [note: 'The intake channel through which the customer submitted the service request. Used for channel analytics, staffing optimization, and digital adoption tracking. Sourced from Salesforce CRM Case Origin or Waste Logics submission metadata.']
  completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the service request was marked as fully completed and fulfilled. Used to calculate actual resolution time, SLA adherence, and operational performance metrics.']
  container_cid varchar [note: 'The physical Container Identification (CID) number or RFID tag identifier printed on or embedded in the waste container. Used for field verification and container tracking without requiring a system lookup.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the service request record was first created in the data platform. Serves as the system audit creation timestamp, distinct from the business event submitted_timestamp.']
  customer_satisfaction_score varchar [note: 'Post-resolution customer satisfaction rating provided by the customer via survey or feedback mechanism. Typically on a 1-5 or 1-10 scale. Contributes to Net Promoter Score (NPS) and service quality analytics.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service request has been escalated to a supervisor, manager, or specialized team due to complexity, customer dissatisfaction, regulatory concern, or SLA breach risk. Used for escalation rate KPI reporting.']
  escalation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded reason explaining why the service request was escalated. Populated only when escalation_flag is True. Examples include repeated missed pickups, regulatory compliance concern, or high-value customer complaint.']
  estimated_weight_tons decimal(10,4) [note: 'Estimated weight in tons of the waste material associated with the service request, as provided by the customer or estimated by the intake agent. Relevant for bulk pickup, extra pickup, and C&D requests. Used for route load planning and tipping fee estimation.']
  item_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual bulk items or containers included in the service request. Applicable for bulk item pickup requests where discrete item counts drive truck capacity planning and labor estimation.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the service request, used to determine SLA tier, dispatch urgency, and resource allocation. Critical priority is reserved for health/safety or regulatory compliance situations (e.g., hazardous waste spill, missed medical waste pickup).']
  regulatory_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service request has regulatory compliance implications, such as hazardous waste handling, RCRA-regulated material pickup, or EPA-reportable incident. When True, triggers compliance workflow and Enviance EHS notification.']
  request_status varchar [note: 'Current fulfillment lifecycle status of the service request. Tracks progression from initial submission through scheduling, active fulfillment, and final resolution or cancellation.']
  request_subtype_code varchar [note: 'Secondary classification providing additional granularity within the request type. For example, under CONTAINER_SWAP: UPSIZE, DOWNSIZE, DAMAGE_REPLACEMENT. Sourced from Salesforce CRM Case Sub-Type.']
  request_type_code varchar [note: 'Categorical code identifying the nature of the customer service request. Drives workflow routing, SLA assignment, and fulfillment process. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: BULK_PICKUP|EXTRA_PICKUP|MISSED_PICKUP|CONTAINER_SWAP|CONTAINER_REPAIR|START_SERVICE|STOP_SERVICE|BILLING_INQUIRY|HAZMAT_INQUIRY|RECYCLING_INQUIRY|SCHEDULE_CHANGE|OTHER — promote to reference product]']
  requested_service_date date [note: 'The date on which the customer has requested the service action to be performed. May differ from the scheduled or actual fulfillment date. Used for scheduling and customer expectation management.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Operational notes documenting how the service request was resolved, including actions taken, exceptions encountered, and any follow-up required. Entered by the field crew, dispatcher, or customer service agent upon completion.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'The date on which the service request has been formally scheduled for fulfillment by the operations team. May differ from the customer-requested date based on route availability and resource constraints.']
  scheduled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the service request was formally scheduled in the dispatch system. Captures the scheduling event for audit trail and SLA milestone tracking.']
  service_frequency_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this service request represents a one-time ad hoc service action or a recurring add-on to an existing service schedule. Distinguishes episodic requests from schedule modifications.']
  service_request_description varchar [note: 'Full narrative description of the service request as provided by the customer or intake agent. Contains details about the specific issue, location, container, or service need. Sourced from Salesforce CRM Case Description or Waste Logics portal notes.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service request was not fulfilled within the defined SLA due date. True when the completion timestamp exceeds the sla_due_date or the request remains open past the due date. Used for SLA compliance reporting and KPI tracking.']
  sla_due_date date [note: 'The contractual or policy-defined deadline by which the service request must be fulfilled to meet the applicable Service Level Agreement (SLA). Calculated from submitted_timestamp based on request type and priority tier.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the originating operational system from which this service request record was sourced. Used for data lineage tracking, reconciliation, and system-of-record attribution in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer.']
  source_system_case_reference varchar [note: 'The native case or ticket identifier from the originating source system (e.g., Salesforce CRM Case ID or Waste Logics Ticket ID). Enables traceability back to the system of record for reconciliation and audit purposes.']
  subject varchar [note: 'Short, descriptive title or subject line summarizing the nature of the service request, as entered by the customer or agent. Sourced from Salesforce CRM Case Subject field.']
  submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the customer submitted the service request. This is the principal business event timestamp representing when the request entered the system. Used as the baseline for SLA calculation.']
  tipping_fee_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a tipping fee (charge per ton for waste disposal at a landfill or transfer station) applies to this service request. True for requests involving disposal charges beyond standard service contract terms.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the service request record was most recently modified in the data platform. Used for incremental data pipeline processing and change tracking.']
  waste_stream_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the waste stream associated with the service request. Municipal Solid Waste (MSW), recycling, organics, Construction and Demolition (C&D), hazardous, bulk items, or yard waste. Drives routing, compliance requirements, and disposal method. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: msw|recycling|organics|c_and_d|hazardous|bulk|yard_waste — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Customer-initiated requests for service actions outside of scheduled recurring collection — including bulk item pickup, extra pickup requests, missed pickup complaints, container swap/exchange, container repair, start/stop service requests, and billing inquiries. Each request has a type code, priority level, requested service date, fulfillment status (open, scheduled, completed, cancelled), resolution notes, SLA due date, and linked service address. Sourced from Salesforce CRM Cases and Waste Logics portal submissions. Distinct from recurring service enrollments.'
}

Table customer.container_assignment {
  container_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each container assignment record in the Waste Management lakehouse silver layer. Serves as the primary key for this entity.']
  asset_container_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical container asset record in the container inventory. Bridges the customer domain with the asset management domain for container utilization tracking.']
  billing_rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_rate_schedule. Business justification: Container rental fees are governed by billing rate schedules.   container_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.container_type.   contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service contract or agreement under which this container is assigned. Links the assignment to the governing billing and service terms.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account (residential, commercial, industrial, or municipal) to whom the container is assigned. Links the container assignment to the customer master record.']
  hazardous_waste_generator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.hazardous_waste_generator. Business justification: Hazardous waste container assignments must be tied to the EPA-registered generator for accumulation time limit enforcement, container labeling compliance, and RCRA inspection readiness. Regulatory aud']
  material_stream_waste_stream_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.service_waste_stream. Business justification: Container assignments designate which waste stream the container accepts for contamination tracking, diversion rate calculation, proper disposal routing, and regulatory reporting. Critical for recycli']
  pm_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.pm_schedule. Business justification: Containers assigned to customers require preventive maintenance schedules for cleaning, repair, and regulatory inspections. Real business process: container PM programs track maintenance cycles based ']
  primary_container_delivery_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Work Order (WO) created in AMCS Platform or SAP PM module to execute the container delivery. Enables traceability from the assignment record back to the field service event.']
  prior_assignment_container_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the previous container assignment at this service address that this record replaces (e.g., after a swap or upgrade). Null for original assignments. Enables container assignment history chain reconstruction.']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to recycling.recycling_program. Business justification: Containers assigned for recycling service belong to a specific recycling program. Operations teams need program-level container fleet reporting, contamination tracking by program, and container retrie']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Container assignments for hazardous waste are governed by specific regulatory requirements (RCRA container standards, DOT packaging requirements). container_assignment has epa_waste_code and regulator']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the collection route to which this container assignment is assigned for regular service. Enables route-level container audits, utilization analysis, and operational planning.']
  service_address_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific service address where the container is physically placed. A customer may have multiple service addresses, each with independent container assignments.']
  service_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.service_enrollment. Business justification: A container assignment is made in the context of a specific service enrollment — the physical container (cart, dumpster, roll-off) is assigned to fulfill a particular enrolled service at a service add']
  stop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to collection.stop. Business justification: Container assignments must reference the recurring stop on the route for operational planning, driver instructions, and service scheduling. Operations teams need to know which stop corresponds to each']
  waste_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.waste_profile. Business justification: Hazardous waste container assignments must reference the approved waste profile to validate the container is authorized for the specific waste type, ensuring RCRA accumulation compliance. epa_waste_co']
  assignment_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable business identifier for this container assignment. Used in customer communications, work orders, and field operations. Format: CA- followed by 8-12 digits.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the container assignment. \'active\' = container is at the service address and in service; \'pending_delivery\' = scheduled for delivery; \'pending_removal\' = scheduled for pickup; \'lost\' = container cannot be located; \'damaged\' = container reported damaged; \'swapped\' = replaced by another container. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|pending_delivery|pending_removal|lost|damaged|swapped|suspended|expired — promote to reference product]']
  cid varchar [note: 'Container Identification (CID) number — the industry-standard physical identifier stamped or affixed to the container asset. Used for field identification, route scanning, and container audits. Sourced from AMCS Platform container tracking.']
  collection_day_of_week varchar [note: 'Scheduled day(s) of the week on which the container is collected. For multi-day schedules, stores the primary collection day. Drives route assignment in AMCS Platform and Locus Dispatch. Sunday collection is not standard in most markets.']
  collection_frequency varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency at which the container is serviced (emptied) under this assignment. Drives route scheduling in AMCS Platform and Locus Dispatch. \'on_call\' = customer requests pickup as needed (common for roll-offs).']
  condition_at_delivery varchar [note: 'Physical condition of the container at the time it was delivered to the service address. Recorded by the driver or dispatcher at delivery. Used for warranty tracking, asset lifecycle management, and customer dispute resolution.']
  condition_at_removal varchar [note: 'Physical condition of the container at the time it was removed from the service address. Recorded by the driver or dispatcher at pickup. Supports damage assessment, customer liability determination, and asset refurbishment decisions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this container assignment record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Represents the business event of assignment initiation. Sourced from AMCS Platform or Waste Logics.']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'Business-to-consumer or business-to-business segment classification of the customer receiving this container assignment. \'residential\' = B2C household; \'commercial\' = B2B business account; \'industrial\' = large-volume industrial client; \'municipal\' = government/municipal contract.']
  damage_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any damage observed on the container at delivery or during the assignment period. Populated when condition_at_delivery or condition_at_removal indicates damage. Supports insurance claims and customer billing for damage charges.']
  delivery_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the container was physically delivered to the service address and the assignment became active. Sourced from the completed delivery work order in AMCS Platform or Waste Logics.']
  enclosure_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the container must be placed within a screened enclosure or enclosure structure per local municipal ordinance, HOA requirements, or customer contract terms. True = enclosure required; False = open placement permitted.']
  last_serviced_date date [note: 'Most recent calendar date on which the container was collected/emptied under this assignment. Updated after each successful collection event. Used for service verification, missed pickup detection, and customer service inquiries.']
  lock_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the container requires a lock mechanism for security or regulatory compliance (e.g., hazardous waste containers, high-theft areas, or customer-requested security). True = lock required; False = no lock required.']
  material_stream varchar [note: 'Waste material stream designated for this container assignment. \'msw\' = Municipal Solid Waste (MSW) general refuse; \'recycling\' = commingled recyclables for Materials Recovery Facility (MRF) processing; \'organics\' = food/organic waste; \'cd_waste\' = Construction and Demolition (C&D) debris; \'hazardous\' = regulated hazardous waste; \'yard_waste\' = landscaping and green waste.']
  missed_pickup_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of missed or skipped scheduled collection events for this container assignment since delivery. Used for SLA compliance monitoring, customer satisfaction tracking, and NPS correlation analysis.']
  overage_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer\'s service agreement permits overage (excess waste placed beside or on top of the container beyond its rated capacity). True = overage collection is permitted and may trigger additional charges; False = overage is not collected.']
  placement_latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'GPS latitude coordinate of the container\'s physical placement location at the service address. Enables precise geolocation for driver navigation, container audits, and lost container recovery. Sourced from Geotab Fleet Telematics or AMCS Platform.']
  placement_location varchar [note: 'Descriptive text indicating the specific placement location of the container at the service address (e.g., \'curbside\', \'back alley\', \'loading dock bay 3\', \'inside gate\'). Used by drivers for accurate container location during collection.']
  placement_longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'GPS longitude coordinate of the container\'s physical placement location at the service address. Enables precise geolocation for driver navigation, container audits, and lost container recovery. Sourced from Geotab Fleet Telematics or AMCS Platform.']
  regulatory_manifest_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Hazardous Waste Manifest (tracking document) is required for the material stream in this container assignment per RCRA regulations. True = manifest required (hazardous waste); False = standard MSW/recycling, no manifest required.']
  removal_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the container was physically removed from the service address, ending the active assignment. Null if the assignment is still active. Used for container utilization duration calculations and billing period closure.']
  rfid_tag varchar [note: 'Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) tag identifier embedded in or affixed to the container. Enables automated container scanning during collection events without manual barcode scanning. Null if container is not RFID-equipped.']
  scheduled_delivery_date date [note: 'Originally planned calendar date for container delivery to the service address. Compared against actual delivery_date to measure delivery SLA compliance and route scheduling accuracy.']
  scheduled_removal_date date [note: 'Originally planned calendar date for container removal from the service address. Compared against actual removal_date to measure removal SLA compliance. Populated when a removal work order is created.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the container assignment supports a recurring scheduled service, an on-call demand service, a temporary placement (e.g., event or construction project), or a one-time service request.']
  size_cubic_yards decimal(10,2) [note: 'Nominal capacity of the container expressed in cubic yards. Used for commercial dumpsters, roll-offs, and compactors (e.g., 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 20, 30, 40 cubic yards). Null for containers measured in gallons. Supports billing, route planning, and capacity management.']
  size_gallons decimal(10,2) [note: 'Nominal capacity of the container expressed in US gallons. Used for residential carts and toters (e.g., 32, 64, 96 gallons). Null for containers measured in cubic yards. Supports container utilization analysis and route weight estimation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this container assignment record originated. Supports data lineage tracking and conflict resolution in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer integration pipeline.']
  swap_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why this container assignment was created as a replacement for a prior assignment. Null if this is an original assignment (not a swap). Supports container lifecycle analysis and operational efficiency reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this container assignment record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change detection, data synchronization, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks silver layer.']

  Note: 'Tracks the assignment of physical waste containers (carts, dumpsters, roll-offs, compactors) identified by CID/RFID to specific customer service addresses. Records container type, size (gallons/cubic yards), material stream (MSW, recycling, organics, C&D), delivery date, pickup/removal date, current assignment status (active, pending removal, lost, damaged), and container condition at assignment. Bridges the customer domain with the asset domain\'s container inventory. Enables container utilization tracking and customer-level container audits.'
}

Table customer.communication_preference {
  communication_preference_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each communication preference record. Primary key for the communication_preference data product in the customer domain.']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific contact person within the customer account for whom these preferences apply. Supports multi-contact accounts (e.g., commercial accounts with multiple authorized contacts).']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account for whom this communication preference record applies. Links to the master customer entity in the customer domain.']
  billing_alert_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has opted in to receive billing-related alerts such as invoice generation, payment due reminders, and payment confirmation notifications. Supports Oracle Revenue Management billing communication workflows.']
  channel varchar [note: 'The preferred communication channel through which the customer or contact wishes to receive outreach. Supports omnichannel engagement across email, SMS, phone, physical mail, the Waste Logics customer portal, and mobile push notifications.']
  consent_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the consent document, contract clause, or recorded call that evidences the consent. For paper forms, this is the document scan ID; for contracts, this is the contract number and clause reference; for recorded calls, this is the call recording ID.']
  consent_expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the recorded consent expires and must be re-obtained. Applicable where regulatory or business policy requires periodic consent renewal. Null indicates consent does not expire under current policy.']
  consent_ip_address varchar [note: 'The IP address from which the customer submitted their consent (e.g., via the Waste Logics customer portal or email signup form). Provides technical evidence of consent origin for regulatory audit purposes. Classified as confidential PII as IP addresses may identify individuals in some jurisdictions.']
  consent_method varchar [note: 'The legal mechanism by which consent was obtained. \'express_written\' is required for TCPA-regulated SMS and autodialed calls. \'double_opt_in\' provides the strongest evidence of consent for email marketing. Drives compliance reporting and legal defensibility.']
  consent_source varchar [note: 'The system or touchpoint through which consent was captured. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: customer_portal|salesforce_crm|paper_form|ivr|mobile_app|service_call|email_signup|contract_signature|third_party — promote to reference product]']
  consent_status varchar [note: 'Current regulatory consent status for the specified channel and notification type. \'granted\' indicates valid consent is on file; \'revoked\' indicates consent was withdrawn; \'pending\' indicates awaiting confirmation (double opt-in); \'expired\' indicates consent has lapsed per retention policy; \'not_required\' applies to transactional communications exempt from consent requirements.']
  consent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the customer or contact granted, revoked, or updated consent for this channel and notification type. Critical audit field for TCPA and CAN-SPAM compliance. Stored in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset.']
  contact_frequency varchar [note: 'The customer or contact\'s preferred frequency for non-transactional outreach (e.g., promotional offers, NPS surveys, service updates). Transactional and compliance-driven communications (e.g., billing alerts, regulatory notices) are not subject to this frequency cap.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this communication preference record was first created in the system. Immutable audit field used for compliance reporting, data lineage, and preference history analysis.']
  do_not_call boolean [note: 'Specific suppression flag for telephone-based outreach (voice calls). Distinct from the master do_not_contact flag to allow granular channel-level suppression. Compliance with the National Do Not Call Registry and TCPA requirements.']
  do_not_contact boolean [note: 'Master suppression flag indicating the customer or contact must not be contacted through any channel. Overrides all individual channel and notification preferences. Must be honored across all outbound communication systems including Salesforce CRM, Oracle Revenue Management, and Waste Logics portal.']
  do_not_email boolean [note: 'Specific suppression flag for email-based outreach. Distinct from the master do_not_contact flag to allow granular channel-level suppression. Compliance with CAN-SPAM Act unsubscribe requirements.']
  do_not_mail boolean [note: 'Specific suppression flag for physical postal mail outreach. Allows customers to opt out of paper-based communications such as billing statements, promotional mailers, and service notices.']
  do_not_sms boolean [note: 'Specific suppression flag for SMS/text message outreach. Required for TCPA compliance as SMS requires prior express written consent. Distinct from the master do_not_contact flag.']
  effective_from date [note: 'The date from which this communication preference record is effective and should be applied to outbound communications. Supports temporal preference management and scheduled preference changes (e.g., a customer requesting preferences to take effect at the start of next billing cycle).']
  effective_until date [note: 'The date until which this communication preference record is effective. Null indicates the preference is open-ended with no planned expiry. Used for temporary preference overrides (e.g., vacation holds, seasonal suppression) and consent expiry management.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this communication preference record is currently active and in effect. Set to False when a preference is superseded by a newer record or when the contact is deactivated, enabling soft-delete and historical audit without data loss.']
  language_preference varchar [note: 'The customer or contact\'s preferred language for all communications, stored as an IETF BCP 47 language tag (e.g., \'en\', \'es\', \'zh-CN\', \'fr\'). Drives language selection in templated outbound communications across email, SMS, IVR, and portal notifications.']
  last_updated_by varchar [note: 'The identifier of the user, system, or agent that last modified this preference record. May be a Salesforce CRM user ID, a Waste Logics portal session identifier, or a system process name (e.g., \'BULK_IMPORT\', \'IVR_SYSTEM\'). Supports audit trail and compliance investigation.']
  notification_type varchar [note: 'The category of notification for which this preference record captures opt-in or opt-out status. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: billing_alert|service_reminder|outage_notification|promotional_offer|nps_survey|collection_schedule|account_update|regulatory_notice|payment_confirmation|route_change — promote to reference product]']
  nps_survey_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has opted in to receive Net Promoter Score (NPS) and customer satisfaction surveys. Supports the customer experience measurement program and drives service improvement analytics.']
  opt_in_status varchar [note: 'Current opt-in or opt-out status of the customer or contact for the specified channel and notification type combination. \'pending\' indicates consent has been requested but not yet confirmed (e.g., double opt-in flow). \'not_set\' indicates no preference has been recorded.']
  opt_out_channel varchar [note: 'The channel or mechanism through which the customer submitted their opt-out request. Supports compliance audit trails and process improvement analysis (e.g., identifying which unsubscribe mechanisms are most used). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: email_unsubscribe|sms_stop|ivr|customer_portal|agent_request|written_request|regulatory_order — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  opt_out_reason varchar [note: 'The reason provided by the customer or contact for opting out of a communication channel or notification type. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: too_frequent|not_relevant|prefer_other_channel|unsubscribe_link|customer_request|complaint|regulatory_request|deceased — promote to reference product]']
  opt_out_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the customer or contact submitted an opt-out request for this channel or notification type. CAN-SPAM requires opt-out requests to be honored within 10 business days; this timestamp is the compliance clock start.']
  outage_notification_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has opted in to receive service disruption and outage notifications such as missed collection alerts, route delays, weather-related service suspensions, and facility closures.']
  portal_notification_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has enabled in-portal notifications within the Waste Logics customer portal. When True, notifications are surfaced within the portal dashboard in addition to or instead of external channels.']
  preference_type varchar [note: 'Categorizes the nature of this preference record: channel (preferred delivery channel), notification (opt-in/out for a notification category), language (preferred language), frequency (preferred contact cadence), or do_not_contact (suppression flag). Enables structured querying of preference records.']
  preferred_contact_time_end varchar [note: 'The end of the customer\'s preferred time window for receiving phone or SMS outreach, stored in HH:MM 24-hour format in the customer\'s local time zone. TCPA restricts calls to 8 AM–9 PM local time; this field captures the customer\'s narrower preference within that window.']
  preferred_contact_time_start varchar [note: 'The start of the customer\'s preferred time window for receiving phone or SMS outreach, stored in HH:MM 24-hour format in the customer\'s local time zone. Used by outbound dialer and SMS scheduling systems to respect contact time preferences.']
  promotional_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has opted in to receive promotional communications including new service offers, recycling program promotions, and sustainability initiative campaigns. Subject to CAN-SPAM and TCPA consent requirements.']
  regulatory_notice_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has opted in to receive regulatory and compliance-related notices such as EPA-mandated service change notifications, RCRA hazardous waste handling updates, and local solid waste authority announcements. Note: some regulatory notices may be mandatory regardless of opt-in status.']
  service_reminder_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has opted in to receive service reminders such as upcoming collection day notifications, container set-out reminders, and scheduled service confirmations. Supports AMCS Platform and Locus Dispatch customer notification workflows.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The originating system from which this preference record was captured or last updated. Enables data lineage tracking and conflict resolution when preferences are updated across multiple systems of record (Salesforce CRM, Waste Logics portal, Oracle Revenue Management). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: salesforce_crm|waste_logics|oracle_revenue_mgmt|ivr|mobile_app|paper_form|bulk_import|agent_entry — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  suppression_list_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact has been added to a global suppression list, preventing all marketing and non-transactional outreach regardless of individual channel preferences. Populated from CAN-SPAM unsubscribe lists, TCPA litigation holds, or internal suppression decisions.']
  suppression_reason varchar [note: 'The reason this contact was added to the suppression list. \'bounce\' indicates a hard email bounce; \'complaint\' indicates a spam complaint; \'litigation_hold\' indicates legal proceedings; \'regulatory\' indicates a government-mandated suppression order. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: unsubscribe|complaint|bounce|litigation_hold|regulatory|deceased|fraud — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'The IANA time zone identifier for the customer\'s or contact\'s local time zone (e.g., \'America/Chicago\', \'America/Los_Angeles\'). Used in conjunction with preferred_contact_time_start and preferred_contact_time_end to schedule compliant outreach.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this communication preference record was most recently modified. Updated on every change to any preference field. Used for change detection, synchronization with downstream systems, and compliance audit trails.']

  Note: 'Stores each customer contact\'s communication channel preferences and consent records for regulatory compliance (TCPA, CAN-SPAM). Captures preferred channels (email, SMS, phone, mail, portal), notification types opted in/out (billing alerts, service reminders, outage notifications, promotional offers, NPS surveys), language preference, do-not-contact flags, and consent timestamps with source of consent. Supports omnichannel customer engagement and ensures compliant outreach across all Waste Management customer communications.'
}

  waste_generator_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the waste generator regulatory profile record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  compliance_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_inspection. Business justification: Generator sites are subject to EPA/state RCRA compliance inspections. The waste_generator_profile tracks last_inspection_date and inspection_result as plain fields — a proper FK to the compliance_insp']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract. Business justification: Waste generator profiles are governed by a service contract specifying handling, disposal, and RCRA compliance obligations. Regulatory audits and EPA reporting require direct traceability from generat']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account that holds this waste generator regulatory profile. Links to the customer master record in the Customer domain.']

Business justification: Regulatory biennial reporting and RCRA manifest compliance require direct association between a hazardous waste generator profile and the designated WM processing/treatment facility. Environmental Ser']

Business justification: Waste generator profiles for non-hazardous and special waste generators designate a specific landfill site for disposal per franchise agreement or contract. Operations and compliance staff need this l']
  disposal_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.disposal_agreement. Business justification: RCRA compliance requires each hazardous waste generator profile to be linked to an approved disposal agreement specifying the designated TSDF, waste codes, and tipping fee terms. Manifest preparation ']

Business justification: DOT and RCRA regulations require each hazardous waste generator to use only authorized haulers under a valid hauling agreement. Generator profiles must reference the governing hauling agreement for ma']
  hazardous_waste_generator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.hazardous_waste_generator. Business justification: waste_generator_profile is the customer-domain representation of a generator site and must directly reference the EPA-registered hazardous_waste_generator for biennial report preparation, RCRA complia']
  monitoring_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.monitoring_program. Business justification: Generator sites subject to post-closure or corrective action monitoring are enrolled in monitoring programs. Linking the generator profile to its monitoring program enables compliance status tracking,']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: EPA generator profiles directly reference RCRA operating permits, state hazardous waste permits, and treatment/storage/disposal authorizations. Manifest generation, waste acceptance decisions, and reg']
  primary_waste_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.waste_profile. Business justification: A generator profile must reference its primary approved waste profile for service scheduling, container authorization, and LDR compliance verification. Role-prefix \'primary_\' used because a generator ']
  primary_waste_stream_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.service_waste_stream. Business justification: Waste generator profiles classify customers by primary waste stream for EPA/RCRA regulatory compliance, manifest generation, biennial reporting, service eligibility, and disposal pathway determination']

  regulatory_corrective_action_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_corrective_action. Environmental services compliance teams need to link the generator profile to its active c']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Generator category (Large Quantity Generator, Small Quantity Generator, CESQG) determines applicable RCRA requirements for manifesting, accumulation time limits, training, and contingency planning. Se']
  regulatory_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_submission. Business justification: Biennial reports and RCRA notifications are regulatory submissions made on behalf of specific generator profiles. The waste_generator_profile has biennial_report_due_date and last_biennial_report_date']
  service_address_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.service_address.   training_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.training_requirement. Business justification: RCRA requires hazardous waste generators to ensure personnel training. The waste_generator_profile has personnel_training_current_flag — a FK to the specific training_requirement governing that genera']
  transfer_station_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to collection.transfer_station. Business justification: Hazardous waste generators must be associated with permitted transfer stations for regulatory compliance, EPA manifest routing, RCRA reporting, and TSDF facility coordination. This link enables proper']
  tsdf_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary designated Treatment, Storage, and Disposal Facility (TSDF) used by this generator for hazardous waste disposal. The TSDF must hold a valid RCRA permit. Links to the facility/asset master in the Asset Management domain.']
  violation_notice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.violation_notice. Business justification: Violation notices (NOVs) are issued directly against waste generators for RCRA non-compliance. The waste_generator_profile tracks open_violation_flag as a plain boolean — a FK to the specific violatio']
  accumulation_area_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the on-site hazardous waste accumulation area based on RCRA generator category and applicable time limits. LQGs may accumulate up to 90 days; SQGs up to 180 days (or 270 days in rural areas); VSQGs have no time limit but volume limits apply. Satellite areas are small accumulation points feeding a central accumulation area.']
  biennial_report_due_date date [note: 'Due date for the next EPA Biennial Hazardous Waste Report submission. Required for LQGs in even-numbered years under RCRA 40 CFR Part 262.41. Tracks compliance calendar obligations for large quantity generators.']
  contingency_plan_on_file boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a RCRA-compliant emergency contingency plan is on file for this generator. LQGs are required to maintain a formal contingency plan under 40 CFR Part 262.261; SQGs must have an emergency plan. Critical for compliance verification and emergency response readiness.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this waste generator profile record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and compliance record-keeping requirements under RCRA and SOX.']
  e_manifest_registered_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this generator is registered in the EPA\'s national e-Manifest system for electronic hazardous waste tracking. The e-Manifest system replaced paper manifests and is operated by the EPA under RCRA authority.']
  emergency_coordinator_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the designated emergency coordinator responsible for implementing the contingency plan and coordinating emergency response at the generator site. Required to be listed in the contingency plan and available to local emergency responders under RCRA.']
  emergency_coordinator_phone varchar [note: '24-hour contact phone number for the designated emergency coordinator at the generator site. Must be posted at the facility and provided to local emergency response agencies as required under RCRA contingency plan regulations.']
  estimated_hazardous_volume_kg_per_month decimal(10,3) [note: 'Estimated monthly generation quantity of RCRA-regulated hazardous waste in kilograms. This is the primary metric used to classify the generator into VSQG (≤100 kg), SQG (100–999 kg), or LQG (≥1,000 kg) categories and must be re-evaluated whenever operations change.']
  estimated_monthly_volume_tons decimal(10,3) [note: 'Estimated total waste generation volume per month expressed in short tons. Used to determine and validate RCRA generator category (VSQG/SQG/LQG), plan collection frequency, and support TPD (Tons Per Day) capacity planning for routing and disposal.']
  hazardous_waste_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list of EPA hazardous waste codes (e.g., D001, F003, U080) applicable to the waste streams generated at this site as determined by TCLP (Toxicity Characteristic Leaching Procedure) testing or listed waste classification. Required for manifest preparation and RCRA compliance reporting.']
  inspection_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the most recent regulatory inspection at this generator site. Significant violations may trigger enforcement actions, fines, or mandatory corrective measures under RCRA or CERCLA authority.']
  last_biennial_report_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent EPA Biennial Hazardous Waste Report was submitted for this generator. Used to verify compliance with LQG biennial reporting obligations and to support audit documentation.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent regulatory compliance inspection conducted at this generator site by the EPA, state environmental agency, or Local Enforcement Agency (LEA). Used to track inspection history and assess compliance standing.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this waste generator profile record in the data platform. Used for change detection, incremental ETL processing, and audit trail maintenance.']
  last_notification_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent RCRA Site ID notification update submitted to the EPA or authorized state agency. Generators must re-notify when their generator category changes, when they begin generating new hazardous waste streams, or when site information changes.']
  manifest_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this generator is required to use a Uniform Hazardous Waste Manifest (EPA Form 8700-22) for off-site shipments of hazardous waste. Mandatory for SQGs and LQGs; VSQGs are exempt from manifest requirements under 40 CFR Part 262.']
  max_accumulation_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days this generator is permitted to accumulate hazardous waste on-site before it must be shipped to a TSDF, as determined by generator category and applicable RCRA regulations. Exceeding this limit triggers LQG-level requirements or potential RCRA violation.']
  naics_code varchar [note: 'Six-digit NAICS code representing the primary industry classification of the waste-generating facility. Used by EPA and state agencies to categorize generator type, assess typical waste streams, and benchmark regulatory compliance requirements.']
  next_notification_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled due date for the next required RCRA notification or re-notification. LQGs must notify every four years under the 2016 Generator Improvements Rule. Used by compliance teams to proactively manage regulatory deadlines.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for compliance officers and environmental managers to record supplementary information about the generator\'s regulatory status, special handling requirements, enforcement history, or operational context not captured in structured fields.']
  open_violation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this generator currently has one or more open, unresolved regulatory violations on record with the EPA or state environmental agency. Triggers enhanced compliance monitoring and may affect service agreements and manifest acceptance.']
  personnel_training_current_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all relevant facility personnel have completed current RCRA hazardous waste management training as required for the generator\'s category. LQGs must provide formal classroom or on-the-job training; SQGs must ensure familiarity with emergency procedures.']
  profile_effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this waste generator regulatory profile is considered valid and active. Represents the start of the current regulatory classification period, which may be triggered by initial registration, a generator category change, or a site re-notification.']
  profile_expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which this waste generator regulatory profile expires or must be renewed. Applicable when state permits or generator registrations have fixed validity periods. Null for open-ended federal EPA registrations.']
  rcra_notification_date date [note: 'Date on which the generator submitted the initial RCRA Site Identification (Site ID) notification to the EPA or authorized state agency, establishing the generator\'s regulatory identity and obligations. Required under 40 CFR Part 262.18.']
  site_name varchar [note: 'Legal or operating name of the waste-generating facility or site as registered with the EPA and state environmental agency. May differ from the customer account name if the customer operates multiple sites.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this waste generator profile was originated or last updated. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation across Enviance EHS, SAP S/4HANA EHS module, EPA RCRAInfo, Salesforce CRM, and Waste Logics.']
  source_system_profile_reference varchar [note: 'The native identifier for this waste generator profile record in the originating operational system (e.g., Enviance EHS record ID, SAP EHS business partner number, or RCRAInfo handler ID). Enables cross-system reconciliation and traceability.']
  state_permit_number varchar [note: 'State-issued environmental permit or registration number for the waste-generating facility, assigned by the applicable State Environmental Quality Department. Required for state-level RCRA-authorized program compliance and may differ from the federal EPA Generator ID.']
  state_program_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter state code identifying the RCRA-authorized state program under which this generator is regulated. In RCRA-authorized states, the state program operates in lieu of the federal program; in non-authorized states, the EPA directly administers RCRA.']
  universal_waste_handler_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this generator is registered as a universal waste handler under 40 CFR Part 273, managing batteries, pesticides, mercury-containing equipment, or lamps under the streamlined universal waste rules rather than full RCRA hazardous waste requirements.']

  Note: 'Regulatory classification profile for commercial, industrial, and hazardous waste generating customers as required under RCRA and state environmental regulations. Captures EPA Generator ID, generator category (Very Small Quantity Generator VSQG, Small Quantity Generator SQG, Large Quantity Generator LQG), primary waste streams generated (MSW, C&D, hazardous, universal waste), estimated monthly waste volume (TPD), state permit numbers, and regulatory notification dates. Required for RCRA compliance and hazardous waste manifest management.'
}

Stop-level complaint tracking enables driver performance management, repeat stop analysis, and targeted servic']
