Insurance claim processing under hauling agreement insurance_certifica']
  landfill_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to landfill.site. Business justification: Accidents occurring on landfill property require site-level OSHA/DOT incident reporting, landfill operator liability tracking, and regulatory notification to the site permit holder. Linking accident_r']
  manifest_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.manifest. Business justification: Accidents involving hazmat shipments require manifest reference for NRC reporting (40 CFR 302), spill response coordination, insurance claims, and determining if DOT reportable quantities were release']
  mrf_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to recycling.mrf_facility. Business justification: Accidents on MRF facility premises require facility-specific OSHA reporting, safety performance tracking by facility, and facility management notification. A domain expert expects accident_report to r']
  route_execution_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to collection.route_execution. Business justification: Accidents occur during specific route executions. OSHA and DOT regulatory filings, insurance claims, and safety investigations require linking accident reports to the specific operational run during w']
  vehicle_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.vehicle_assignment. Business justification: Vehicle accidents occur during specific driver-vehicle assignments. Linking accident_report to vehicle_assignment provides the full operational context of the incident — which route was being run, wha']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fleet vehicle involved in the accident. Links to the fleet vehicle master record.']
  violation_notice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.violation_notice. Business justification: Accidents with moving violations, hazmat releases, or DOT-reportable incidents generate violation notices from regulatory agencies. Risk managers link accident reports to notices for claims defense an']
  waste_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.waste_shipment. Business justification: 49 CFR 171.15/171.16 requires immediate notification and written reports for hazmat incidents. This FK links the accident report to the specific waste shipment being transported, providing shipment de']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: Post-accident vehicle repair is a named business process requiring a work order. Linking accident_report to the resulting work_order enables insurance claim reconciliation, repair cost attribution to ']
  accident_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of how the accident occurred, including sequence of events, contributing factors, and circumstances.']
  citation_description varchar [note: 'Description of the traffic violation or citation issued, including violation code and description if applicable.']
  citation_issued_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a traffic citation or ticket was issued to the driver as a result of the accident.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this accident report record was first created in the system.']
  csa_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the accident is reportable under FMCSA CSA program and will impact the carriers safety score.']
  dot_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the accident meets DOT reportability criteria (fatality, injury requiring immediate medical treatment away from scene, or disabling damage requiring tow-away).']
  driver_at_fault_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the company driver was determined to be at fault for the accident based on investigation findings.']
  estimated_property_damage_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated total dollar amount of property damage resulting from the accident, including damage to company vehicle, other vehicles, and third-party property.']
  incident_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the accident occurred.']
  incident_severity varchar [note: 'Overall severity classification of the accident based on injuries, fatalities, and property damage.']
  incident_time timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the accident occurred, including timezone information.']
  incident_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the type of accident that occurred (collision with another vehicle, rollover, backing incident, pedestrian involvement, property damage only, single vehicle incident).']
  insurance_carrier varchar [note: 'Name of the insurance company handling the claim for this accident.']
  insurance_claim_number varchar [note: 'Claim number assigned by the insurance carrier for processing the accident claim.']
  investigation_completed_date date [note: 'Date when the internal accident investigation was completed and findings documented.']
  investigation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the internal accident investigation process.']
  light_conditions varchar [note: 'Lighting conditions at the time of the accident.']
  location_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'GPS latitude coordinate of the accident location, captured from telematics or manually entered.']
  location_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'GPS longitude coordinate of the accident location, captured from telematics or manually entered.']
  location_zip_code varchar [note: 'ZIP or postal code where the accident occurred.']
  number_of_fatalities varchar [note: 'Total count of fatalities resulting from the accident.']
  number_of_injuries varchar [note: 'Total count of individuals injured in the accident, including driver, passengers, pedestrians, and occupants of other vehicles.']
  police_department varchar [note: 'Name of the law enforcement agency that responded to and documented the accident.']
  police_report_number varchar [note: 'Official police report or case number assigned by law enforcement agency responding to the accident scene.']
  post_accident_test_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the required post-accident drug and alcohol test was completed within the regulatory timeframe.']
  post_accident_test_date date [note: 'Date when the post-accident drug and alcohol test was administered.']
  post_accident_test_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a post-accident drug and alcohol test is required per FMCSA regulations (49 CFR Part 382) based on accident severity and circumstances.']
  post_accident_test_result varchar [note: 'Result of the post-accident drug and alcohol test (negative, positive, refused, or not completed).']
  preventable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the accident was determined to be preventable by the driver through proper defensive driving techniques, based on investigation findings.']
  reported_date date [note: 'Date when the accident was first reported to the company by the driver or other party.']
  road_conditions varchar [note: 'Road surface conditions at the time and location of the accident.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this accident report record was last modified in the system.']
  vehicle_speed_mph varchar [note: 'Estimated or recorded speed of the company vehicle in miles per hour at the time of the accident, captured from telematics or driver report.']
  weather_conditions varchar [note: 'Weather conditions at the time and location of the accident. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: clear|rain|snow|fog|ice|wind|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'DOT-reportable and non-reportable vehicle accident and incident records for fleet units. Captures incident date and time, vehicle unit, driver, incident location (address, GPS coordinates), incident type (collision, rollover, backing incident, pedestrian, property damage), DOT reportability flag (fatality, injury, tow-away), number of injuries, number of fatalities, estimated property damage amount, police report number, citation issued flag, citation description, drug and alcohol post-accident test required flag, test completion status, insurance claim number, and incident investigation status. Required for FMCSA accident register (49 CFR 390.15) and CSA score management.'
}

Table fleet.vehicle_class {
  vehicle_class_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the vehicle class reference record. Primary key.']
  container_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.container_type. Business justification: Vehicle class lift mechanism and body type must be compatible with container types (front-load, rear-load, roll-off). Fleet procurement and route planning require this link to ensure vehicles can serv']
  dot_hazmat_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.dot_hazmat_classification. Business justification: Dispatch eligibility rules require that only vehicle classes certified for a specific DOT hazmat classification (e.g., Class 3 tanker, Class 8 bulk carrier) can be assigned to matching hazmat shipment']
  parent_vehicle_class_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on vehicle_class (parent_vehicle_class_id)']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Vehicle classes are governed by specific regulatory requirements (EPA emissions standards, DOT axle/weight regulations). Fleet procurement and compliance officers use this link to determine applicable']
  service_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.line. Business justification: Vehicle classes are defined per service line (e.g., rear-loader for residential MSW, roll-off for commercial). Fleet procurement, budgeting, and route planning depend on this link. service.line.fleet_']
  training_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.training_requirement. Business justification: Vehicle classes mandate specific driver training (CDL class, hazmat endorsement, specialized equipment certification). Linking vehicle_class to training_requirement enables automated training gap anal']
  waste_stream_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.waste_stream. Business justification: Vehicle classes are engineered for specific waste streams (organics, MSW, hazmat, recyclables). Fleet procurement, driver PPE requirements, and DOT/EPA compliance reporting require knowing which waste']
  annual_inspection_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether vehicles in this class require annual DOT safety inspections. True if required, False if not.']
  average_acquisition_cost_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Average acquisition cost for a new vehicle in this class, measured in US dollars. Used for capital planning and budgeting.']
  average_annual_operating_cost_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Average annual operating cost for vehicles in this class including fuel, maintenance, insurance, and depreciation, measured in US dollars.']
  average_stops_per_shift varchar [note: 'Typical number of service stops this vehicle class can complete in a standard shift. Used for route planning and capacity modeling.']
  average_tons_per_day decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average tonnage of waste this vehicle class typically collects per operating day. TPD = Tons Per Day.']
  axle_configuration varchar [note: 'Axle configuration of the vehicle class (e.g., 4x2, 6x4, 8x4). Format is drive_axles x total_axles.']
  backup_camera_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether backup cameras are required safety equipment for this vehicle class. True if required, False if not.']
  body_capacity_cubic_yards decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard body capacity of the vehicle class measured in cubic yards. Represents the volume of waste the vehicle can hold.']
  body_manufacturer varchar [note: 'Typical or preferred body manufacturer for vehicles in this class (e.g., McNeilus, Heil, Labrie, New Way).']
  body_type varchar [note: 'Type of vehicle body configuration. ASL = Automated Side Loader, FEL = Front End Loader, REL = Rear End Loader. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ASL|FEL|REL|roll-off|transfer|hook-lift|grapple|compactor|flatbed|service — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  cdl_class_required varchar [note: 'Minimum CDL class required to operate vehicles in this class (A, B, C, or none if no CDL required).']
  cdl_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Commercial Driver License is required to operate vehicles in this class. True if CDL is required, False if not.']
  class_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the vehicle class (e.g., ASL-20, FEL-30, REL-25, ROLLOFF-40). Used as the business identifier for classification.']
  class_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the vehicle class (e.g., Automated Side Loader 20-Yard, Front End Loader 30-Yard, Rear End Loader 25-Yard, Roll-Off Truck 40-Yard).']
  compaction_ratio decimal(5,2) [note: 'Typical compaction ratio achieved by this vehicle class (e.g., 3.5:1 means 3.5 cubic yards of loose waste compacts to 1 cubic yard). Null for non-compacting vehicles.']
  compaction_type varchar [note: 'Type of compaction system used in the vehicle body (packer for rear loaders, compactor for side loaders, non-compacting for roll-offs, stationary for transfer vehicles).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this vehicle class record was first created in the system.']
  crew_size varchar [note: 'Standard crew size for vehicles in this class (e.g., 1 for automated side loaders, 2-3 for rear loaders with helpers).']
  dot_vehicle_class varchar [note: 'DOT vehicle classification number (1-8) based on GVWR. Class 1-2: light duty, 3-6: medium duty, 7-8: heavy duty.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this vehicle class definition became effective and available for fleet planning and procurement.']
  emissions_standard varchar [note: 'EPA emissions standard certification level for this vehicle class (e.g., EPA 2010, EPA 2021, CARB Ultra-Low NOx).']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when this vehicle class was deprecated or discontinued. Null for active classes.']
  endorsements_required varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of CDL endorsements required for this vehicle class (e.g., H for hazmat, N for tank, X for hazmat+tank). Null if no endorsements required.']
  expected_useful_life_miles varchar [note: 'Expected useful life of vehicles in this class measured in miles before replacement is typically required.']
  expected_useful_life_years varchar [note: 'Expected useful life of vehicles in this class measured in years before replacement is typically required.']
  fuel_tank_capacity_gallons decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard fuel tank capacity for this vehicle class measured in gallons. For CNG vehicles, this represents gasoline gallon equivalent (GGE).']
  fuel_type varchar [note: 'Primary fuel type for this vehicle class. CNG = Compressed Natural Gas, RNG = Renewable Natural Gas.']
  gvwr_lbs varchar [note: 'Gross Vehicle Weight Rating in pounds. Maximum total weight of the vehicle including chassis, body, fuel, and payload as specified by the manufacturer.']
  hydraulic_system_type varchar [note: 'Type of hydraulic system used for lifting and compaction operations (single-stage, two-stage, or none for non-hydraulic vehicles).']
  lift_mechanism varchar [note: 'Type of lifting mechanism used by this vehicle class to handle containers or waste (automated arm for ASL, front forks for FEL, rear forks for REL, hook/cable for roll-off, grapple for transfer, none for non-collection vehicles). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: automated_arm|front_forks|rear_forks|hook|cable|grapple|none — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  manufacturer_make varchar [note: 'Typical or preferred manufacturer make for vehicles in this class (e.g., Peterbilt, Mack, Freightliner, Autocar).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, specifications, or operational guidance for this vehicle class.']
  payload_capacity_lbs varchar [note: 'Maximum payload weight capacity in pounds that the vehicle class is rated to carry safely.']
  pm_interval_days varchar [note: 'Standard preventive maintenance interval in calendar days for vehicles in this class. PM = Preventive Maintenance.']
  pm_interval_miles varchar [note: 'Standard preventive maintenance interval in miles for vehicles in this class. PM = Preventive Maintenance.']
  pto_equipped_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether vehicles in this class are equipped with Power Take-Off systems for hydraulic operations. True if PTO-equipped, False if not.']
  telematics_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether telematics devices are required for vehicles in this class. True if required, False if optional.']
  typical_route_type varchar [note: 'Typical route type this vehicle class is optimized for (residential collection, commercial collection, industrial, transfer haul, landfill operations, or mixed use).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this vehicle class record was last modified.']
  vehicle_category varchar [note: 'High-level operational category of the vehicle class (collection, transfer, roll-off, support, administrative).']
  vehicle_class_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this vehicle class. Active = currently in use, Inactive = no longer procuring but existing vehicles still operational, Deprecated = phased out, Planned = future class not yet deployed.']

  Note: 'Reference classification of fleet vehicle types and body configurations (ASL, FEL, REL, roll-off, CNG, RNG, electric) with capacity specs, weight ratings, and operational parameters'
}

Table fleet.maintenance_schedule {
  maintenance_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the preventive maintenance schedule entry. Primary key.']
  assigned_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance facility or yard where this preventive maintenance is scheduled to be performed.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.fixed_asset. Business justification: Preventive maintenance schedules are asset maintenance strategies that drive condition-based maintenance planning, asset reliability analysis, and maintenance cost capitalization decisions per IAS 16.']
  pm_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.pm_schedule. Business justification: Fleet-domain maintenance schedules are governed by maintenance-domain PM plans. Linking fleet.maintenance_schedule to maintenance.pm_schedule allows fleet managers to trace which master PM plan drives']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: maintenance_schedule has fmcsa_required_flag and dot_inspection_flag indicating regulatory-driven schedules, but no FK to the specific regulatory_requirement mandating them. Compliance officers need t']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to collection.route. Business justification: Preventive maintenance scheduling in waste management is route-aware — PM windows must avoid disrupting high-priority routes. Linking maintenance_schedule to route enables route-impact analysis during']
  superseded_maintenance_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on maintenance_schedule (superseded_maintenance_schedule_id)']
  type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.type. Business justification: fleet.maintenance_schedule.pm_type is a denormalized plain-text field. Replacing it with a FK to maintenance.type normalizes the PM type classification, enabling consistent work order type assignment,']
  vehicle_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.vehicle_class. Business justification: Preventive maintenance schedules in fleet operations are defined at both the individual vehicle level AND the vehicle class level. A vehicle_class defines PM intervals (pm_interval_days, pm_interval_m']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vehicle assigned to this maintenance schedule.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: Fleet PM schedule completion generates a maintenance work order. Linking fleet.maintenance_schedule to the resulting work_order enables closed-loop PM tracking — schedulers verify the work was execute']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'The actual date when this preventive maintenance was completed.']
  actual_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise timestamp when this preventive maintenance was completed.']
  actual_duration_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'The actual duration (in hours) taken to complete this preventive maintenance service.']
  calendar_interval_days varchar [note: 'The calendar-based interval (in days) at which this preventive maintenance is scheduled to occur.']
  certification_number varchar [note: 'The certification or inspection number issued upon successful completion of this preventive maintenance, particularly for DOT or emissions inspections.']
  completion_engine_hours varchar [note: 'The vehicle engine hours at the time this preventive maintenance was completed.']
  completion_odometer_miles varchar [note: 'The vehicle odometer reading (in miles) at the time this preventive maintenance was completed.']
  cost_currency varchar [note: 'The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for cost amounts (e.g., USD).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this maintenance schedule record was first created in the system.']
  defects_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of any defects or issues identified during this preventive maintenance service.']
  defects_found_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any defects or issues were identified during this preventive maintenance service.']
  dot_inspection_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this preventive maintenance includes a DOT annual inspection requirement.']
  emissions_inspection_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this preventive maintenance includes an emissions inspection requirement per EPA and state regulations.']
  engine_hours_interval varchar [note: 'The engine hours interval at which this preventive maintenance is scheduled to occur.']
  estimated_duration_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'The estimated duration (in hours) required to complete this preventive maintenance service.']
  estimated_labor_cost decimal(10,2) [note: 'The estimated labor cost for this preventive maintenance service.']
  estimated_parts_cost decimal(10,2) [note: 'The estimated cost of parts required for this preventive maintenance service.']
  fmcsa_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this preventive maintenance is required by FMCSA regulations for DOT compliance.']
  last_completed_date date [note: 'The date when this preventive maintenance was last completed.']
  last_completed_engine_hours varchar [note: 'The vehicle engine hours at the time this preventive maintenance was last completed.']
  last_completed_odometer_miles varchar [note: 'The vehicle odometer reading (in miles) at the time this preventive maintenance was last completed.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this maintenance schedule record was last modified.']
  mileage_interval varchar [note: 'The mileage interval (in miles) at which this preventive maintenance is scheduled to occur.']
  next_due_date date [note: 'The calculated date when this preventive maintenance is next due based on calendar interval.']
  next_due_engine_hours varchar [note: 'The calculated engine hours at which this preventive maintenance is next due based on engine hours interval.']
  next_due_odometer_miles varchar [note: 'The calculated odometer reading (in miles) at which this preventive maintenance is next due based on mileage interval.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments related to this preventive maintenance schedule entry.']
  out_of_service_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vehicle was placed out of service as a result of findings during this preventive maintenance.']
  priority varchar [note: 'The priority level assigned to this preventive maintenance schedule entry.']
  schedule_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the preventive maintenance schedule, used for tracking and reference in Infor EAM.']
  schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the maintenance schedule entry.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'The date on which this preventive maintenance is scheduled to be performed.']
  scheduled_start_time timestamp [note: 'The precise timestamp when this preventive maintenance is scheduled to begin.']
  trigger_type varchar [note: 'The basis for triggering this preventive maintenance event: mileage-based, engine hours-based, calendar-based, or combined criteria per FMCSA requirements.']

  Note: 'Fleet-specific preventive maintenance schedule linking vehicles to PM intervals based on mileage, engine hours, and calendar triggers per FMCSA requirements'
}

SLA compliance']

EPA and state agencies require documented corrective maintenance tied to spe']

  waste_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.waste_profile. Business justification: Work orders generating hazardous waste (parts cleaning, fluid changes, contaminated materials) must reference approved waste profiles for proper characterization, manifest preparation, and disposal co']
  actual_cost decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total actual cost incurred for the work order upon completion, including actual labor, parts consumed, and external contractor charges in USD. Posted to the SAP FI/CO cost center. Used for EBITDA reporting, maintenance budget variance, and asset lifecycle cost analysis.']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the maintenance work was completed and the asset was returned to service. Used to calculate actual labor duration, downtime, and mean time to repair (MTTR) metrics.']
  actual_labor_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Actual number of labor hours expended by technicians to complete the work order. Captured from time confirmations in SAP PM or Infor EAM. Used for labor cost calculation, productivity analysis, and maintenance KPI reporting.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the technician began executing the work order. Used to calculate schedule adherence, labor hours, and downtime duration. Compared against scheduled_start_date for KPI reporting.']
  asset_meter_reading decimal(12,2) [note: 'Odometer (miles) or engine hours reading of the asset at the time the work order was executed. Used to track PM intervals (e.g., oil change every 5,000 miles), calculate mean time between failures (MTBF), and validate DOT inspection compliance.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the work order was formally closed in the originating system, confirming all costs have been settled and the asset has been returned to service. Used for cycle time analysis, cost settlement confirmation, and regulatory record completion.']
  corrective_action varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the repair, adjustment, or corrective action performed by the technician to resolve the failure. Captured at work order completion. Required for DOT inspection records and OSHA incident documentation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the work order was first created in the originating system (SAP PM or Infor EAM). Represents the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED canonical category. Used for aging analysis, SLA measurement, and audit trails.']
  dot_inspection_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this work order is associated with or fulfills a DOT vehicle inspection requirement under 49 CFR Part 396. When True, the work order record serves as part of the DOT inspection documentation and must be retained per regulatory retention schedules.']
  dot_inspection_type varchar [note: 'Type of DOT inspection associated with this work order, if applicable. Annual = federally mandated annual vehicle inspection; Pre_Trip = driver pre-trip inspection defect repair; Post_Trip = post-trip defect repair; Roadside = repair following roadside enforcement inspection; None = not a DOT inspection work order.']
  downtime_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the asset was returned to operational service following maintenance completion. Used with downtime_start_timestamp to calculate total asset downtime duration for fleet availability reporting.']
  downtime_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the asset was taken out of service due to the maintenance event. May precede actual_start_timestamp if the asset was grounded before a technician was assigned. Critical for fleet availability and route impact analysis.']
  estimated_cost decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total estimated cost of the work order at time of creation, including planned labor, parts, and external services in USD. Used for budget planning, purchase order approval thresholds, and maintenance cost forecasting.']
  estimated_labor_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Planned number of labor hours required to complete the work order, as estimated at time of creation. Used for workforce scheduling, capacity planning, and cost estimation. Compared against actual_labor_hours for variance analysis.']
  external_service_cost decimal(14,2) [note: 'Cost of external contractor or vendor services engaged to complete or support the work order in USD. Linked to purchase orders (PO) in SAP MM. Relevant for outsourced repairs, specialized inspections, and warranty work.']
  loto_cleared boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the required LOTO energy isolation procedure has been completed and verified prior to work commencement. Must be True before work begins on LOTO-required work orders. Supports OSHA compliance audit trails.']
  loto_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Lockout/Tagout (LOTO) energy isolation procedures are required before executing this work order. Mandatory safety clearance flag per OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147 for work on equipment with hazardous energy sources (hydraulics, electrical, pneumatic).']
  meter_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the asset_meter_reading field. Miles or kilometers for vehicle odometers; hours for engine hour meters; cycles for equipment with cycle-based PM intervals.']
  originating_system varchar [note: 'Source system that created and owns this work order record. Determines the authoritative system of record for reconciliation. SAP_PM indicates SAP S/4HANA Plant Maintenance module; INFOR_EAM indicates Infor Enterprise Asset Management.']
  parts_cost decimal(14,2) [note: 'Actual cost of spare parts and materials consumed during work order execution in USD. Sourced from SAP MM goods issue or Infor EAM parts transactions. Enables parts vs. labor cost split analysis for maintenance optimization.']
  po_number varchar [note: 'Purchase order number from SAP MM associated with external services or parts procurement for this work order. Links maintenance execution to the procurement process for AP matching and spend management.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the work order, driving scheduling urgency and resource allocation. Critical = immediate safety or regulatory risk (e.g., brake failure, DOT out-of-service condition); High = significant operational impact; Medium = moderate impact; Low = minor issue; Routine = standard PM cycle.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this work order is required to satisfy a regulatory compliance obligation beyond DOT (e.g., EPA equipment inspection, OSHA mandated maintenance, RCRA hazardous waste equipment certification, Clean Air Act emission control maintenance). Triggers enhanced documentation and retention requirements.']
  requested_date date [note: 'The business event date on which the maintenance need was identified and the work order was formally requested. Represents the BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP canonical category. Distinct from created_timestamp (system entry time) — captures the real-world trigger date for SLA and compliance measurement.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Technician-documented root cause of the failure or maintenance need, captured upon work order completion. Supports reliability-centered maintenance (RCM) analysis, repeat failure prevention, and regulatory reporting for DOT and OSHA incidents.']
  scheduled_end_date date [note: 'Planned date on which the maintenance work is expected to be completed. Used for fleet availability commitments, route planning coordination, and maintenance window management.']
  scheduled_start_date date [note: 'Planned date on which the maintenance work is scheduled to begin. Used for workforce scheduling, parts pre-staging, and fleet availability planning. May differ from actual_start_date if work is rescheduled.']
  source_system_wo_reference varchar [note: 'Native primary key or internal identifier of this work order in the originating system (SAP PM order number or Infor EAM internal WO ID). Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse Silver layer and the operational system of record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the work order record in the originating system. Represents the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED canonical category. Used for change detection in ETL pipelines and audit compliance.']
  warranty_claim_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the repair or parts replacement on this work order is eligible for or has been submitted as a warranty claim to the OEM or parts supplier. When True, actual_cost may be partially or fully recovered. Supports warranty recovery tracking and vendor management.']
  wo_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable work order number assigned by the originating system (SAP PM or Infor EAM). Used for cross-system reference, technician communication, and regulatory documentation such as DOT inspection records.']
  wo_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the work order. Open = created but not started; In_Progress = technician actively working; On_Hold = paused pending approval or resources; Pending_Parts = awaiting parts from inventory; Completed = work finished, pending closure review; Closed = fully closed and costed; Cancelled = voided without execution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open|in_progress|on_hold|pending_parts|completed|closed|cancelled — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Core transactional entity representing a maintenance work order (WO) for fleet vehicles or facility assets. Captures WO type (preventive/corrective/emergency), priority, status lifecycle (open, in-progress, on-hold, closed), originating system (SAP PM or Infor EAM), estimated vs. actual labor hours, estimated vs. actual cost, downtime start/end, failure description, root cause, corrective action taken, safety clearance flags (LOTO), DOT inspection linkage, and regulatory compliance indicators. SSOT for all maintenance execution records.'
}

Business justification: PM schedules for waste processing equipment (MRF conveyors, compactors, shredders) are scoped to the waste streams they handle. EPA and RCRA compliance requires demonstrating that equipment handling s']
  asset_category varchar [note: 'High-level category of the asset covered by this PM schedule. Determines applicable regulatory inspection requirements (e.g., DOT for fleet vehicles, EPA for landfill equipment). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fleet_vehicle|facility_equipment|container|transfer_station|landfill_equipment|mrf_equipment — promote to reference product]']
  auto_wo_generation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether work orders are automatically generated by the CMMS (Infor EAM or SAP PM) when the PM due date or meter threshold is reached. When false, a planner must manually release the work order. Supports both automated and manual scheduling workflows.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP FI/CO cost center code to which maintenance costs for this PM schedule are allocated. Enables financial reporting of maintenance expenditure by business unit, region, or asset class. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI/CO module.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this PM schedule record was first created in the lakehouse Silver layer. Populated by the ingestion pipeline. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX per enterprise data model conventions.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this PM schedule becomes active and begins generating work orders. Allows future-dated PM plans to be configured in advance (e.g., when a new asset is commissioned or a regulatory requirement takes effect).']
  estimated_duration_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Estimated number of labor hours required to complete the PM task. Used for technician scheduling, crew capacity planning, and asset downtime forecasting. Sourced from the task list standard times in Infor EAM or SAP PM.']
  estimated_labor_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated labor cost to complete one cycle of this PM task, in US dollars. Calculated from estimated_duration_hours multiplied by the applicable technician labor rate. Used for maintenance budget planning and COGS allocation.']
  estimated_parts_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated cost of parts and materials required to complete one cycle of this PM task, in US dollars. Sourced from the Bill of Materials (BOM) associated with the task list. Used for maintenance budget planning and cost forecasting.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which this PM schedule is no longer active and will not generate new work orders. Applicable for temporary PM plans, asset disposal schedules, or time-limited regulatory inspection programs. Null for open-ended schedules.']
  frequency_interval varchar [note: 'Numeric value representing how often the PM task recurs, interpreted in conjunction with frequency_unit. For example, a value of 90 with frequency_unit of \'days\' means every 90 days. For meter-based plans, this is the meter increment (e.g., 5000 miles).']
  frequency_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the frequency_interval. Defines whether the PM cycle is driven by elapsed time (days, weeks, months) or asset utilization meters (miles, engine_hours, operating_hours). Critical for calculating next due date or meter reading.']
  functional_location_code varchar [note: 'SAP PM or Infor EAM functional location code identifying the physical installation point or system hierarchy node where the asset is installed (e.g., fleet depot bay, landfill pump station). Supports hierarchical maintenance reporting.']
  is_dot_inspection boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this PM schedule represents a federally mandated Department of Transportation (DOT) vehicle inspection cycle (e.g., annual inspection per 49 CFR Part 396.17). When true, completion records are subject to DOT recordkeeping requirements and regulatory audit.']
  is_epa_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this PM schedule is mandated by EPA environmental regulations (e.g., landfill gas (LFG) collection system inspections, leachate system checks, or CNG fueling station inspections). When true, records are subject to EPA reporting requirements.']
  is_osha_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this PM schedule is mandated by OSHA regulations (e.g., crane inspections per 29 CFR 1910.179, powered industrial truck inspections per 29 CFR 1910.178). When true, records must be retained per OSHA recordkeeping requirements.']
  last_performed_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the most recent PM task was completed for this schedule. Used as the baseline for calculating the next due date for time-based plans. Populated from work order completion records in Infor EAM or SAP PM.']
  last_performed_meter_reading decimal(12,2) [note: 'Odometer (miles) or engine hour meter reading recorded at the time the most recent PM was completed. Used as the baseline for calculating the next due meter reading for meter-based plans. Sourced from Geotab Fleet Telematics or Infor EAM meter readings.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days before the next_due_date that a work order should be automatically generated to allow for parts procurement, technician scheduling, and asset availability planning. Ensures PM tasks are not missed due to resource constraints.']
  next_due_date date [note: 'Calculated calendar date by which the next PM task must be completed. For time-based plans, derived from last_performed_date plus frequency_interval. Drives work order generation and maintenance scheduling. Critical for DOT compliance tracking.']
  next_due_meter_reading decimal(12,2) [note: 'Calculated odometer or engine hour meter value at which the next PM task must be performed. For meter-based plans, derived from last_performed_meter_reading plus frequency_interval. Null for time-based plans.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for maintenance planners to record special instructions, regulatory references, seasonal considerations, or asset-specific requirements associated with this PM schedule. Not used for structured data.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name of the PM schedule plan (e.g., \'Annual DOT Brake Inspection – Class 8 Trucks\', \'Monthly Compactor Hydraulic Service\'). Used for display and reporting.']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the PM plan as assigned in Infor EAM or SAP PM. Used for cross-system reference and audit trail. Format: PM- followed by 4-20 alphanumeric characters.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to this PM schedule, used for work order queue management and resource allocation when multiple PM tasks compete for the same technician or asset downtime window. Critical priority is reserved for safety-critical and regulatory-mandated inspections.']
  revision_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent revision to this PM schedule was approved and applied. Used in conjunction with revision_number for change history tracking and regulatory audit documentation.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential revision counter incremented each time the PM schedule definition is modified (e.g., frequency change, task list update, regulatory requirement change). Supports change management and audit trail for compliance purposes.']
  schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the PM schedule record. Active schedules generate work orders on due dates. Suspended schedules are temporarily paused (e.g., asset out of service). Archived schedules are retained for historical reference.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this PM schedule record was sourced: Infor EAM PM module, SAP PM maintenance plans, or manually entered. Used for data lineage tracking and reconciliation in the lakehouse Silver layer.']
  source_system_plan_reference varchar [note: 'Native identifier of this PM plan record in the originating source system (Infor EAM plan number or SAP PM maintenance plan number). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver layer and the operational system of record.']
  technician_role varchar [note: 'Skill or trade classification required to perform this PM task. Used for workforce planning and technician qualification matching. Examples include mechanic for engine service, electrician for CNG system checks, or inspector for DOT annual inspections. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mechanic|electrician|hydraulic_tech|tire_tech|inspector|welder|general_tech — promote to reference product]']
  tolerance_days varchar [note: 'Allowable number of days before or after the next_due_date within which the PM task can be completed without being flagged as overdue. Provides scheduling flexibility while maintaining compliance. Negative values indicate early completion tolerance.']
  tolerance_meter_units decimal(10,2) [note: 'Allowable meter reading variance (miles or engine hours) above or below the next_due_meter_reading within which the PM task can be completed without being flagged as overdue. Applicable only to meter-based PM plans. Null for time-based plans.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this PM schedule record was last modified in the lakehouse Silver layer. Updated by the ingestion pipeline on each delta load. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX per enterprise data model conventions.']
  work_order_type varchar [note: 'Type of work order that will be generated when this PM schedule triggers. Aligns with SAP PM order types and Infor EAM work order categories. PM is standard preventive maintenance, inspection is a check-only task, lubrication is a fluid service, calibration is for measurement equipment, and overhaul is a major rebuild.']

  Note: 'Preventive Maintenance (PM) schedule master defining recurring maintenance cycles for fleet and facility assets. Stores PM plan type (time-based, meter-based, condition-based), frequency interval (days, miles, engine hours), last performed date/meter reading, next due date/meter reading, estimated duration, assigned crew or technician role, associated task list, DOT inspection cycle flags, and active/inactive status. Sourced from Infor EAM PM module and SAP PM maintenance plans.'
}

Table maintenance.wo_task {
  wo_task_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for an individual task line within a work order. Serves as the primary key for the wo_task entity in the Infor EAM / SAP PM integrated maintenance execution workflow.']

Business justification: wo_task has defect_found_flag (BOOLEAN), defect_description (STRING), and inspection_result (STRING) — all indicating that task-level defect/failure classification is needed. Adding failure_mode_id to']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific fleet vehicle or facility asset on which this task is performed. Enables asset-level maintenance history, reliability analysis, and lifecycle cost tracking in Infor EAM.']
  inspection_checklist_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.inspection_checklist. Business justification: Task-level inspection protocol: wo_task has inspection_result and dot_inspection_flag but no FK to the governing checklist. Technicians performing DOT, EPA, or OSHA inspection tasks must follow a spec']
  inspection_finding_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.inspection_finding. Business justification: Work order tasks are created to remediate specific inspection findings. Linking tasks to findings enables tracking of finding-level remediation progress, supports regulatory verification that each cit']
  invoice_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice_line. Business justification: Time-and-materials contract billing: on T&M maintenance contracts, individual work order tasks (labor, inspection steps) are billed as separate invoice line items. Waste Management contract billing re']
  permit_condition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit_condition. Business justification: Individual maintenance tasks satisfy specific permit conditions (e.g., verifying leachate pump operation satisfies a specific permit condition limit). Task-level permit condition linkage enables granu']
  pm_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Preventive Maintenance (PM) schedule that generated this task, if applicable. Links corrective and preventive tasks to their originating maintenance plan for PM compliance tracking and schedule adherence reporting.']
  primary_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent work order header that this task belongs to. Establishes the header-detail relationship between the work order and its constituent task steps.']
  waste_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.waste_profile. Business justification: Individual maintenance tasks (fluid changes, equipment cleaning) generate specific hazardous waste streams requiring RCRA compliance tracking. Task-level waste profile reference enables EPA waste gene']
  actual_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the technician marked this task as complete. Triggers downstream WO completion logic and asset return-to-service workflows. Critical for downtime duration and Mean Time to Repair (MTTR) calculations.']
  actual_labor_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Actual labor cost incurred for this task based on actual hours worked and the technician\'s labor rate. Feeds into work order total cost and asset maintenance cost reporting in SAP FI/CO.']
  actual_labor_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Actual labor hours recorded by the technician upon task completion. Compared against estimated hours for variance analysis, technician productivity measurement, and future labor estimate refinement.']
  actual_parts_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Actual cost of parts and materials consumed during task execution, recorded upon parts issuance from inventory. Used for work order cost variance analysis and asset lifecycle cost tracking.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the technician actually began executing this task. Captured via CMMS mobile app or time-clock entry. Used for schedule adherence analysis and downtime duration calculation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this task record was first created in the CMMS system, either by the work order planner or automatically generated from a PM schedule. Supports audit trail requirements and SOX financial controls for maintenance cost records.']
  defect_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of any defect, fault, or abnormal condition discovered during task execution. Populated by the technician and used to initiate corrective work orders, parts procurement, and safety incident reporting.']
  defect_found_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a defect, fault, or out-of-tolerance condition was identified during execution of this task. Triggers corrective action workflow and defect documentation in Infor EAM and Enviance EHS.']
  dot_inspection_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this task constitutes or contributes to a federally mandated Department of Transportation (DOT) vehicle inspection under 49 CFR Part 396. Triggers regulatory documentation requirements and inspection record retention obligations.']
  downtime_minutes varchar [note: 'Total minutes the asset was taken out of service specifically for this task. Contributes to overall work order downtime and feeds fleet availability and Mean Time to Repair (MTTR) calculations. Distinct from labor hours as it includes wait time for parts or approvals.']
  estimated_labor_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Planned labor cost for this task calculated from estimated labor hours multiplied by the technician\'s standard labor rate. Used for work order cost budgeting and maintenance financial planning.']
  estimated_labor_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Planned labor hours required to complete this task, derived from the standard task template or planner estimate. Used for work order scheduling, technician capacity planning, and maintenance cost budgeting.']
  estimated_parts_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Planned cost of parts and materials required for this task, derived from the Bill of Materials (BOM) and current inventory pricing. Used for work order cost estimation and maintenance budget planning.']
  inspection_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of an inspection task, indicating whether the inspected component passed, failed, or passed with conditions. Required for DOT inspection records and regulatory compliance reporting. Null for non-inspection task types.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this task record was most recently modified, including status changes, labor hour updates, or technician note additions. Supports data lineage tracking and change audit requirements in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  loto_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Lockout/Tagout (LOTO) energy isolation procedures are required before performing this task, per OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147. Enforces hazardous energy control compliance for tasks involving electrical, hydraulic, or pneumatic systems.']
  meter_reading_at_task decimal(12,2) [note: 'Odometer (miles) or engine hours reading recorded at the time this task was performed. Critical for mileage-based and hour-based PM interval tracking, DOT inspection records, and asset lifecycle management in Infor EAM.']
  meter_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the meter reading recorded at task execution (e.g., miles for vehicle odometer, hours for engine hour meter). Required to correctly interpret meter_reading_at_task for PM interval calculations.']
  parts_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this task requires one or more parts or materials from inventory to be completed. Drives parts reservation workflow in Infor EAM and SAP MM materials management integration.']
  ppe_requirements varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list of required Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) items for this task (e.g., \'safety_glasses,gloves,steel_toe_boots,hearing_protection\'). Sourced from the EHS system and enforced per OSHA 29 CFR 1910.132 standards.']
  priority_code varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to this task, which may differ from the parent work order priority if specific steps are more time-critical (e.g., a safety-critical brake inspection step within a broader PM work order). Drives technician scheduling and dispatch sequencing.']
  required_certification varchar [note: 'Specific regulatory or industry certification required to perform this task (e.g., CDL for vehicle brake inspection, ASE for engine diagnostics, DOT_INSPECTOR for federal vehicle inspection). Enforces compliance with DOT 49 CFR Part 396 and OSHA standards. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CDL|ASE|DOT_INSPECTOR|HAZMAT|OSHA_30|FORKLIFT|NONE|EPA_608|RCRA_HAZWASTE — promote to reference product]']
  required_skill_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the minimum skill or trade qualification required to perform this task (e.g., \'MECH-DIESEL\', \'ELEC-HV\', \'HVAC-CERT\'). Used for technician assignment validation and workforce capability gap analysis.']
  safety_instructions varchar [note: 'Mandatory safety procedures, lockout/tagout (LOTO) requirements, Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) specifications, and hazard warnings that must be followed before and during task execution. Sourced from EHS system and linked to OSHA compliance requirements.']
  scheduled_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time when the technician is scheduled to begin this task. Used for maintenance scheduling, shift planning, and asset downtime window management.']
  skip_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why a task was skipped or bypassed in the work order execution. Required when task_status is \'skipped\' to maintain audit trail integrity and support PM compliance reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: not_applicable|deferred|parts_unavailable|asset_unavailable|safety_hold|supervisor_override|weather|other — promote to reference product]']
  standard_task_code varchar [note: 'Reference code linking this task to a standardized maintenance task template in the CMMS library (e.g., \'HYD-INSP-001\' for hydraulic inspection). Enables consistent task definition, labor estimation benchmarking, and cross-fleet maintenance analytics.']
  task_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the maintenance task to be performed, including the specific action, component, and expected outcome (e.g., \'Inspect and replace hydraulic hose on compactor lift arm\'). Sourced from standard task library or entered by planner.']
  task_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible alphanumeric identifier for the task, typically formatted as the parent WO number suffixed with a task sequence (e.g., WO-2024-00123-T01). Used in technician communications, printed work packets, and audit trails.']
  task_sequence varchar [note: 'Numeric ordering of this task within the parent work order, defining the execution sequence of multi-step maintenance procedures (e.g., 10, 20, 30 in SAP PM operation numbering convention). Enables dependency management and step-by-step workflow tracking.']
  task_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the maintenance task within the work order execution workflow. Drives technician dashboard visibility, WO completion logic, and downtime reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|in_progress|complete|skipped|on_hold|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  task_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the maintenance task by the nature of the work performed. Used for labor planning, parts forecasting, and maintenance cost categorization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: inspection|repair|replacement|lubrication|calibration|cleaning|adjustment|testing|documentation — promote to reference product]']
  technician_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for the assigned technician to record observations, findings, deviations from standard procedure, or recommendations during task execution. Captured via Infor EAM mobile app and retained for maintenance history and knowledge management.']
  verification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the supervisor or quality inspector verified and approved the completed task. Establishes the regulatory sign-off timestamp for DOT inspection records and OSHA compliance documentation.']

  Note: 'Individual task or operation line within a work order, representing a discrete step in the maintenance execution workflow. Captures task sequence number, task description, standard task code, estimated labor hours, actual labor hours, assigned technician, task status (pending, in-progress, complete, skipped), required skill/certification (e.g., CDL, ASE), safety instructions, and completion timestamp. Enables granular tracking of multi-step maintenance procedures and technician productivity.'
}

Table maintenance.parts_usage {
  parts_usage_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each parts usage transaction record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this transactional line entity.']
  bom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.bom. Business justification: BOM consumption tracking: parts_usage has bom_reference_number as a denormalized text field. Linking to asset.bom enables tracking which BOM line items are consumed during maintenance, supporting asse']
  cell_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to landfill.cell. Business justification: Parts costs for cell-specific repairs (liner materials, gas collection components) must be allocated to the specific cell for closure cost accounting and regulatory financial assurance calculations. E']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fleet vehicle or facility asset for which the parts were consumed. Enables asset-level parts cost tracking and lifecycle analysis. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE RESOURCE_REFERENCE category.']
  generated_waste_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.waste_profile. Business justification: Hazardous parts replacement transactions must reference waste profiles for disposal tracking, manifest preparation, and cost allocation. Critical for tracking waste generation at the transaction level']
  landfill_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to landfill.site. Business justification: Parts used in landfill equipment maintenance must be tracked by site for environmental compliance reporting, cost allocation to specific landfill operations, and regulatory audits. Landfill sites have']
  manifest_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.manifest. Business justification: Parts usage events generating hazardous waste (spent solvents, used oil, contaminated filters) must reference the manifest under which that waste was shipped for disposal. Supports RCRA cradle-to-grav']
  parts_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parts catalog master record identifying the specific part or material consumed. Links to Bill of Materials (BOM) for traceability and reorder analysis.']
  storeroom_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the issuing warehouse or storeroom from which the part was physically dispensed. Supports inventory location tracking and storeroom replenishment analysis.']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.vehicle. Business justification: Parts usage tracks component consumption by vehicle for cost allocation and inventory planning. Fleet financial analysts need vehicle→parts usage linkage for TCO analysis and parts demand forecasting.']
  wo_task_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.wo_task. Business justification: parts_usage already references work_order_id (the WO header), but in CMMS operations parts are issued at the task level — a specific task within a WO drives the parts requirement. Adding wo_task_id to']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent work order against which parts were issued. Links parts consumption to the specific maintenance event (corrective or preventive). Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE HEADER_REFERENCE category.']
  asset_type varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the asset category for which parts were consumed. Distinguishes fleet vehicles (trucks, loaders) from facility assets (compactors, conveyors) and containers for parts spend segmentation.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP FI/CO cost center to which the parts cost is allocated. Enables departmental cost tracking, budget variance analysis, and financial reporting by organizational unit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this parts usage record was first created in the source system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and Silver Layer ingestion reconciliation. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this record (e.g., USD). Supports multi-currency operations for international or cross-border maintenance activities.']
  dot_inspection_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the parts usage was associated with a DOT-required vehicle inspection or compliance repair. Supports regulatory reporting for DOT out-of-service violations and fleet compliance audits.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General Ledger account code used to post the parts cost in SAP FI. Determines whether the expense is capitalized (asset improvement) or expensed (maintenance COGS) per accounting policy.']
  goods_issue_number varchar [note: 'SAP MM goods issue document number generated at the time of physical parts issuance from inventory. Provides cross-system traceability between Infor EAM work order and SAP MM inventory movement.']
  hazmat_part_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the part or material is classified as hazardous (e.g., certain lubricants, solvents, refrigerants). Triggers special handling, storage, and disposal requirements per EPA and OSHA regulations.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the parts were physically issued from the storeroom to the work order. Used for inventory aging, consumption trend analysis, and maintenance scheduling alignment. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE LINE_VALUE_OR_RESULT date dimension.']
  issue_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the parts issuance transaction. Tracks progression from initial request through reservation, physical issuance, and potential return. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE LINE_VALUE_OR_RESULT lifecycle state.']
  issue_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the parts were physically issued from the storeroom. Enables time-of-day analysis for storeroom operations and supports audit trail requirements. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number ordering this parts usage record within the parent work order. Enables multi-part work orders to be reconstructed in original sequence. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE LINE_SEQUENCE category.']
  manufacturer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the original equipment manufacturer (OEM) or parts manufacturer. Used for vendor performance analysis, warranty tracking, and preferred supplier compliance.']
  manufacturer_part_number varchar [note: 'Original equipment manufacturer (OEM) part number as assigned by the manufacturer. Enables cross-referencing between internal catalog numbers and OEM catalogs for procurement and warranty claims.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this parts usage record in the source system. Supports incremental data loading, change detection, and audit compliance. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for technician or storeroom clerk notes regarding the parts issuance (e.g., substitution reason, special handling instructions, warranty claim details). Supports operational context and audit documentation.']
  part_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the part type used for spend analysis and inventory segmentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mechanical|electrical|hydraulic|pneumatic|body|consumable|lubricant|tire|safety|other — promote to reference product]']
  part_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the part or material consumed (e.g., \'Air Filter - Heavy Duty Diesel\', \'Hydraulic Pump Seal Kit\'). Supports reporting and technician communication.']
  part_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer or internal catalog part number uniquely identifying the part or material. Used for procurement, cross-referencing with vendor catalogs, and BOM traceability.']
  quantity_issued decimal(18,4) [note: 'Actual number of units physically dispensed from the storeroom to the work order. May differ from quantity requested due to partial availability or over-issuance. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE LINE_QUANTITY category.']
  quantity_requested decimal(18,4) [note: 'Number of units of the part originally requested by the technician or planner for the work order. Compared against quantity issued to identify fulfillment gaps and partial issuances.']
  quantity_returned decimal(18,4) [note: 'Number of units returned to the storeroom after work order completion because they were not consumed. Used to calculate net consumption and adjust inventory balances.']
  reorder_triggered_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this parts issuance triggered an automatic reorder request because inventory fell below the minimum reorder point. Supports proactive inventory replenishment and prevents stockout-driven maintenance delays.']
  requested_date date [note: 'Date on which the parts were originally requested or reserved for the work order. Compared against issue date to measure storeroom fulfillment lead time and parts availability performance.']
  reservation_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known reservation or pick ticket number generated when parts are reserved against a work order prior to physical issuance. Enables pre-staging and kitting of parts for planned maintenance. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category.']
  return_date date [note: 'Date on which unused parts were returned to the storeroom after work order completion. Null if no parts were returned. Used to calculate net consumption and storeroom restocking timelines.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this parts usage record originated. Infor EAM for CMMS-driven issuances, SAP MM for goods issue transactions, or manual for paper-based entries. Supports data lineage and reconciliation.']
  source_transaction_reference varchar [note: 'Native transaction identifier from the originating source system (Infor EAM parts issuance ID or SAP MM material document number). Enables cross-system reconciliation and audit trail back to the system of record.']
  total_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total cost of parts issued calculated as quantity issued minus quantity returned multiplied by unit cost. Represents the net parts spend charged to the work order for COGS allocation and maintenance budget tracking. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE LINE_VALUE_OR_RESULT monetary category.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the nature of the parts movement: issue (parts dispensed to work order), return (unused parts returned to storeroom), transfer (moved between storerooms), or adjustment (inventory correction). Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE LINE_VALUE_OR_RESULT discriminator.']
  unit_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Standard or moving average cost per unit of the part at the time of issuance, sourced from SAP MM material valuation. Used for COGS allocation and maintenance cost reporting.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measure for the part quantity (e.g., EA=Each, QT=Quart, GL=Gallon, LB=Pound, KG=Kilogram, FT=Feet, L=Liter). Aligns with SAP MM base unit of measure for inventory valuation consistency. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|QT|GL|LB|KG|FT|M|L|BOX|SET|KIT|PR — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  warranty_claim_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the parts usage is associated with a warranty claim against the OEM or supplier. Enables warranty cost recovery tracking and vendor chargeback processing.']
  work_order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the parent work order type indicating whether parts were consumed for planned preventive maintenance (PM), unplanned corrective repair, scheduled inspection, major overhaul, or emergency breakdown response.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of parts and materials consumed against a work order. Captures part number, part description, quantity requested, quantity issued, unit of measure, unit cost, total cost, issuing warehouse/storeroom, reservation number, actual issue date, and return quantity. Links to BOM and parts catalog for traceability. Supports COGS allocation, parts consumption analysis, and reorder triggering. Sourced from Infor EAM parts issuance and SAP MM goods issue.'
}

Business justification: Repair history records document repairs performed in response to compliance inspection findings. This linkage supports regulatory audit trails showing that inspection-identified deficiencies were corr']

  downtime_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.downtime_event.   facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance facility, shop, or service location where the repair was performed (e.g., fleet maintenance yard, landfill workshop, transfer station). Enables geographic and facility-level maintenance cost and performance analysis.']
  failure_mode_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.failure_mode. Business justification: Repair history records should link to standardized failure mode catalog for consistent failure tracking and trend analysis. Currently has failure_mode (STRING) and failure_code (STRING) which duplicat']

  pm_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Preventive Maintenance (PM) schedule or maintenance plan that triggered this work order, if applicable. Populated for planned/preventive repairs; null for corrective or emergency repairs. Enables PM compliance rate analysis.']

  asset_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known asset tag or equipment number (e.g., fleet unit number, facility equipment tag) as assigned in Infor EAM or SAP PM. Enables direct lookup without requiring a join to the asset master.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the Work Order (WO) was formally closed and archived in the CMMS, creating this repair history record. May differ from completion_timestamp if administrative review or cost settlement occurs after physical completion.']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the repair or maintenance event was completed and the asset was returned to service. Used to calculate actual repair duration and Mean Time To Repair (MTTR). Distinct from the WO close date which may occur later during administrative closure.']
  component_repaired varchar [note: 'Name or description of the specific component, sub-assembly, or system on the asset that was repaired or replaced (e.g., rear axle, hydraulic pump, compactor blade, engine air filter, brake caliper). Supports Bill of Materials (BOM) analysis and component-level MTBF tracking.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP General Ledger (GL) cost center code to which the repair costs are allocated. Enables financial reporting of maintenance expenditure by business unit, region, or operational division for EBITDA and COGS analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this repair history record was first created in the source system (Infor EAM or SAP PM). Serves as the audit trail creation marker for data lineage and SOX compliance.']
  dot_inspection_number varchar [note: 'Official DOT inspection report number assigned by the inspecting authority for DOT-mandated inspections. Required for regulatory compliance documentation and FMCSA audit response. Populated only when is_dot_inspection is True.']
  downtime_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total hours the asset was out of service (unavailable for operations) due to this repair event, from the time it was taken out of service to the time it was returned to active duty. Key input for fleet availability rate and operational impact assessment.']
  engine_hours_at_repair decimal(10,1) [note: 'Engine hour meter reading recorded at the time of repair for hour-metered assets (compactors, generators, heavy equipment, Waste-to-Energy (WTE) plant equipment). Used for hour-based PM interval tracking and MTBF analysis where mileage is not applicable.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'SAP General Ledger (GL) account code used to post the repair costs in the financial system. Distinguishes between capital expenditure (CapEx) and operating expenditure (OpEx) maintenance costs for GAAP/IFRS financial reporting.']
  is_dot_inspection boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this repair event was associated with or triggered by a Department of Transportation (DOT) mandated vehicle inspection (e.g., annual inspection per 49 CFR Part 396.17, roadside inspection, or out-of-service order). Critical for DOT compliance audit trail.']
  is_warranty_claim boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the repair costs are recoverable under an OEM or vendor warranty. When True, triggers the warranty recovery process and cost reclassification from maintenance expense to warranty receivable.']
  labor_cost decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total labor cost in US dollars for this repair event, calculated from technician hours and applicable labor rates. Component of total repair cost and lifecycle cost analysis. Confidential as it reflects internal labor rate structures.']
  labor_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total actual labor hours expended by all technicians on this repair event, as recorded in the CMMS time entry. Used for Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) calculation, technician productivity analysis, and labor cost allocation.']
  odometer_at_repair varchar [note: 'Odometer reading in miles recorded at the time of repair for wheeled fleet assets (trucks, ASL, FEL, REL vehicles). Used to calculate mileage-based Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF), establish PM trigger intervals, and support DOT inspection compliance records.']
  parts_cost decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total cost in US dollars of all parts and materials consumed during this repair event, sourced from the Bill of Materials (BOM) and parts inventory. Component of total repair cost and lifecycle cost analysis.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the work order at the time of execution, indicating urgency and impact on fleet availability or regulatory compliance (e.g., critical for DOT out-of-service violations, routine for scheduled Preventive Maintenance (PM)).']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'SAP Purchase Order (PO) number associated with external parts procurement or subcontractor services for this repair. Links the maintenance record to the Accounts Payable (AP) process for cost reconciliation.']
  repair_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative describing the work performed, findings, corrective actions taken, and any relevant observations by the technician. Provides the detailed maintenance record for audit, warranty claims, and future diagnostic reference.']
  repair_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the repair history record. Completed indicates work was finished and verified; closed indicates the WO was formally archived; cancelled indicates the WO was voided; deferred indicates work was postponed and re-scheduled.']
  repair_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the maintenance event: corrective (reactive repair after failure), preventive (scheduled Preventive Maintenance (PM) cycle), predictive (condition-based), emergency (unplanned critical failure), or inspection (DOT/regulatory inspection). Drives MTBF and MTTR segmentation.']
  reported_date date [note: 'Date the failure, defect, or maintenance need was first reported or identified, either by a driver vehicle inspection report (DVIR), operator, or automated telematics alert. Establishes the start of the repair lifecycle for Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) calculation.']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Technician or engineer analysis of the underlying root cause of the failure or maintenance need (e.g., deferred maintenance, operator misuse, manufacturing defect, environmental exposure). Supports root cause analysis (RCA) and continuous improvement programs.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'Date the maintenance or repair event was scheduled to be performed, as planned in the Preventive Maintenance (PM) schedule or work order dispatch. Used to measure schedule adherence and PM compliance rate.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this repair history record was sourced (Infor EAM for asset-centric maintenance, SAP PM for work order execution, or Manual for paper-based records). Supports data lineage and reconciliation in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the technician physically began work on the asset. Used as the primary business event timestamp for Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) calculation and technician utilization analysis.']
  subcontractor_cost decimal(14,2) [note: 'Cost in US dollars paid to external vendors or subcontractors for work performed on this repair event (e.g., specialized engine rebuilds, body shop work, OEM warranty repairs). Tracked separately from internal labor cost for make-vs-buy analysis.']
  supervisor_approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee ID of the maintenance supervisor or fleet manager who reviewed and approved the completed repair before work order closure. Required for DOT annual inspection certification and internal quality control.']
  technician_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary technician who performed the repair, as recorded at the time of work order closure. Retained in the historical record for audit and accountability purposes even if the technician record changes. Classified confidential as it is employee data.']
  total_repair_cost decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total cost in US dollars for the complete repair event, encompassing labor, parts, subcontractor, and any other direct costs. Primary financial metric for asset lifecycle cost analysis, EBITDA impact assessment, and repair vs. replace decision support.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this repair history record in the source system. Supports incremental data pipeline processing and audit trail requirements.']
