  eam_type_code varchar [note: 'Native maintenance type code as defined in Infor EAM, used for system integration and data lineage tracing. Enables bidirectional synchronization between the lakehouse silver layer and the Infor EAM CMMS source system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this maintenance type classification becomes valid and available for use in work order and PM schedule creation. Supports versioning and phased rollout of new maintenance type definitions aligned with regulatory or operational changes.']
  estimated_duration_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Standard estimated labor hours required to complete a maintenance activity of this type. Used for technician scheduling, shift planning, and asset downtime forecasting. Serves as the default value populated on new work orders of this type in Infor EAM.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which this maintenance type classification is no longer valid for new work order or PM schedule creation. Null indicates no expiration. Supports retirement of obsolete maintenance types while preserving historical records.']
  external_service_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether maintenance activities of this type are typically performed by an external vendor or contractor (True) rather than internal technicians (False). When True, triggers purchase order (PO) creation workflow in SAP MM for external service procurement.']
  failure_mode_applicable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether failure mode and root cause analysis is required for work orders of this maintenance type (True) or not (False). When True, technicians must document failure codes, cause codes, and remediation actions in Infor EAM for reliability-centered maintenance (RCM) analysis.']
  ghg_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether maintenance activities of this type generate reportable GHG emissions data (True) or not (False). Applicable for maintenance involving CNG/RNG fuel systems, LFG equipment, or WTE facility assets where EPA GHG reporting under the Clean Air Act is required.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'SAP General Ledger account code to which maintenance costs of this type are posted. Ensures accurate financial reporting, cost center allocation, and compliance with GAAP/IFRS standards for maintenance expense recognition.']
  loto_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Lockout/Tagout (LOTO) energy isolation procedures are required for maintenance activities of this type (True) or not (False). Mandatory safety control per OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147 for hazardous energy control during maintenance on fleet and facility equipment.']
  max_allowable_downtime_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Maximum number of hours an asset is permitted to remain out of service for maintenance of this type before escalation is triggered. Used to enforce SLA compliance for fleet availability and to drive escalation workflows in Infor EAM and AMCS dispatch.']
  meter_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the recurrence_interval_meter field, defining the measurement basis for meter-triggered PM schedules (e.g., miles for vehicle odometer, hours for engine runtime, cycles for lift/compaction cycles, tons for throughput-based equipment).']
  planning_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard number of calendar days required to plan and prepare for a maintenance activity of this type before execution. Used by maintenance planners to schedule parts procurement, technician availability, and asset downtime windows. Sourced from Infor EAM PM cycle configuration.']
  pm_trigger_basis varchar [note: 'Defines the basis on which PM schedules of this maintenance type are triggered: time-based (fixed calendar intervals), meter-based (mileage, engine hours, cycles), condition-based (sensor thresholds), event-based (after specific operational events), or calendar-based (fixed dates). Drives PM cycle configuration in Infor EAM.']
  ppe_requirements varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of mandatory PPE items required for maintenance activities of this type (e.g., safety_glasses, gloves, hard_hat, hi_vis_vest, respirator). Populated on work orders to ensure technician safety compliance per OSHA PPE standards.']
  recurrence_interval_days varchar [note: 'Default calendar-based recurrence interval in days for PM schedules of this maintenance type. Used when pm_trigger_basis is \'time\' or \'calendar\'. Drives automatic PM work order generation in Infor EAM and SAP PM scheduling cycles.']
  recurrence_interval_meter decimal(10,2) [note: 'Default meter-based recurrence interval for PM schedules of this maintenance type (e.g., miles, engine hours, lift cycles). Used when pm_trigger_basis is \'meter\'. The unit of measure is defined by meter_unit. Drives meter-based PM work order generation in Infor EAM.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether maintenance activities of this type must be reported to a regulatory authority (True) or not (False). Triggers mandatory documentation workflows in Enviance EHS and SAP EHS for DOT inspections, EPA compliance events, and OSHA-required maintenance records.']
  required_certification_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific technician certification required to perform maintenance of this type (e.g., CDL_CLASS_A, ASE_MASTER, DOT_INSPECTOR, HAZMAT_HANDLER). Enforced during work order technician assignment in Infor EAM and Workday HCM. Applicable when requires_certified_technician is True.']
  requires_asset_shutdown boolean [note: 'Indicates whether maintenance of this type requires the asset (vehicle, equipment, or facility) to be taken out of service (True) or can be performed while the asset remains operational (False). Drives downtime planning and route coverage adjustments in AMCS and Locus Dispatch.']
  requires_certified_technician boolean [note: 'Indicates whether maintenance activities of this type must be performed by a technician holding a specific certification (True) or can be performed by any qualified technician (False). When True, the required_certification_code field specifies the certification. Supports CDL, ASE, and DOT certification enforcement.']
  sap_order_type_code varchar [note: 'SAP PM order type code corresponding to this maintenance type (e.g., PM01, PM02, PM03). Used for GL posting, cost settlement, and integration with SAP FI/CO for maintenance cost reporting and EBITDA analysis.']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Numeric value controlling the display sequence of maintenance types in dropdown lists, reports, and scheduling interfaces. Lower values appear first. Enables business users to prioritize frequently used types for operational efficiency.']
  type_category varchar [note: 'High-level grouping of the maintenance type indicating whether the activity is planned (scheduled in advance), unplanned (reactive/emergency), regulatory (DOT/EPA/OSHA mandated), or warranty (covered under manufacturer or service warranty). Supports cost allocation and KPI segmentation.']
  type_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the maintenance type, used as a business key across work orders, PM schedules, and reporting systems (e.g., PREV, CORR, PRED, EMER, CBM, DOT, WARR, RBLD). Sourced from Infor EAM and SAP PM type classification.']
  type_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explaining the purpose, scope, and application of this maintenance type. Provides guidance to planners and technicians on when and how to apply this classification across fleet vehicles, containers, and facility assets.']
  type_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the maintenance type as recognized across fleet and facility operations (e.g., Preventive, Corrective, Predictive, Emergency, Condition-Based, DOT Inspection, Warranty, Rebuild). Used in work order displays, PM schedule labels, and management reporting.']
  type_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the maintenance type record indicating whether it is actively used in work order and PM schedule creation (active), temporarily disabled (inactive), or retired from use (deprecated). Controls availability in downstream scheduling and dispatch systems.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this maintenance type record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change tracking, data synchronization between Infor EAM and the lakehouse silver layer, and audit compliance.']
  warranty_applicable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether maintenance activities of this type may be covered under an OEM or service warranty (True) or are not warranty-eligible (False). When True, triggers warranty claim validation workflow in Infor EAM before cost posting to internal cost centers.']
  work_order_class varchar [note: 'SAP PM and Infor EAM work order class associated with this maintenance type. Determines the work order processing workflow, approval routing, and cost settlement rules. Maps directly to the order type configuration in SAP PM (e.g., PM01 Corrective, PM02 Preventive, PM03 Inspection).']

  Note: 'Reference classification of maintenance activity types used across work orders and PM schedules. Captures maintenance type code, maintenance type name (Preventive, Corrective, Predictive, Emergency, Condition-Based, DOT Inspection, Warranty, Rebuild), description, planning lead time standard, priority default, cost category mapping, and regulatory reporting flag. Provides standardized taxonomy for maintenance classification across fleet and facility assets.'
}

Table recycling.mrf_facility {
  mrf_facility_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each Materials Recovery Facility (MRF) record. Serves as the primary key and SSOT reference for MRF facility identity across the recycling domain.']
  epa_facility_id varchar [note: 'EPA-assigned facility identifier registered in the EPA Facility Registry System (FRS). Required for federal environmental reporting, RCRA compliance submissions, and EPA inspection records. Serves as the federal regulatory identity of the facility.']
  monitoring_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.monitoring_program. Business justification: MRF facilities operate under mandatory environmental monitoring programs (air emissions, stormwater, groundwater per permit conditions). This link enables compliance staff to associate facility operat']
  regulated_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulated_facility. Business justification: MRF facilities are registered regulated entities under RCRA and state solid waste regulations. Compliance officers use this link for inspection scheduling, permit management, and regulatory status rep']
  accepts_cd_waste boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the MRF facility is permitted and equipped to process Construction and Demolition (C&D) waste streams including concrete, wood, drywall, and metals. C&D processing requires specialized equipment and separate permit conditions.']
  accepts_commercial boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the MRF facility is permitted and equipped to accept commercial and industrial recyclable materials. Drives commercial hauling route assignments in AMCS and contract eligibility in Salesforce CRM.']
  accepts_residential boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the MRF facility is permitted and equipped to accept residential curbside recyclable materials. Drives routing logic in AMCS and service enrollment eligibility in Waste Logics.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the MRF facility. Used for regulatory filings, logistics routing, emergency response coordination, and vendor/hauler directions. Classified as confidential organizational contact data.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for the MRF facility (suite, building, gate number, or additional location detail). Supplements address_line1 for precise facility identification in logistics and regulatory submissions.']
  baling_line_count varchar [note: 'Number of baling lines (balers) at the MRF facility used to compress and bind recovered commodities (paper, plastics, metals) into marketable bales. Determines bale production capacity and influences commodity sales throughput.']
  city varchar [note: 'City in which the MRF facility is located. Used for geographic reporting, regulatory jurisdiction determination, and service area mapping.']
  commissioned_date date [note: 'Date on which the MRF facility was officially commissioned and began commercial operations. Used for asset age calculations, depreciation schedules, permit anniversary tracking, and capital planning.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA FI/CO cost center code assigned to this MRF facility for financial cost allocation, P&L reporting, and budget management. Links facility operational costs to the general ledger (GL) for EBITDA and COGS reporting.']
  county varchar [note: 'County in which the MRF facility is located. Relevant for Local Enforcement Agency (LEA) jurisdiction, county solid waste authority reporting, and local permit compliance under SWIS.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the MRF facility master record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage, audit trail compliance, and record provenance tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date on which the MRF facility was permanently decommissioned and ceased operations. Null for active facilities. Used for asset retirement accounting, permit closure filings, and historical reporting.']
  design_capacity_tpd decimal(10,2) [note: 'Engineering-designed maximum daily throughput capacity in tons per day (TPD) as specified in the facility\'s original design documentation. May differ from permitted capacity due to regulatory limits or operational constraints. Used for capital planning and equipment sizing.']
  facility_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the MRF facility, used in operational systems (AMCS, SAP MM), regulatory submissions, and inter-system integration. Format: MRF-[STATE]-[SEQUENCE].']
  facility_name varchar [note: 'Official human-readable name of the MRF facility as registered with regulatory authorities and used in customer-facing communications, contracts, and reporting (e.g., \'Houston North MRF\').']
  facility_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the MRF by processing methodology. Single-stream accepts all recyclables commingled; dual-stream separates fiber from containers; mixed-waste MRF processes unsorted MSW; source-separated handles pre-sorted materials; drop-off serves public drop-off programs.']
  iso_14001_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the MRF facility holds a current ISO 14001 Environmental Management System certification. ISO 14001 certification demonstrates systematic environmental management and is required for certain municipal contracts and sustainability reporting.']
  iso_45001_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the MRF facility holds a current ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety Management System certification. Relevant for OSHA compliance, worker safety programs, and contract qualification requirements.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the MRF facility master record. Used for change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and incremental load processing in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the MRF facility in decimal degrees (WGS84). Used for GPS-based routing in AMCS, geospatial analytics, emergency response mapping, and regulatory geographic submissions.']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the MRF facility in decimal degrees (WGS84). Used for GPS-based routing in AMCS, geospatial analytics, emergency response mapping, and regulatory geographic submissions.']
  max_contamination_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable contamination rate percentage for outbound commodity bales as specified in commodity offtake contracts and quality standards. Exceeding this threshold may result in load rejection, price penalties, or contract violations. Used in quality control and commodity sales management.']
  operating_days varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of days of the week the MRF facility operates (e.g., \'MON,TUE,WED,THU,FRI,SAT\'). Used for route scheduling in AMCS, customer service availability, and workforce planning in Workday HCM.']
  operating_hours_end varchar [note: 'Standard daily end time for MRF facility operations in HH:MM (24-hour) format. Used for inbound vehicle scheduling, tipping fee window management in Oracle Revenue Management, and AMCS route dispatch timing.']
  operating_hours_start varchar [note: 'Standard daily start time for MRF facility operations in HH:MM (24-hour) format. Used for inbound vehicle scheduling, tipping fee window management in Oracle Revenue Management, and AMCS route dispatch timing.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the MRF facility. Active indicates full operations; temporarily_closed covers planned or unplanned shutdowns; decommissioned indicates permanent closure; permitted_not_operating indicates permit held but no active processing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|inactive|under_construction|temporarily_closed|decommissioned|permitted_not_operating — promote to reference product]']
  optical_sorter_count varchar [note: 'Number of optical sorting units (near-infrared or AI-vision sorters) installed at the facility for automated material identification and separation. Indicates automation level, influences contamination rates, and is tracked in Infor EAM for maintenance scheduling.']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Indicates the ownership or operational arrangement for the facility. Company-owned assets are on Waste Management\'s balance sheet; leased facilities carry lease obligations; joint ventures involve shared ownership; contracted facilities are operated under service agreements with municipalities or third parties.']
  permitted_capacity_tpd decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum permitted daily processing throughput in tons per day (TPD) as authorized by the facility\'s state environmental permit. This is the regulatory ceiling for inbound material acceptance and is used for compliance monitoring, capacity planning, and diversion rate reporting.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary operational phone number for the MRF facility. Used for inbound hauler coordination, customer service escalations, emergency contact, and regulatory agency communications.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA MM plant code corresponding to this MRF facility. Used for inventory management of recovered commodities, purchase orders (PO) for consumables, and materials management integration between the MRF and SAP MM module.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'ZIP or ZIP+4 postal code for the MRF facility address. Used for geographic analysis, regulatory jurisdiction mapping, and logistics routing.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Internal Waste Management operational region code to which this MRF facility is assigned (e.g., \'SOUTH\', \'NORTHEAST\', \'WEST\'). Used for regional performance reporting, P&L allocation in SAP FI/CO, and organizational hierarchy management.']
  sort_line_count varchar [note: 'Number of active sorting lines (conveyor-based manual or automated sort systems) at the MRF facility. Directly determines sorting throughput capacity and staffing requirements. Used in capacity planning and equipment maintenance scheduling in Infor EAM.']
  state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter USPS state abbreviation for the state in which the MRF facility is located. Determines applicable state environmental regulations, permit requirements, and reporting jurisdictions.']
  storage_capacity_tons decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum on-site storage capacity in tons for processed and baled commodities awaiting outbound shipment. Used for inventory management in SAP MM, commodity sales scheduling, and market timing decisions.']
  target_diversion_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Facility-level target percentage of inbound material weight that is successfully recovered and diverted from landfill disposal. Expressed as a percentage (0-100). Used as a KPI benchmark for operational performance, EPA recycling reporting, and sustainability goal tracking.']
  tipping_floor_area_sqft decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total area of the tipping floor in square feet where inbound recyclable materials are unloaded from collection vehicles. Determines maximum simultaneous unloading capacity and influences throughput planning and safety compliance under OSHA.']
  total_floor_area_sqft decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total enclosed floor area of the MRF facility building in square feet, including tipping floor, sort lines, baling area, storage, and office space. Used for property tax assessments, lease calculations, and facility planning.']

  Note: 'Master record for each Materials Recovery Facility (MRF) operated by Waste Management. Captures facility identity, physical address, permitted capacity (TPD), facility type (single-stream, dual-stream, mixed-waste MRF), operational status, EPA permit number, state permit number, licensed processing capacity, tipping floor area (sq ft), baling line count, sort line count, ownership type, and commissioning date. SSOT for MRF facility identity across the recycling domain.'
}

Table recycling.inbound_load {
  inbound_load_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each inbound material load received at the Materials Recovery Facility (MRF). Serves as the primary key for all load-level transactions and reconciliations.']
  area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.area. Business justification: Inbound loads originate from specific service areas. service_area_code is a plain-text denormalization of service.area. This FK enables area-level contamination analysis, diversion rate reporting by s']
  asset_container_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_container. Business justification: Inbound loads arrive in tracked asset containers (roll-offs, compactors). Container return logistics, tare weight reconciliation, and container utilization reporting all require knowing which specific']
  billing_tipping_fee_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_tipping_fee_schedule. Business justification: Each inbound load is assessed a tipping fee using a specific fee schedule. Linking inbound_load to billing_tipping_fee_schedule enables fee audit trails, rate change impact analysis, and billing dispu']
  compliance_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_inspection. Business justification: Inbound loads are subject to regulatory waste acceptance audits and contamination inspections by state/EPA inspectors. inbound_load has is_audited flag but no FK to compliance_inspection. This link pr']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account (residential, commercial, or municipal) associated with this inbound load. Used for tipping fee billing reconciliation in Oracle Revenue Management and Salesforce CRM account linkage.']
  driver_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the CDL-licensed driver who delivered the inbound load. Used for driver performance tracking, DOT compliance, and load accountability.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Materials Recovery Facility (MRF) that received this inbound load. Supports multi-facility operations and facility-level throughput reporting.']
  generator_epa_id_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.epa_id_registration. Business justification: MRFs receiving contaminated loads from generators must track EPA ID registrations for regulatory compliance and enforcement. When hazardous contamination exceeds thresholds, generator EPA ID is requir']
  hauling_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.hauling_agreement. Business justification: Inbound loads transported under third-party hauling contracts require linkage to the governing hauling_agreement for contracted rate validation, DOT compliance verification, and freight invoice reconc']
  mrf_facility_id varchar [note: 'FK to recycling.mrf_facility.mrf_facility_id — Every inbound load must reference which MRF received it. Required for facility throughput reporting and contamination rate tracking by facility.']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to recycling.recycling_program. Business justification: Each inbound load originates from a specific recycling collection program (municipal curbside, commercial recycling, C&D, etc.). inbound_load has no recycling_program_id currently. This FK enables pro']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the collection route that generated this inbound load. Links the MRF receipt back to the originating residential or commercial collection route for AMCS route-to-MRF load reconciliation and diversion rate reporting.']
  sort_session_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to recycling.sort_session. Business justification: An inbound load is processed during a specific sort session. inbound_load currently has sort_line_assignment as a denormalized string. Adding sort_session_id FK links the load to the session that proc']
  transfer_station_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the transfer station that consolidated and dispatched this load to the MRF, if applicable. Null for direct-haul loads from collection routes. Supports transfer station throughput and chain-of-custody tracking.']
  vehicle_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.vehicle_assignment. Business justification: Inbound loads must be reconciled against the specific vehicle assignment (driver+vehicle+shift) for pre-trip inspection compliance, HOS verification, and scale ticket reconciliation. inbound_load has ']
  vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fleet vehicle that delivered the inbound load to the MRF. Used for fleet performance tracking, load-per-vehicle analytics, and Geotab telematics reconciliation.']
  waste_acceptance_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Inbound loads must comply with facility waste acceptance permits specifying allowed material types, contamination limits, and capacity thresholds. Critical for scale house permit verification and regu']
  waste_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.waste_code. Business justification: Inbound loads containing hazardous fractions (contamination_type, material_stream_type) require RCRA waste code classification for regulatory compliance, proper routing decisions, and manifest prepara']
  waste_stream_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.waste_stream. Business justification: Each inbound MRF load carries a specific waste stream type. material_stream_type is a plain-text denormalization of service.waste_stream. This FK enables contamination rate analysis by waste stream, d']
  amcs_load_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier from the AMCS Platform linking this MRF inbound load record to the originating route dispatch and collection event. Enables route-to-MRF load reconciliation and confirms that collected materials were delivered to the correct facility.']
  bill_of_lading_number varchar [note: 'Bill of Lading number associated with this inbound load, used for third-party hauler loads and transfer station deliveries. Provides chain-of-custody documentation and supports SAP MM goods receipt matching.']
  contamination_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the inbound load was visually identified as contaminated at the time of receipt. True = contamination detected; False = load appears clean. Triggers contamination workflow including surcharge assessment, customer notification, and Enviance EHS incident logging if contamination exceeds threshold.']
  contamination_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Initial visual estimate of contamination level as a percentage of total load volume, assessed by the MRF receiving operator at the tipping floor. Expressed as 0.00 to 100.00. Used for contamination trending, customer education programs, and EPA recycling quality reporting. Formal contamination rate may be updated after sort audit.']
  contamination_surcharge decimal(10,2) [note: 'Additional fee assessed on this load due to contamination exceeding the contractual threshold, expressed in US dollars. Applied per customer contract terms when contamination percentage exceeds the agreed acceptable contamination rate. Posted to Oracle Revenue Management for billing.']
  contamination_type varchar [note: 'Primary category of contamination identified in the load (e.g., food waste, hazardous materials, non-recyclable plastics, bagged recyclables, electronics, textiles, medical waste). Used for contamination root cause analysis and targeted customer outreach. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: food_waste|hazardous|non_recyclable_plastic|bagged_recyclables|electronics|textiles|medical_waste|other — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the inbound load record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  estimated_diversion_tons decimal(10,4) [note: 'Estimated tonnage of this load that is expected to be diverted from landfill through recycling or recovery, based on material stream type and historical contamination-adjusted yield rates. Expressed in short tons. Used for real-time diversion rate tracking and EPA recycling program reporting. Distinct from actual recovered commodity weight which is captured at bale production.']
  gross_weight_tons decimal(10,4) [note: 'Total weight of the loaded vehicle as measured at the MRF inbound scale, expressed in short tons. Gross weight is the first weigh-in measurement before tare deduction. Critical for Tons Per Day (TPD) throughput reporting and tipping fee calculation.']
  is_audited boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inbound load has been selected for and completed a formal sort audit or waste characterization study. True = audit completed. Sort audits are used to validate contamination estimates, measure commodity composition, and support EPA recycling program reporting accuracy.']
  load_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for scale operator or supervisor notes regarding this inbound load. May include observations about load quality, unusual materials, driver communications, or special handling instructions. Supports operational exception management.']
  load_source_type varchar [note: 'Classification of how the load arrived at the MRF. \'direct_haul\' = collection vehicle delivering directly from route; \'transfer_station\' = consolidated load from a transfer station; \'drop_off_center\' = aggregated materials from a public drop-off facility; \'special_event\' = community recycling event; \'third_party_hauler\' = independent hauler delivering under contract.']
  load_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the inbound load record. \'received\' = weighed and logged at scale; \'accepted\' = cleared for processing; \'rejected\' = refused entry (contamination, wrong material, etc.); \'pending_review\' = flagged for supervisor review; \'posted\' = SAP MM material receipt posted; \'voided\' = record cancelled.']
  manifest_number varchar [note: 'Hazardous waste tracking manifest number, if applicable. Required under RCRA for loads containing any hazardous waste fraction. Null for standard recyclable loads. Links to Enviance EHS manifest tracking for regulatory compliance.']
  net_weight_tons decimal(10,4) [note: 'Net weight of the inbound material load, calculated as gross weight minus tare weight, expressed in short tons. This is the primary tonnage figure used for TPD throughput reporting, tipping fee billing, diversion rate calculations, and SAP MM material receipt posting.']
  processing_shift varchar [note: 'Work shift during which this inbound load was received and processed at the MRF. Used for shift-level throughput reporting, labor allocation, and operational performance benchmarking.']
  received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the hauling vehicle arrived at the MRF scale house and the load receipt process was initiated. This is the principal business event timestamp for the inbound load transaction. Recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset.']
  receiving_dock varchar [note: 'Identifier of the MRF tipping floor dock or bay assigned to receive this load. Used for floor traffic management, sorting line assignment, and operational throughput balancing across dock positions.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code or description explaining why a load was rejected at the MRF gate. Populated only when load_status = \'rejected\'. Common reasons include excessive contamination, prohibited materials, missing documentation, or capacity constraints. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: excessive_contamination|prohibited_materials|missing_documentation|capacity_exceeded|wrong_material_type|hazardous_detected|other — promote to reference product]']
  sap_material_doc_number varchar [note: 'SAP MM material document number generated upon goods receipt posting for this inbound load. Confirms the load has been formally received into SAP inventory management. Null until load_status = \'posted\'. Enables reconciliation between MRF scale records and SAP MM inventory.']
  scale_ticket_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible scale house ticket number assigned at the weigh station upon load receipt. Used as the primary operational reference for load reconciliation between the MRF, hauling contractor, and SAP MM material receipt posting. Printed on the physical scale ticket given to the driver.']
  supervisor_review_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this load record has been flagged for or has undergone supervisor review. True = supervisor review required or completed. Triggered by contamination above threshold, weight anomalies, rejected loads, or missing documentation.']
  supervisor_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the supervisor completed review of this flagged load record. Null if supervisor_review_flag is False or review is pending. Used for SLA compliance on review turnaround and audit trail.']
  tare_weight_tons decimal(10,4) [note: 'Weight of the empty hauling vehicle as measured at the MRF outbound scale after unloading, expressed in short tons. Used with gross weight to calculate net material weight. May be sourced from vehicle tare records if a second weigh is not performed.']
  tipping_fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total tipping fee charged for this inbound load in US dollars, calculated as net weight multiplied by the tipping fee rate. Represents the gross revenue amount for this load transaction. Posted to Oracle Revenue Management and SAP FI/CO for billing and GL recognition.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the inbound load record was most recently modified. Tracks post-receipt corrections such as weight adjustments, contamination re-assessments, or status changes.']
  vehicle_type varchar [note: 'Type of collection or hauling vehicle that delivered the load. ASL = Automated Side Loader; FEL = Front End Loader; REL = Rear End Loader; roll_off = roll-off container truck; transfer_trailer = semi-trailer from transfer station; rear_load = rear-loading compactor. Affects expected compaction ratio and material quality.']
  weather_condition varchar [note: 'Weather condition at the MRF at the time of load receipt. Used for operational analytics correlating weather with contamination rates, load volumes, and processing throughput. Supports capacity planning and seasonal trend analysis.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each inbound material load received at a MRF. Captures load receipt timestamp, originating route or transfer station, hauling vehicle ID, gross weight (tons), tare weight, net weight, material stream type (single-stream residential, commercial, C&D, drop-off), contamination flag, initial visual contamination estimate (%), tipping fee applied, scale ticket number, driver ID, and receiving dock assignment. Integrates with AMCS for route-to-MRF load reconciliation and SAP MM for material receipt posting.'
}

Table recycling.sort_line {
  sort_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each sort line record within the Materials Recovery Facility (MRF). Serves as the primary key for this master resource entity.']
  air_quality_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Sort lines operate under equipment-specific air quality permits governing particulate emissions, VOC controls, and operational parameters. Enables tracking of permit-mandated throughput limits, emissi']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Materials Recovery Facility (MRF) where this sort line is physically installed and operated.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Infor EAM system asset identifier for this sort line, used to link to maintenance schedules, work orders, and asset lifecycle records in the EAM system.']
  inspection_checklist_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.inspection_checklist. Business justification: Sort lines require OSHA and EPA-mandated periodic safety and operational inspections. The inspection_checklist defines the protocol for each sort line type. Regulatory compliance programs and ISO 4500']
  mrf_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to recycling.mrf_facility. Business justification: A sort line physically belongs to a specific MRF facility. sort_line already has facility_id → asset.facility (cross-domain), but lacks the in-domain link to mrf_facility which carries MRF-specific op']
  pm_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Preventive Maintenance (PM) schedule in Infor EAM that governs routine maintenance intervals, inspection tasks, and lubrication schedules for this sort line.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Sort lines are subject to specific regulatory requirements beyond air quality permits (OSHA noise/ergonomic standards, EPA emission limits per line type). This link enables compliance scheduling of li']
  waste_stream_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.waste_stream. Business justification: Sort lines are configured to process specific waste streams (e.g., single-stream residential, commercial OCC). material_stream is a plain-text denormalization of service.waste_stream. This FK enables ']
  amcs_line_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier used to reference this sort line within the AMCS Platform for recycling route management and material flow tracking integration. Enables cross-system reconciliation between MRF operations and collection route data.']
  automation_level varchar [note: 'Degree of automation on the sort line. Manual lines rely entirely on human sorters; semi-automated lines combine optical sorters or mechanical separators with manual quality control; fully automated lines use robotics and optical sorting with minimal human intervention.']
  baler_count varchar [note: 'Number of baling machines installed on or directly serving this sort line. Balers compress recovered commodities into bales for outbound commodity sales. Affects throughput capacity and commodity output rates.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date on which the sort line was formally accepted into production operation following installation testing and validation. Marks the start of the operational lifecycle and warranty period.']
  conveyor_length_m decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total length of the primary sort conveyor belt in meters. Used for maintenance planning (belt replacement scheduling), throughput modeling, and facility layout documentation.']
  conveyor_speed_mpm decimal(6,2) [note: 'Rated operating speed of the sort line conveyor belt in meters per minute (MPM). Directly affects sort quality and throughput rate; used in process optimization and OSHA ergonomic compliance for sorter workload assessment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this sort line master record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and record lifecycle management in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date on which the sort line was permanently taken out of service. Null for active lines. Used for asset lifecycle reporting, capital planning, and EPA facility capacity reporting.']
  design_recovery_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Engineered target material recovery rate expressed as a percentage of inbound material weight that is successfully recovered as saleable commodity. Used as the baseline for diversion rate reporting and performance benchmarking against EPA recycling goals.']
  design_throughput_tpd decimal(10,2) [note: 'Engineered design capacity of the sort line expressed in tons per day (TPD), calculated based on standard operating shift hours. Used for daily production planning, EPA recycling reporting, and diversion rate calculations.']
  design_throughput_tph decimal(10,2) [note: 'Engineered design capacity of the sort line expressed in tons per hour (TPH). Represents the maximum rated processing rate under optimal conditions as specified by the equipment manufacturer or system integrator. Used for capacity planning and performance benchmarking.']
  dust_suppression_system boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a dust suppression or dust collection system is installed on this sort line. Required for air quality compliance under the Clean Air Act and OSHA particulate matter exposure limits, particularly on glass and shredder lines.']
  eddy_current_separator_count varchar [note: 'Number of eddy current separator units installed on this sort line. Eddy current separators use magnetic induction to eject non-ferrous metals (aluminum, copper) from the material stream. Critical for aluminum recovery rates.']
  equipment_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual equipment assets installed on this sort line (e.g., balers, optical sorters, conveyors, eddy current separators, magnetic separators, shredders, glass breakers). Used for maintenance planning and asset inventory management.']
  fire_suppression_system boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an automated fire suppression system (e.g., sprinkler, spark detection and suppression) is installed on this sort line. MRF sort lines processing paper and plastics carry elevated fire risk; fire suppression is required by NFPA 13 and local fire codes.']
  floor_area_sqm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total floor area occupied by the sort line and its associated equipment in square meters. Used for facility layout planning, fire safety compliance, and OSHA egress requirement calculations.']
  glass_breaker_count varchar [note: 'Number of glass breaking/crushing units installed on this sort line. Glass breakers reduce glass to cullet for downstream processing or disposal. Relevant for container sort lines processing single-stream material.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date on which the sort line was physically installed and commissioned at the facility. Used to calculate asset age, depreciation schedules, and to trigger lifecycle replacement planning.']
  last_major_overhaul_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent major overhaul or capital refurbishment of the sort line. Used to assess remaining useful life, schedule next overhaul, and support capital expenditure planning.']
  line_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the sort line within the facility (e.g., MRF-SL-01, OCC-LINE-A). Used in operational communications, work orders, and reporting.']
  line_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name for the sort line (e.g., \'OCC Pre-Sort Line 1\', \'Fiber Sorting Line B\'). Used in operational dashboards, maintenance scheduling, and facility floor plans.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of the sort line. Active indicates normal production operation; maintenance indicates the line is taken offline for scheduled or unscheduled maintenance; commissioning indicates new installation under testing; decommissioned indicates permanently retired.']
  line_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the sort line by the material stream it processes. Pre-sort lines handle initial separation; OCC (Old Corrugated Cardboard) lines process cardboard; fiber lines handle paper; container lines process plastics, metals, and glass; residue lines handle non-recoverable material. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pre_sort|occ|fiber|container|residue|glass|mixed — promote to reference product]']
  magnetic_separator_count varchar [note: 'Number of magnetic separator (overhead magnet or drum magnet) units installed on this sort line for ferrous metal recovery. Ferrous metal recovery rate is directly dependent on magnetic separator performance.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Name of the original equipment manufacturer (OEM) who designed and built the sort line system (e.g., Machinex, Van Dyk, CP Group, Bollegraaf). Used for warranty management, spare parts sourcing, and technical support.']
  max_contamination_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable contamination rate (percentage of non-target material in the output stream) for this sort line before commodity quality is rejected by buyers. Exceeding this threshold triggers quality control interventions and may affect commodity sale prices.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer\'s model designation for the sort line system. Used for spare parts identification, technical documentation retrieval, and warranty claims.']
  noise_level_db decimal(6,2) [note: 'Rated operational noise level of the sort line in decibels (dB). Used for OSHA hearing conservation program compliance, PPE (Personal Protective Equipment) requirements determination, and facility noise permit compliance.']
  operating_shift_count varchar [note: 'Number of production shifts per day this sort line is designed to operate (typically 1, 2, or 3 shifts). Used for daily throughput capacity calculations, workforce scheduling, and operational cost modeling.']
  optical_sorter_count varchar [note: 'Number of optical sorting units installed on this sort line. Optical sorters use near-infrared (NIR) or visible spectrum technology to identify and eject specific material types. Drives automation level classification and throughput capacity.']
  power_consumption_kw decimal(10,2) [note: 'Rated electrical power consumption of the sort line in kilowatts (KW) at full operating capacity. Used for energy cost modeling, utility budgeting, and greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions reporting under EPA and ISO 14001 requirements.']
  residue_rate_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target percentage of inbound material that is expected to be classified as residue (non-recoverable waste sent to landfill disposal). Lower residue rates indicate higher diversion performance. Used in EPA diversion rate reporting and sustainability KPI tracking.']
  rfid_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether RFID (Radio Frequency Identification) technology is installed on this sort line for automated container or material tracking. Supports integration with AMCS Platform for container management and material flow tracking.']
  sap_functional_location varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA Plant Maintenance (PM) functional location code assigned to this sort line. Used to link maintenance work orders, preventive maintenance plans, and cost center allocations in SAP PM and FI/CO modules.']
  screen_count varchar [note: 'Number of disc screen or trommel screen units installed on this sort line for size-based material separation. Screens separate 2D materials (paper, film) from 3D materials (containers, cans) and are a primary separation mechanism in MRF operations.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned serial number uniquely identifying this sort line system. Required for warranty registration, insurance documentation, and regulatory equipment records.']
  shift_duration_hours decimal(4,2) [note: 'Standard duration of each operating shift in hours for this sort line. Combined with operating_shift_count and design_throughput_tph to derive design_throughput_tpd. Used in workforce planning and production scheduling.']
  shredder_count varchar [note: 'Number of shredding units installed on this sort line for size reduction of incoming material. Shredders improve downstream sorting efficiency and are common on OCC and residue lines.']
  station_count varchar [note: 'Total number of manual sort stations installed along the sort line. Determines labor requirements for staffing the line at full capacity and is used in workforce planning and ergonomic compliance assessments.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this sort line master record. Used for change tracking, data synchronization with source systems, and audit compliance.']

  Note: 'Master record for each sort line (conveyor-based sorting system) and its installed equipment within a MRF facility. Captures sort line identity, facility reference, line type (pre-sort, OCC, fiber, container, residue), design throughput (TPH), station count, automation level, and operational status. Includes equipment inventory: individual equipment assets (balers, optical sorters, conveyors, eddy current separators, magnetic separators, shredders, glass breakers) with manufacturer, model, serial number, installation date, design capacity, maintenance schedule references, and Infor EAM asset references. Supports equipment performance tracking, throughput capacity planning, and maintenance coordination.'
}

Table recycling.commodity {
  commodity_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate identifier for each recovered commodity master record in the Materials Recovery Facility (MRF). Primary key for the commodity reference table. Role: MASTER_RESOURCE.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Commodities are governed by specific regulatory requirements (RCRA material classifications, EPA export rules, state hazardous thresholds). Compliance managers need this link to enforce handling/proce']
  waste_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.waste_code. Business justification: Commodities with hazardous_material_flag=true carry a denormalized rcra_waste_code plain column. A proper FK to hazmat.waste_code enforces referential integrity, enables RCRA regulatory reporting by c']
  waste_stream_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.waste_stream. Business justification: Each recyclable commodity originates from a specific waste stream (e.g., OCC from commercial cardboard stream, HDPE from residential single-stream). This link supports diversion credit calculations, c']
  accepted_materials varchar [note: 'Narrative description of materials explicitly accepted within this commodity stream (e.g., \'Corrugated containers, kraft paper bags\' for OCC). Complements the prohibited materials list to fully define the commodity specification for MRF operations and customer education.']
  amcs_commodity_code varchar [note: 'Commodity identifier as configured in the AMCS Platform for route management and MRF dispatching. Used to link commodity master records to AMCS recycling route assignments, container tracking, and inbound material receiving workflows.']
  baling_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this commodity must be baled prior to outbound shipment to end-market buyers. Drives MRF baler scheduling, bale production tracking, and outbound logistics planning. False for loose commodities such as glass cullet or scrap metal.']
  base_price_per_ton decimal(10,2) [note: 'Current or standard base price per ton (USD) for this commodity as used in MRF commodity sales. For positive-value commodities, this is the revenue received per ton sold. For negative-value commodities, this is the cost paid per ton disposed. Sensitive commercial pricing data. Integrated with Oracle Revenue Management and SAP FI/CO.']
  commodity_category varchar [note: 'High-level material category grouping for the commodity. Drives MRF sorting line configuration, equipment routing, and EPA diversion rate reporting by material stream. Serves as the primary CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE for this MASTER_RESOURCE. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: paper|plastics|metals|glass|organics|electronics|textiles|residue|other — promote to reference product]']
  commodity_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the commodity type (e.g., OCC, HDPE, ALU, ONP). Used as the business-facing identifier in SAP MM material master, AMCS commodity catalog, and commodity sales contracts. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this MASTER_RESOURCE.']
  commodity_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the recovered commodity (e.g., \'Old Corrugated Containers\', \'High-Density Polyethylene Natural\', \'Aluminum Used Beverage Cans\'). Serves as the IDENTITY_LABEL for this MASTER_RESOURCE and is used in customer-facing reports, commodity sales invoices, and EPA recycling disclosures.']
  commodity_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the commodity master record. Active commodities are processed and sold from MRF operations. Inactive or discontinued commodities are retained for historical reporting. Pending_approval records are under review for addition to the MRF commodity catalog. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS for this MASTER_RESOURCE.']
  contamination_fee_per_ton decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fee charged per ton (USD) when a commodity load exceeds the maximum contamination percentage threshold. Applied as a deduction from the commodity sale price or as a separate charge to the originating customer account. Configured in Oracle Revenue Management billing rules.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this commodity master record was first created in the system. Supports data lineage, audit trail requirements, and SOX-compliant record management. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  diversion_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether tonnage of this commodity counts toward the diversion rate calculation (percentage of waste diverted from landfill). True for recyclable and compostable commodities; false for residue streams that are landfilled. Drives EPA and state-level diversion rate reporting.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this commodity master record and its associated grade specifications became effective for MRF operations and commodity sales. Used to manage commodity specification version history and ensure correct specifications are applied to historical transactions.']
  end_market_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this commodity is sold to domestic end-market buyers, exported to international markets, or both. Drives logistics planning, export documentation requirements (BOL, customs), and market pricing strategy. Export commodities may require additional DOT and customs compliance.']
  epa_material_category_code varchar [note: 'EPA-assigned material category code used in mandatory recycling and diversion rate reporting submissions. Aligns commodity records with EPA\'s Advancing Sustainable Materials Management (SMM) reporting framework and state environmental quality department reporting requirements.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which this commodity master record or its grade specifications expire and must be reviewed or replaced. Null for open-ended commodity definitions. Supports commodity specification lifecycle management and ensures outdated specifications are not applied to active operations.']
  ghg_emission_factor decimal(10,4) [note: 'Greenhouse gas (GHG) emission factor in metric tons of carbon dioxide equivalent (CO2e) per ton of commodity recycled versus landfilled. Used to calculate GHG emission reductions from recycling activities for sustainability reporting, EPA GHG inventory submissions, and circular economy goal tracking.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'SAP General Ledger (GL) account code to which commodity sales revenue or disposal costs are posted. Ensures accurate financial reporting and P&L attribution by commodity type. Required for SAP FI/CO integration and SOX-compliant financial controls.']
  grade_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the ISRI grade specification including allowable materials, prohibited items, and quality characteristics. Used by MRF quality control staff to assess bale acceptance and by sales teams to communicate product quality to end-market buyers.']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this commodity is classified as a hazardous material under RCRA or DOT regulations. When true, triggers special handling, manifesting, and TSDF routing requirements. Integrates with Enviance EHS for compliance tracking and SAP EHS module for hazardous material management.']
  isri_grade_code varchar [note: 'Standardized grade code assigned by the Institute of Scrap Recycling Industries (ISRI) Scrap Specifications Circular. Defines the accepted quality standard for the commodity and is referenced in commodity purchase/sale contracts, bill of lading (BOL) documentation, and end-market buyer specifications.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this commodity master record was most recently modified. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline, audit trail compliance, and detecting stale commodity specifications. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  market_classification varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the commodity generates revenue (positive_value — MRF receives payment from buyer), incurs a cost (negative_value — MRF pays buyer to accept), or is non-marketable residue. Critical for commodity sales pricing strategy, P&L reporting, and Oracle Revenue Management billing configuration.']
  max_bale_density_lbs_per_cf decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable bale density in pounds per cubic foot for this commodity. Overly dense bales may be rejected by buyers due to handling or processing constraints. Used alongside minimum density to define the acceptable bale density range for quality control.']
  max_contamination_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable contamination percentage (by weight) for an accepted bale or load of this commodity. Loads exceeding this threshold are subject to rejection, downgrade, or contamination fee deduction. Drives MRF quality control acceptance/rejection decisions and end-market buyer contract compliance.']
  max_moisture_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable moisture content percentage (by weight) for this commodity. Excess moisture reduces commodity value, increases transportation weight, and may cause rejection by end-market buyers. Used in MRF quality control inspections and bale certification.']
  max_storage_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days this commodity may be stored at the MRF before quality degradation or regulatory limits require outbound shipment. Drives inventory turnover alerts in SAP MM and AMCS commodity inventory management. Critical for hazardous commodities with RCRA storage time limits.']
  min_bale_density_lbs_per_cf decimal(8,2) [note: 'Minimum required bale density in pounds per cubic foot for this commodity. Bales below this density threshold may be rejected by buyers or subject to price discounts. Used to configure MRF baling equipment settings and quality control acceptance criteria.']
  min_processing_throughput_tpd decimal(8,2) [note: 'Minimum expected processing throughput in tons per day (TPD) for this commodity on the MRF sort line. Used for MRF capacity planning, equipment performance benchmarking, and operational KPI reporting. Below-threshold throughput triggers operational review.']
  ppe_requirements varchar [note: 'Description of required Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) for MRF workers handling this commodity (e.g., \'Cut-resistant gloves, safety glasses\' for metals; \'Respirator, chemical-resistant gloves\' for hazardous commodities). Supports OSHA compliance and worker safety training programs.']
  price_floor_per_ton decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable sale price per ton (USD) below which the commodity should not be sold without management approval. Used in Oracle Revenue Management to trigger pricing exception workflows and protect MRF revenue margins during market downturns.']
  pricing_basis varchar [note: 'Method by which the commodity sale price is determined: spot (current market spot price), index (tied to a published commodity index such as RISI or AMM), contract (fixed price per sales contract), or negotiated (case-by-case negotiation). Drives Oracle Revenue Management pricing configuration and commodity sales contract terms.']
  prohibited_materials varchar [note: 'Narrative description of materials explicitly prohibited from inclusion in this commodity stream (e.g., \'No food-soiled paper, no wax-coated board\' for OCC; \'No PVC, no colored HDPE\' for natural HDPE). Used by MRF sorting staff, quality control inspectors, and driver education programs to prevent contamination.']
  recyclability_tier varchar [note: 'Classification of the commodity\'s recyclability and market demand: tier_1 (high-value, readily recyclable with established markets), tier_2 (moderate-value, recyclable with limited markets), tier_3 (low-value, difficult to recycle), residue (non-recyclable, destined for disposal). Drives diversion rate calculations and sustainability reporting.']
  reference_price_index varchar [note: 'Name of the published commodity price index used as the pricing reference for index-priced commodities (e.g., \'RISI OCC Index\', \'AMM Aluminum Index\', \'Fastmarkets HDPE Index\'). Null for spot or contract-priced commodities. Used in Oracle Revenue Management for automated price updates.']
  resin_code varchar [note: 'Society of the Plastics Industry (SPI) Resin Identification Code (RIC) for plastic commodities (1=PET, 2=HDPE, 3=PVC, 4=LDPE, 5=PP, 6=PS, 7=Other). Null for non-plastic commodities. Required for plastics market reporting and end-market buyer specifications. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 1|2|3|4|5|6|7 — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sort_line_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the MRF sorting line or processing stream assigned to this commodity (e.g., \'FIBER-1\', \'PLASTIC-2\', \'METAL-OCC\'). Used by AMCS Platform for route-to-sort-line assignment and MRF equipment configuration. Supports throughput (TPD) reporting by sort line.']
  standard_bale_weight_lbs decimal(8,2) [note: 'Target or standard weight in pounds for a single bale of this commodity as produced by MRF baling equipment. Used for bale production planning, outbound load configuration, and weight-based pricing reconciliation with end-market buyers.']
  storage_requirement varchar [note: 'Storage condition requirement for this commodity at the MRF prior to outbound shipment (e.g., indoor for paper to prevent moisture, hazmat_storage for hazardous commodities, outdoor for metals). Drives MRF facility layout planning and OSHA/EPA storage compliance.']
  subcategory varchar [note: 'Secondary classification within the material category providing finer segmentation (e.g., \'corrugated\' within paper, \'PET bottles\' within plastics, \'ferrous\' within metals). Used for detailed MRF throughput reporting and commodity-specific market pricing.']
  tclp_testing_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Toxicity Characteristic Leaching Procedure (TCLP) testing is required for this commodity prior to disposal or sale. Applicable to commodities that may exhibit hazardous characteristics under RCRA. Triggers laboratory testing workflow in Enviance EHS.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measure used for weighing, pricing, and reporting this commodity (e.g., ton for paper/metals, lb for small-volume materials, bale for pre-baled commodities). Aligns with SAP MM base unit of measure and Oracle Revenue Management pricing unit. Serves as the MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE classification for this MASTER_RESOURCE.']

  Note: 'Reference master for all recovered commodity types and their quality grade specifications processed and sold from MRF operations. Captures commodity identity (code, name, category), ISRI grade codes, grade-level quality specifications (max contamination %, moisture threshold, bale density requirements, prohibited materials), market classification (positive-value vs residue), unit of measure, and recyclability tier. Grade specifications define acceptance/rejection criteria and drive sales pricing premiums or discounts. SSOT for commodity identity and quality standards across recycling and billing domains.'
}

Table recycling.commodity_grade {
  commodity_grade_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each commodity grade specification record in the Materials Recovery Facility (MRF) quality reference table. [REFERENCE_LOOKUP role — this is a reference/specification table defining grade standards; per canonical-attrs-enforced rule, REFERENCE_LOOKUP is exempt from per-role minimums. _canonical_skip_reason: commodity_grade is a reference lookup table defining quality grade specifications for MRF commodities; it has its own identity and lifecycle but functions as a standards reference, not a transactional or master-party entity.]']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to recycling.commodity. Business justification: commodity_grade defines quality specifications for a specific commodity type. Currently commodity_type and commodity_subtype are stored as denormalized strings on commodity_grade. Adding commodity_id ']
  superseded_by_grade_commodity_grade_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the commodity_grade_id of the newer grade specification that replaces this record when a grade is deprecated or revised. Null if this is the current active version. Supports grade version lineage tracking and ensures operational systems transition to updated specifications.']
  applicable_mrf_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or operational division for which this grade specification applies (e.g., \'Northeast\', \'Southeast\', \'National\'). Some grade specifications may vary by region due to local buyer requirements or state recycling regulations. Null or \'National\' indicates the grade applies enterprise-wide.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which this commodity grade specification was formally approved by the authorized quality manager or operations director. Required for quality management system documentation and regulatory audit readiness.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the MRF quality manager or operations director who approved this grade specification for operational use. Required for quality management system compliance and audit trail. Sourced from Workday HCM employee records.']
  bale_dimension_spec varchar [note: 'Required or target bale dimensions (length x width x height in inches) for this commodity grade as specified by buyers or ISRI standards (e.g., \'60x30x48\'). Ensures compatibility with buyer receiving equipment and maximizes trailer load efficiency. Used in baler setup configuration.']
  buyer_acceptance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether material classified under this grade requires explicit buyer acceptance confirmation before outbound shipment. True for specialty or off-grade materials where buyers must pre-approve loads. False for standard grades with standing purchase agreements. Drives outbound logistics workflow in AMCS.']
  co2e_avoided_per_ton decimal(10,4) [note: 'Estimated metric tons of CO2e greenhouse gas emissions avoided per ton of commodity recovered and sold at this grade, compared to virgin material production. Used for GHG emissions reporting, sustainability disclosures, and circular economy impact quantification. Sourced from EPA WARM (Waste Reduction Model) emission factors.']
  color_specification varchar [note: 'Color or color mix specification required for this commodity grade (e.g., \'clear only\', \'clear and green\', \'mixed colors acceptable\', \'brown only\'). Primarily applicable to glass and PET plastic grades where color separation directly impacts end-market value and processing requirements at buyer facilities.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this commodity grade specification record was first created in the system. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and compliance documentation requirements. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX per enterprise data standards.']
  diversion_rate_credit boolean [note: 'Indicates whether material sold under this commodity grade qualifies for diversion rate credit in EPA and state recycling program reporting. True for grades that represent genuine material recovery; False for off-grade or contaminated material routed to landfill or WTE. Impacts sustainability KPI reporting and circular economy goal tracking.']
  domestic_export_eligibility varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this commodity grade is eligible for domestic sale only, export sale only, or both. Export eligibility is subject to Basel Convention restrictions on hazardous waste exports and country-specific import regulations. Drives market routing decisions in commodity sales management.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this commodity grade specification becomes effective and applicable for quality control acceptance/rejection decisions and commodity pricing. Enables versioned grade specifications to align with market condition changes or updated ISRI standards.']
  end_market_type varchar [note: 'Type of end-market or buyer facility that typically purchases this commodity grade (e.g., \'paper mill\', \'plastic reprocessor\', \'aluminum smelter\', \'glass beneficiator\', \'steel mill\', \'export broker\'). Informs market development strategy and outbound logistics planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: paper_mill|plastic_reprocessor|aluminum_smelter|glass_beneficiator|steel_mill|export_broker|compost_facility|wte_facility — promote to reference product]']
  epa_commodity_code varchar [note: 'EPA-assigned commodity classification code used for federal recycling diversion rate reporting and Solid Waste Information System (SWIS) submissions. Enables accurate tracking of material recovery volumes by commodity type for regulatory compliance and sustainability reporting.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which this commodity grade specification is no longer valid and must be superseded by a new version. Null indicates the grade specification is open-ended with no planned expiration. Supports versioned grade management when ISRI standards or buyer contract requirements change.']
  grade_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the commodity grade specification (e.g., OCC-A, PET-1, ALU-P). Used as the externally-known business identifier in SAP MM material master, AMCS commodity tracking, and buyer purchase orders. Aligns with ISRI commodity grade coding conventions.']
  grade_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the commodity grade specification (e.g., \'Grade A Old Corrugated Containers\', \'Premium Clear PET\', \'Standard Mixed Paper\', \'Off-Grade Aluminum\'). Used in quality control reports, buyer contracts, and MRF operational dashboards.']
  grade_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the commodity grade specification. Active grades are in use for quality control and pricing. Inactive grades are suspended from use. Deprecated grades have been superseded by updated specifications. Under Review grades are pending revision approval. Controls whether the grade is available for selection in MRF quality control workflows.']
  grade_tier varchar [note: 'Quality tier classification of the grade specification indicating relative market value and purity level. Premium and Grade A command market premiums; Standard meets baseline buyer requirements; Off-Grade triggers price discounts; Rejected material is non-saleable and requires alternative disposition. Drives pricing factor application in Oracle Revenue Management.']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this commodity grade involves or may contain hazardous materials subject to RCRA, DOT, or EPA hazardous waste regulations. True triggers mandatory hazardous waste handling protocols, manifest documentation requirements, and TSDF routing. Integrates with Enviance EHS compliance tracking.']
  isri_spec_reference varchar [note: 'Official ISRI Scrap Specifications Circular grade designation code that this internal grade maps to (e.g., \'ISRI-11\' for OCC, \'ISRI-600\' for Aluminum Cans). Enables standardized communication with commodity buyers, brokers, and end-markets. Required for export commodity sales documentation.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this commodity grade specification record was most recently modified. Used to detect stale specifications, trigger review workflows, and maintain data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX per enterprise data standards.']
  market_price_factor decimal(6,4) [note: 'Multiplicative premium or discount factor applied to the base commodity market price for this grade (e.g., 1.15 = 15% premium for Premium grade; 0.85 = 15% discount for Off-Grade; 1.00 = no adjustment for Standard). Used in Oracle Revenue Management to calculate commodity sale prices and MRF revenue projections.']
  max_bale_weight_lbs decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable bale weight in pounds for this commodity grade. Ensures compliance with DOT weight regulations for transport and safe handling limits for forklift and loading equipment. Bales exceeding this limit require splitting before outbound shipment.']
  max_contamination_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum percentage of non-target or prohibited materials allowable in a bale or load for this grade to be accepted by buyers (e.g., 2.00 for Grade A OCC = max 2% contamination). Exceeding this threshold triggers grade downgrade or rejection. Used in MRF quality control acceptance/rejection decisions and contamination tracking.']
  max_moisture_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable moisture content percentage for this commodity grade. Excess moisture reduces commodity value, increases transport weight, and can cause material degradation. Measured at bale production or outbound load inspection. Critical for paper and fiber grades where moisture directly impacts pulp mill acceptance.']
  max_particle_size_inches decimal(6,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable individual particle or item size in inches for material classified under this grade. Oversized items can damage processing equipment at end-market facilities and may trigger load rejection. Particularly relevant for glass, metal, and plastic grades.']
  min_bale_density_lbs_per_cuft decimal(7,2) [note: 'Minimum required bale density in pounds per cubic foot for this commodity grade. Ensures structural integrity during transport and storage, and maximizes trailer payload efficiency. Bales failing density requirements may be rejected by buyers or require re-baling. Monitored via baler equipment sensors integrated with AMCS.']
  min_purity_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum required percentage of the target commodity material in a bale or load for this grade classification (e.g., 98.00 for Premium Aluminum = minimum 98% pure aluminum content). Complements the contamination threshold by specifying the positive purity floor rather than the negative contamination ceiling.']
  min_sample_size_lbs decimal(7,2) [note: 'Minimum weight in pounds of material sample required for quality control testing under this grade specification. Ensures statistical validity of contamination and purity measurements. Applies when sort_purity_test_method is grab_sample or full_sort.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, buyer-specific requirements, special handling instructions, or clarifications related to this commodity grade specification. May include references to specific buyer contracts, seasonal market conditions, or equipment-specific processing notes.']
  prohibited_materials varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list of materials explicitly prohibited from inclusion in this commodity grade (e.g., \'food waste, hazardous materials, medical waste, batteries, Styrofoam, plastic bags\'). Any presence of prohibited materials may result in load rejection, buyer chargebacks, or regulatory violations. Referenced during MRF sorting quality audits and inbound load inspections.']
  rejection_disposition varchar [note: 'Default disposition action for material that fails to meet this grade specification. Landfill routes rejected material to disposal; Reprocess returns material to the MRF sorting line; Downgrade reclassifies to a lower grade; Return to Sender sends contaminated loads back to the collection route origin; WTE (Waste-to-Energy) routes to energy recovery. Drives operational workflow in AMCS.']
  requires_lab_testing boolean [note: 'Indicates whether material classified under this grade requires laboratory analysis (e.g., TCLP — Toxicity Characteristic Leaching Procedure testing, moisture analysis, or purity assay) before sale or shipment. True for grades with strict purity or regulatory requirements. Triggers lab sample workflow in Enviance EHS.']
  sort_purity_test_method varchar [note: 'Quality control testing method used to verify that a bale or load meets this grade specification. Visual Sort is a manual inspector review; Grab Sample is a representative sample extraction; Full Sort is complete material separation and weighing; Lab Analysis is laboratory chemical or physical testing; Automated Sensor uses optical or NIR sorting equipment data.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of this commodity grade specification record. Incremented each time the grade specification is revised (e.g., contamination threshold updated, ISRI reference changed). Enables audit trail of specification changes and ensures quality control decisions reference the correct version.']

  Note: 'Reference table defining quality grade specifications for each commodity type processed at MRF facilities. Captures grade code, commodity reference, grade name (e.g., Grade A, Premium, Standard, Off-Grade), maximum allowable contamination percentage, moisture content threshold, minimum bale density (lbs/cubic ft), prohibited materials list, ISRI specification reference, and grade-specific market premium or discount factor. Used for quality control acceptance/rejection decisions and commodity sales pricing.'
}

Table recycling.sort_session {
  sort_session_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for a discrete sorting production session on a Materials Recovery Facility (MRF) sort line. Primary key for this transactional record.']
  compliance_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_inspection. Business justification: Compliance inspections often observe active sort sessions to verify operational compliance with permit conditions (throughput rates, contamination handling, safety protocols). Links inspection observa']
  downtime_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order.
