  program_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the recycling program. Active indicates the program is currently collecting and processing materials. Pending indicates the program is approved but not yet operational. Suspended indicates temporary halt (e.g., due to contamination issues or contract dispute). Closed indicates the program has permanently ended.']
  public_education_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the program contract or municipal agreement requires Waste Management to provide public education and outreach materials (e.g., accepted materials guides, contamination reduction campaigns). Drives marketing and communications obligations.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Internal Waste Management region code identifying the operational region responsible for this recycling program. Aligns with SAP organizational hierarchy and AMCS regional dispatching configuration.']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which diversion rate, tonnage, and contamination reports must be submitted to the sponsoring municipality, state agency, or EPA under this program\'s contractual and regulatory obligations.']
  revenue_share_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of net commodity sales revenue shared back with the sponsoring municipality or customer account under this program. Expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00). Null if no revenue-share arrangement exists. Tracked in Oracle Revenue Management.']
  start_date date [note: 'The date on which the recycling program became or is scheduled to become operationally active. Used for contract compliance tracking, diversion rate reporting, and program lifecycle management.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this recycling program record was last modified in the Databricks Silver Layer. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for incremental data processing, change tracking, and audit compliance.']

  Note: 'Master record defining recycling collection programs served by MRF facilities, including municipal curbside programs, commercial recycling programs, drop-off center programs, and special collection events. Captures program name, program type, sponsoring municipality or customer account reference, service area (geographic boundary), accepted materials list, program start date, program end date, diversion rate target (%), annual tonnage commitment, program status, and AMCS program reference. Links collection operations to MRF processing for end-to-end diversion tracking.'
}

Table recycling.program_facility_assignment {
  program_facility_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the program_facility_assignment association']
  mrf_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the MRF facility that processes inbound recyclables for the assigned recycling program.']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the recycling program that is being assigned to an MRF facility under this operational agreement.']
  annual_tonnage_commitment decimal [note: 'Contractually committed annual volume of recyclable material (in short tons) allocated to this specific MRF facility under this recycling program. When a program spans multiple MRFs, the total program tonnage is split across facilities — this attribute captures the per-facility allocation. Sourced from detection phase relationship_data.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of this program-facility assignment. Allows a program to be active at one MRF while suspended at another without affecting the program master record.']
  diversion_rate_target_pct decimal [note: 'Target percentage of collected recyclable volume to be diverted from landfill through this MRF facility for this specific program. When a program spans multiple MRFs, each facility may carry a different diversion target. Sourced from detection phase relationship_data.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this program-facility assignment became or is scheduled to become operationally active. Required to track when a program began routing loads to a specific MRF.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this program-facility assignment is scheduled to end or has ended. Null if the assignment is open-ended. Enables historical tracking of which MRFs served a program over time.']
  processing_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to inbound loads from this recycling program when scheduled for sort line processing at this specific MRF facility. Differs per program-facility pairing and cannot be stored on the program or facility alone.']
  tipping_fee_rate decimal [note: 'The per-ton tipping fee rate charged for inbound loads from this recycling program at this specific MRF facility. Rates may vary by program type and facility, making this a true relationship-level attribute. Sourced explicitly from detection phase relationship_data.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the operational Contract/Assignment between a recycling_program and an mrf_facility. It captures the specific terms and operational parameters governing how a given recycling program\'s inbound recyclable loads are processed at a specific MRF facility. Each record links one recycling_program to one mrf_facility and carries attributes — processing priority, tonnage allocation, diversion target, and tipping fee rate — that exist only in the context of this specific program-facility pairing and cannot be correctly stored on either the program or the facility alone.. Existence Justification: In Waste Management\'s recycling operations, a single recycling program (e.g., a regional municipal curbside program) can be processed across multiple MRF facilities — particularly for large regional programs that span service areas served by different MRFs. Conversely, each MRF facility processes inbound recyclables from multiple distinct recycling programs simultaneously (municipal, commercial, drop-off, special events). The business actively manages these program-facility assignments with operational attributes such as processing priority, tonnage allocation, and diversion targets that are specific to each program-facility pairing, not to either entity alone.'
}

Table landfill.site {
  site_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the landfill site. Primary key for all landfill operational, environmental, and compliance data anchored to this facility.']
  billing_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_account. Business justification: Large landfill sites operate as separate billing entities for host community agreements and inter-company transfers. Site generates internal invoices for corporate waste or bills host community for im']
  franchise_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.franchise_agreement. Business justification: A landfill site operates under a municipal franchise agreement that governs its territorial rights and operational obligations. Franchise compliance reporting and renewal management require direct lin']
  regulated_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulated_facility. Business justification: Landfill sites are regulated facilities. Compliance domain maintains canonical regulated facility registry with EPA IDs, RCRA status, Title V designation, and compliance risk levels that landfill oper']
  site_enviance_facility_id varchar [note: 'Facility identifier as configured in the Enviance EHS system, which is the system of record for environmental compliance, permit tracking, and incident management at this facility. Used for cross-system data integration and compliance record linkage.']
  site_epa_facility_id varchar [note: 'EPA-assigned facility identifier used for federal environmental reporting, RCRA compliance tracking, and CERCLA/Superfund obligations. Format: two-letter state code followed by 10-digit numeric identifier.']
  tsdf_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.tsdf_facility. Business justification: Landfill sites permitted to accept hazardous waste must maintain RCRA TSDF status. The tsdf_facility record governs permitted waste codes, financial assurance requirements, and manifest approval statu']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the landfill facility. Used for regulatory correspondence, emergency response coordination, and logistics routing.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for the landfill facility (suite, building, gate number, or additional location descriptor).']
  airspace_survey_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent engineering airspace survey used to calculate remaining airspace. Ensures remaining_airspace_cy reflects current conditions and supports audit trail for capacity reporting.']
  cap_system_type varchar [note: 'Type of final cover (cap) system installed or planned for the landfill. The cap is the final cover system placed over closed portions of the landfill to minimize infiltration and control LFG emissions. \'final_cap\' indicates permanent closure cap installed; \'interim_cap\' indicates temporary cover on inactive areas; \'none\' indicates active filling area; \'planned\' indicates cap design approved but not yet constructed.']
  cercla_status varchar [note: 'Current CERCLA/Superfund status of the facility. Indicates whether the site is listed on the National Priorities List (NPL) or subject to CERCLA remedial action obligations. Drives legal liability accruals and environmental compliance reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: not_listed|npl_listed|remedial_investigation|remedial_action|completed|other — promote to reference product]']
  city varchar [note: 'Municipality or city in which the landfill facility is located. Used for jurisdictional determination, local enforcement agency (LEA) assignment, and host community agreement management.']
  classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory and operational classification of the landfill facility. MSW (Municipal Solid Waste) accepts household and commercial waste; C&D (Construction and Demolition) accepts construction debris; monofill accepts a single waste type; industrial accepts non-hazardous industrial waste; hazardous is a RCRA Subtitle C facility. Drives applicable regulatory framework and permitted waste streams.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date on which the landfill facility ceased accepting waste and initiated the formal closure process. Null for active facilities. Triggers post-closure care obligations under RCRA Subtitle D including 30-year monitoring requirements.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the facility location (e.g., USA). Supports multi-country enterprise reporting and regulatory framework determination.']
  county varchar [note: 'County in which the landfill facility is located. Critical for determining applicable local solid waste authority jurisdiction, host community agreement obligations, and county-level regulatory reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the landfill site master record was first created in the enterprise data platform. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and record lifecycle management per SOX internal controls.']
  ghg_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility is subject to mandatory greenhouse gas (GHG) reporting under EPA 40 CFR Part 98 (Subpart HH for MSW landfills). Facilities exceeding the 25,000 metric ton CO2e threshold must report annually. Drives GHG data collection workflows in Enviance EHS.']
  groundwater_monitoring_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility is subject to groundwater monitoring requirements under RCRA Subtitle D or state permit conditions. Drives scheduling of monitoring well sampling events and Enviance EHS compliance tracking.']
  host_community_agreement_ref varchar [note: 'Reference number or document identifier for the host community agreement (HCA) between the facility operator and the local municipality. HCAs govern tipping fee sharing, traffic mitigation, odor controls, and community benefit obligations. Links to contract management records in Salesforce CRM.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'GPS latitude coordinate of the landfill facility centroid in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum). Used for geospatial analysis, route optimization, emergency response, and environmental monitoring zone mapping.']
  lea_name varchar [note: 'Name of the Local Enforcement Agency (LEA) responsible for routine inspection and enforcement at the facility. In California and other states, LEAs are county-level agencies delegated enforcement authority by the state. Required for compliance contact management and inspection scheduling.']
  leachate_management_type varchar [note: 'Type of leachate collection and management system installed at the facility. Leachate is the liquid that drains through the waste mass and must be managed to prevent groundwater contamination. Drives operational costs, permit conditions, and Clean Water Act (CWA) compliance obligations.']
  lfg_collection_system boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an active landfill gas (LFG) collection and control system is installed at the facility. LFG systems capture methane for waste-to-energy (WTE) or renewable natural gas (RNG) conversion, and are required under EPA New Source Performance Standards (NSPS) for qualifying facilities. Drives Clean Air Act compliance and sustainability reporting.']
  lfg_utilization_type varchar [note: 'Method by which captured landfill gas (LFG) is utilized or destroyed. \'flare_only\' indicates gas is combusted without energy recovery; \'electricity_generation\' indicates WTE power generation; \'RNG\' indicates renewable natural gas pipeline injection; \'direct_use\' indicates direct thermal use; \'none\' indicates no LFG system. Drives GHG emissions reporting and sustainability metrics.']
  liner_system_type varchar [note: 'Type of engineered liner system installed at the base of the landfill to prevent leachate migration into groundwater. Determines applicable regulatory standards, leachate collection design, and groundwater monitoring requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: single_composite|double_composite|clay|geomembrane|no_liner|other — promote to reference product if additional liner subtypes are needed]']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'GPS longitude coordinate of the landfill facility centroid in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum). Used for geospatial analysis, route optimization, emergency response, and environmental monitoring zone mapping.']
  monitoring_well_count varchar [note: 'Total number of groundwater monitoring wells installed at the facility perimeter and downgradient locations as required by the facility permit. Used for compliance scheduling, sampling cost estimation, and permit condition verification.']
  open_date date [note: 'Date on which the landfill facility first began accepting waste. Used for site age calculations, post-closure care timeline planning, and historical capacity consumption analysis.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the landfill site. \'active\' indicates waste is actively being received; \'closed\' indicates waste acceptance has ceased; \'post_closure\' indicates the site is in the regulatory post-closure care period; \'permitted\' indicates permit obtained but operations not yet commenced; \'construction\' indicates liner/cell construction in progress; \'suspended\' indicates operations temporarily halted.']
  permit_expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the current RCRA Subtitle D or state solid waste operating permit expires. Triggers permit renewal workflow in Enviance EHS. Facilities operating beyond this date without renewal are in violation of RCRA and state regulations.']
  permit_issued_date date [note: 'Date on which the current operating permit was issued by the regulatory authority. Used to calculate permit duration, track renewal cycles, and support compliance audit documentation.']
  permitted_acreage decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total land area in acres authorized under the current operating permit for waste disposal activities. Drives capacity planning, cell development scheduling, and permit boundary compliance monitoring.']
  permitted_daily_receipt_tons decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum tonnage per day (TPD) the facility is permitted to receive under its current operating permit. Operational throughput must not exceed this limit without permit modification. Used for daily tonnage monitoring and permit compliance alerts in Enviance EHS.']
  post_closure_end_date date [note: 'Projected or actual date on which the mandatory post-closure care period ends (minimum 30 years after closure under RCRA Subtitle D). Used for long-range financial liability estimation and closure cost accrual under IFRS/GAAP.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'US ZIP code for the landfill facility address. Used for geographic analysis, regulatory jurisdiction mapping, and logistics coordination.']
  rcra_permit_number varchar [note: 'State-issued RCRA Subtitle D operating permit number authorizing the facility to receive and dispose of municipal solid waste (MSW) or other permitted waste streams. Required for regulatory compliance and annual permit renewal tracking.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Name of the primary state or local regulatory agency with permitting and enforcement authority over the facility (e.g., \'California Department of Resources Recycling and Recovery\', \'Texas Commission on Environmental Quality\'). Determines applicable state-specific regulatory requirements beyond federal RCRA Subtitle D baseline.']
  remaining_airspace_cy decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current remaining permitted airspace in cubic yards, calculated from the most recent engineering survey. Critical KPI for capacity planning, site life estimation, and financial asset valuation under IFRS/GAAP. Updated following each annual or semi-annual airspace survey.']
  sap_plant_code varchar [note: 'Four-character SAP S/4HANA plant code assigned to this landfill facility. Used for financial cost center assignment, materials management (MM), plant maintenance (PM), and EHS module integration. Enables cross-domain financial and operational reporting.']
  site_code varchar [note: 'Internally assigned alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the landfill site across enterprise systems including SAP S/4HANA, Enviance EHS, and Infor EAM. Used as the cross-system business key.']
  site_name varchar [note: 'Official operating name of the landfill facility as registered with regulatory authorities and used in all internal and external communications.']
  standard_tipping_fee decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard gate rate charged per short ton for waste disposal at the facility (tipping fee). This is the base rate before contractual discounts or surcharges. Confidential commercial pricing data used in Oracle Revenue Management rate configuration and financial planning.']
  state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state abbreviation where the landfill facility is located. Determines applicable state environmental quality department regulations, state permit requirements, and state-specific RCRA program authority.']
  swis_registration_number varchar [note: 'State Solid Waste Information System (SWIS) registration number assigned by the state environmental agency. Used for EPA SWIS reporting submissions and state-level compliance tracking.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the landfill facility location (e.g., \'America/Chicago\', \'America/Los_Angeles\'). Required for accurate timestamp interpretation of operational events, regulatory reporting deadlines, and scheduling across multi-site enterprise operations.']
  total_design_capacity_cy decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total permitted airspace capacity of the landfill in cubic yards as approved in the facility design engineering report. Represents the maximum volume of waste and cover material the site is engineered and permitted to contain. Used for long-range capacity planning and permit compliance.']
  total_design_capacity_tons decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total permitted disposal capacity of the landfill in short tons as approved in the facility design engineering report. Complements cubic yard capacity for tonnage-based regulatory reporting and financial capacity valuation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the landfill site master record. Used for change detection, data synchronization across integrated systems, and audit trail maintenance.']
  waste_streams_accepted varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list of waste stream types the facility is permitted to accept (e.g., \'MSW, C&D, industrial non-hazardous, special waste\'). Drives waste acceptance screening, manifest verification, and permit compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MSW|C&D|industrial_non_hazardous|special_waste|ash|sludge|other — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Master record for each landfill facility. Captures site identity, physical location (GPS coordinates, address, county, state), permitted acreage, total design capacity (cubic yards and tons), current operational status (active/closed/post-closure), RCRA Subtitle D permit number, EPA facility ID, state SWIS registration number, liner system type, leachate management system type, LFG collection system presence, host community agreement reference, regulatory jurisdiction, and site classification (MSW, C&D, monofill). Top-level anchor for all landfill operational, environmental, and compliance data.'
}

Table landfill.cell {
  cell_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the landfill cell. Primary key for the cell master record.']
  capital_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.capital_project. Business justification: Each landfill cell is constructed as a discrete capital project with design, permitting, and construction phases. Capital project completion triggers cell activation and asset capitalization. Construc']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.facility. Business justification: Cells are sub-components of facility structures for asset hierarchy, cost center allocation, capital project tracking, and consolidated environmental reporting. New attribute establishes proper facili']
  landfill_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent landfill site where this cell is located. Links to the landfill facility master record.']
  actual_fill_date date [note: 'Actual date when the cell reached design capacity and was closed to further waste placement.']
  area_acres decimal(10,2) [note: 'Horizontal footprint area of the cell in acres, measured at the base liner surface.']
  average_daily_tonnage decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average tons per day (TPD) received in this cell during active operations, used for capacity planning and operational forecasting.']
  base_elevation_feet decimal(10,2) [note: 'Elevation of the cell base (liner surface) in feet above mean sea level, used for airspace calculations and grading plans.']
  cell_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name for the cell used in operational and planning documents.']
  cell_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the cell, typically alphanumeric code used in operational communications and regulatory reporting (e.g., CELL-A1, PHASE2-C3).']
  cell_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the cell in its lifecycle: planned (designed but not built), under_construction (being prepared), active (receiving waste), filled (at capacity but not closed), closed (operations ceased, interim cover applied), capped (final cover system installed).']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date when formal closure activities (final grading, interim cover installation) were completed and the cell transitioned from active to closed status.']
  compaction_ratio_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target volume reduction ratio for waste compaction operations in this cell, typically expressed as ratio of loose volume to compacted volume (e.g., 4.0 means 4:1 reduction).']
  construction_completion_date date [note: 'Date when cell construction was completed and the cell was certified ready for waste placement.']
  construction_start_date date [note: 'Date when physical construction of the cell (excavation, liner installation) commenced.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this cell record was first created in the system.']
  current_elevation_feet decimal(10,2) [note: 'Current elevation of the active waste surface in feet above mean sea level, updated from periodic topographic surveys.']
  daily_cover_material_type varchar [note: 'Type of material used for daily cover application to control odors, vectors, and litter: soil (traditional 6-inch soil cover), alternative_daily_cover (ADC such as compost, shredded green waste), tarps (reusable geomembrane tarps), foam (spray-applied foam), auto_daily_cover (automated tarp systems).']
  design_capacity_cubic_yards decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total permitted airspace volume for waste placement in cubic yards, as specified in the engineering design and regulatory permit.']
  design_capacity_tons decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total permitted waste tonnage capacity, calculated from design volume and assumed compaction density.']
  environmental_monitoring_frequency varchar [note: 'Required frequency for environmental monitoring activities (groundwater sampling, gas monitoring, leachate testing) as specified in the permit.']
  final_cap_installation_date date [note: 'Date when the final cover system (cap) was installed over the closed cell, marking the beginning of post-closure care period.']
  gas_collection_start_date date [note: 'Date when active gas collection operations commenced in this cell, required for EPA NSPS/EG compliance tracking.']
  gas_collection_system_installed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an active landfill gas collection system (vertical wells and/or horizontal collectors) has been installed in this cell.']
  geometry_coordinates varchar [note: 'Well-Known Text (WKT) representation of the cell boundary polygon coordinates for GIS mapping and spatial analysis.']
  groundwater_monitoring_wells varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of groundwater monitoring well identifiers assigned to this cell for environmental compliance monitoring per RCRA Subtitle D requirements.']
  liner_system_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the engineered liner system installed at the base of the cell to prevent leachate migration: single_composite (one geomembrane + clay layer), double_composite (two geomembrane layers with leak detection), single_geomembrane, clay_only (compacted clay liner), enhanced_composite (advanced multi-layer system).']
  liner_thickness_inches decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total thickness of the liner system in inches, including all geomembrane and clay layers.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special conditions, or historical context relevant to this cell.']
  opening_date date [note: 'Date when the cell was opened for active waste placement operations.']
  permitted_waste_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of waste types authorized for disposal in this cell per regulatory permit (e.g., MSW, C&D, special waste, industrial non-hazardous). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MSW|C&D|special_waste|industrial_non_hazardous|asbestos|contaminated_soil|sludge|ash — promote to reference product]']
  phase_number varchar [note: 'Development phase identifier indicating the sequential construction phase of the landfill expansion plan.']
  post_closure_care_end_date date [note: 'Projected or actual date when the 30-year post-closure care period (monitoring, maintenance, gas management) will end or ended.']
  projected_fill_date date [note: 'Forecasted date when the cell is expected to reach design capacity based on current waste placement rates and airspace consumption trends.']
  remaining_capacity_cubic_yards decimal(15,2) [note: 'Current available airspace for waste placement in cubic yards, updated based on survey measurements and waste placement records.']
  responsible_engineer varchar [note: 'Name of the licensed professional engineer responsible for design certification and ongoing compliance oversight for this cell.']
  tipping_fee_per_ton decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard disposal charge per ton of waste placed in this cell, used for revenue tracking and cost allocation. May vary by waste type and customer contract.']
  top_elevation_feet decimal(10,2) [note: 'Permitted final elevation of the cell top surface in feet above mean sea level, defining the maximum fill height.']
  total_tonnage_received decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cumulative tonnage of waste placed in this cell since opening, tracked for capacity management and regulatory reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this cell record was last modified in the system.']

  Note: 'Master record for each discrete waste placement cell within a landfill site. Tracks cell identifier (CID), cell phase (active, filled, closed), design volume (cubic yards), permitted waste types (MSW, C&D, special waste), liner system specifications (single, double, composite), leachate collection system tie-in, cell opening date, projected fill date, actual closure date, base elevation, top elevation, and cell geometry coordinates. Supports airspace consumption tracking and cell sequencing for capacity planning. Sourced from AMCS Platform and Enviance EHS.'
}
