  treatment_standard_code varchar [note: 'Land Disposal Restrictions (LDR) treatment standard code that applies to this waste before land disposal. References 40 CFR Part 268 treatment standards. Specifies required treatment technology or concentration limits (e.g., DEACT for deactivation, IMERC for incineration, specific mg/L or mg/kg limits for TCLP constituents).']
  treatment_subcategory varchar [note: 'Subcategory designation for wastes with multiple treatment standards based on waste form or concentration. Used when a single waste code has different treatment requirements for wastewater versus non-wastewater forms, or for different concentration ranges.']
  universal_treatment_standard varchar [note: 'Universal Treatment Standard concentration limits for underlying hazardous constituents. Specifies maximum allowable concentrations (in mg/L for TCLP or mg/kg total) for each constituent before the waste can be land disposed. Multiple constituents may be listed with their respective limits.']
  waste_code varchar [note: 'EPA or state hazardous waste code identifier. Format follows EPA classification: D-codes (D001-D043 for characteristic wastes), F-codes (F001-F039 for non-specific source wastes), K-codes (K001-K181 for specific source wastes), P-codes (P001-P205 for acutely hazardous commercial chemical products), U-codes (U001-U411 for toxic commercial chemical products). This is the business identifier used across manifests, profiles, and regulatory reports.']
  waste_code_type varchar [note: 'Classification category of the waste code. D-listed are characteristic wastes (ignitability, corrosivity, reactivity, toxicity). F-listed are wastes from non-specific sources. K-listed are wastes from specific sources. P-listed are acutely hazardous discarded commercial chemical products. U-listed are toxic discarded commercial chemical products. State-specific codes are additional classifications imposed by state environmental agencies.']
  waste_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the waste stream or material covered by this code. Includes physical characteristics, chemical composition, source process, and typical examples. Used by generators and TSDFs to match waste streams to appropriate codes.']
  waste_minimization_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating applicable waste minimization or source reduction strategies for this waste stream. References EPA waste minimization guidance and best management practices. Used in biennial reports and pollution prevention planning.']

  Note: 'Reference table of EPA and state hazardous waste codes used to classify hazardous waste streams. Captures waste code (D001–D043, F-listed, K-listed, P-listed, U-listed), waste code description, hazard basis (ignitability, corrosivity, reactivity, toxicity, listed), regulatory citation (40 CFR Part 261 subpart), applicable treatment standards, state-specific code flag, and active status. Used across waste profiles, manifests, and regulatory reports for standardized waste classification.'
}

Table hazmat.dot_hazmat_classification {
  dot_hazmat_classification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the DOT hazardous materials classification record. Primary key.']
  surcharge_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.surcharge. Business justification: DOT hazmat classifications (hazard class, packing group, placard requirements) trigger specific billing surcharges (hazmat handling surcharge, placard surcharge, emergency response surcharge). Billing']
  cargo_aircraft_quantity_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum net quantity per package permitted on cargo-only aircraft. May be expressed as weight (kg), volume (L), or \'Forbidden\' if not permitted.']
  classification_effective_date date [note: 'Date when this classification entry became effective in the Hazardous Materials Regulations. Used to track regulatory changes over time.']
  classification_status varchar [note: 'Current regulatory status of this classification entry. Active = currently in force, Superseded = replaced by newer entry, Withdrawn = no longer valid.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this classification record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  erg_guide_number varchar [note: 'Three-digit guide number from the DOT Emergency Response Guidebook that provides first responders with initial action guidance for incidents involving this material.']
  excepted_quantity_code varchar [note: 'Code (E0-E5) indicating the maximum quantity per inner packaging permitted under excepted quantity provisions, which allow minimal regulatory requirements for very small quantities.']
  exceptions varchar [note: 'Reference to regulatory sections (e.g., 173.###) that provide exceptions or reduced requirements for limited quantities, small quantities, or specific transport modes.']
  forbidden_cargo_aircraft boolean [note: 'Indicates whether transport of this material on cargo-only aircraft is prohibited under any circumstances.']
  forbidden_passenger_aircraft boolean [note: 'Indicates whether transport of this material on passenger-carrying aircraft is prohibited under any circumstances.']
  hazard_class varchar [note: 'Primary hazard class or division (1-9) assigned to the material based on its predominant hazard. Examples: 1.1 (explosives), 2.1 (flammable gas), 3 (flammable liquid), 4.1 (flammable solid), 5.1 (oxidizer), 6.1 (toxic), 7 (radioactive), 8 (corrosive), 9 (miscellaneous).']
  hazard_division varchar [note: 'Subdivision within a hazard class that further categorizes the material by specific hazard characteristics (e.g., 1.1, 1.2, 2.1, 2.2, 6.1). May be null for classes without divisions.']
  inhalation_hazard_zone varchar [note: 'For materials that are poisonous by inhalation, the hazard zone (A, B, C, or D) indicating the degree of inhalation toxicity. Zone A is the most toxic.']
  label_code varchar [note: 'Code(s) identifying the required hazard warning label(s) that must be affixed to packages. Multiple labels may be required for materials with subsidiary hazards. Examples: 3 (flammable liquid), 6.1 (poison), 8 (corrosive).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this classification record was most recently updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  limited_quantity_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this material is eligible for limited quantity exceptions, which allow reduced packaging, marking, and labeling requirements when transported in small quantities.']
  limited_quantity_max_net varchar [note: 'Maximum net quantity per inner receptacle or article permitted under limited quantity provisions. Expressed as weight or volume.']
  marine_pollutant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material is designated as a marine pollutant under DOT and International Maritime Dangerous Goods (IMDG) Code regulations. Requires special marking and handling for water transport.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, clarifications, or special instructions related to this classification entry. May include references to specific regulatory amendments or interpretations.']
  packaging_authorizations varchar [note: 'Codes referencing the authorized packaging sections (e.g., 173.###) that specify which container types and specifications are approved for this material.']
  packing_group varchar [note: 'Degree of danger presented by the material during transport. I = high danger, II = medium danger, III = low danger. Used to determine packaging requirements and quantity limits.']
  passenger_aircraft_quantity_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum net quantity per package permitted on passenger-carrying aircraft. May be expressed as weight (kg), volume (L), or \'Forbidden\' if not permitted.']
  placard_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific placard design required for the transport vehicle. Examples: FLAMMABLE, CORROSIVE, POISON, OXIDIZER, DANGEROUS.']
  placard_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether placards must be displayed on the transport vehicle for this material when transported in specified quantities.']
  proper_shipping_name varchar [note: 'The official DOT-approved name used to describe the hazardous material on shipping papers, manifests, and labels. Must match the Hazardous Materials Table entry exactly.']
  reportable_quantity_lbs decimal(10,2) [note: 'The threshold quantity (in pounds) at or above which a spill or release of this material must be reported to the National Response Center under CERCLA. Null if no RQ applies.']
  severe_marine_pollutant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material is classified as a severe marine pollutant (PP designation) requiring enhanced environmental protection measures during marine transport.']
  special_provisions varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of special provision codes (e.g., B1, IB2, T4, TP1) that impose additional requirements or grant exceptions for packaging, handling, or transport of this material.']
  subsidiary_hazard_class varchar [note: 'Secondary hazard class(es) for materials that present more than one type of hazard. Multiple subsidiary classes may be listed, separated by commas.']
  superseded_by_un_na_number varchar [note: 'If this classification has been superseded, the UN/NA number of the replacement classification entry. Null if status is active.']
  technical_name_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the technical or chemical name of the hazardous constituent(s) must be entered in parentheses after the proper shipping name on shipping papers.']
  un_na_number varchar [note: 'Four-digit identification number assigned by the United Nations or North America to hazardous materials for international and domestic transport. Format: UN#### or NA####.']
  vessel_stowage_location varchar [note: 'Code indicating where the material must be stowed on a vessel (e.g., A = on deck only, B = under deck, C = on or under deck). Applies to marine transport.']
  vessel_stowage_other varchar [note: 'Additional stowage requirements or restrictions for marine transport, such as segregation from other materials, ventilation requirements, or temperature controls.']

  Note: 'Reference table of DOT Hazardous Materials classifications per 49 CFR Parts 171–180 (HMR). Captures hazard class/division (1–9), UN/NA number, proper shipping name, packing group (I/II/III), label requirements, placard requirements, special provisions, packaging authorizations, quantity limits for passenger/cargo aircraft, and marine pollutant flag. Used to populate manifest shipping descriptions and ensure DOT HMR compliance for all hazardous waste transport.'
}

Table hazmat.storage_unit {
  storage_unit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the hazardous waste storage unit. Primary key for the storage unit master record.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the TSDF facility or generator site where this storage unit is located. Links to the facility master record for site-level attributes.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.fixed_asset. Business justification: Hazmat storage units (tanks, containment vaults, satellite accumulation areas) are capitalized fixed assets. Linking enables capital project planning for storage unit upgrades, depreciation tracking, ']
  hazardous_waste_generator_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the hazardous waste generator that operates this storage unit. Applicable when the unit is at a generator site rather than a TSDF.']
  inspection_checklist_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.inspection_checklist. Business justification: RCRA mandates documented periodic inspections of hazmat storage units with standardized checklists. Linking storage_unit to asset.inspection_checklist assigns the regulatory-compliant inspection proto']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Storage units operate under facility permit with specific conditions (40 CFR 264/265 Subpart I). Permit defines storage capacity, waste codes, and time limits. Required for permit compliance verificat']
  tsdf_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.tsdf_facility. Business justification: storage_unit can exist at a generator site OR at a TSDF. Currently storage_unit links to asset.facility (via facility_id) and hazardous_waste_generator (for generator-side units). For TSDF-side storag']
  waste_stream_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.waste_stream. Business justification: Storage units are designated for specific waste streams; waste stream classification determines permitted waste codes, incompatibility restrictions, accumulation time limits, and inspection protocols.']
  building_location varchar [note: 'Physical building or structure identifier where the storage unit is located within the facility campus.']
  closure_certification_number varchar [note: 'Certification number issued by regulatory authority confirming that the storage unit closure was completed in accordance with RCRA requirements.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date when the storage unit was permanently closed and decommissioned. Applicable for units that have completed RCRA closure procedures.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this storage unit record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data governance.']
  current_inventory_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Current quantity of hazardous waste stored in the unit at the time of last inventory update. Measured in the unit of measure specified for this storage unit.']
  emergency_response_zone varchar [note: 'Emergency response zone designation for this storage unit within the facility emergency action plan. Used for HAZWOPER response coordination.']
  exemption_basis varchar [note: 'Regulatory basis under which this storage unit operates without a full RCRA permit. Identifies the specific exemption category that applies.']
  fire_suppression_system varchar [note: 'Type of fire suppression system installed to protect the storage unit. Required for certain hazardous waste storage configurations.']
  gps_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the storage unit location in decimal degrees format for spatial tracking and emergency response.']
  gps_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the storage unit location in decimal degrees format for spatial tracking and emergency response.']
  incompatible_waste_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of EPA waste codes that must not be stored in this unit due to incompatibility concerns. Used to prevent dangerous chemical reactions.']
  inspection_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Required frequency for routine inspections of the storage unit measured in days. Satellite accumulation areas require weekly inspections; 90-day areas require daily inspections per RCRA.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date when the storage unit was installed and commissioned for use. Used for tracking unit age and maintenance scheduling.']
  inventory_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure used for tracking inventory quantity in this storage unit. Must align with the capacity measurement unit.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent routine inspection performed on this storage unit. Used to track compliance with inspection frequency requirements.']
  maximum_capacity_gallons decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum permitted storage capacity of the unit measured in gallons. Applicable for liquid waste storage tanks and container storage areas. Regulatory limit that must not be exceeded.']
  maximum_capacity_pounds decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum permitted storage capacity of the unit measured in pounds. Applicable for solid waste storage areas and satellite accumulation points. Regulatory limit that must not be exceeded.']
  maximum_temperature_fahrenheit decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable temperature in Fahrenheit for safe storage of waste in this unit. Applicable when temperature control is required.']
  minimum_temperature_fahrenheit decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum allowable temperature in Fahrenheit for safe storage of waste in this unit. Applicable when temperature control is required.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this storage unit record was last modified. Audit trail for tracking changes to unit configuration or status.']
  next_inspection_due_date date [note: 'Calculated date when the next routine inspection is required based on inspection frequency. Used for compliance scheduling and alerts.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, special handling instructions, or historical information about the storage unit.']
  permitted_waste_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of EPA hazardous waste codes that are authorized for storage in this unit. Restricts the types of waste that may be placed in the unit.']
  ppe_requirements varchar [note: 'Description of personal protective equipment required for personnel working in or inspecting this storage unit. Based on hazard assessment of stored wastes.']
  room_area_designation varchar [note: 'Specific room number, area code, or zone designation within the building for precise location identification.']
  storage_time_limit_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days that hazardous waste may be stored in this unit before shipment is required. Varies by unit type: satellite accumulation has no time limit until 55 gallons accumulated; 90-day areas have 90-day limit.']
  temperature_control_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this storage unit requires active temperature control to maintain waste stability and prevent hazardous conditions. True for temperature-sensitive wastes.']
  unit_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or designation for the storage unit, used by facility personnel for identification and communication.']
  unit_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the storage unit, used in facility documentation, permits, and operational tracking. Externally visible unit designation.']
  unit_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the storage unit in its lifecycle. Active units are available for waste storage; inactive units are temporarily out of service; closed units have completed closure procedures.']
  unit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the storage unit based on RCRA regulatory categories. Determines applicable storage time limits, capacity restrictions, and operational requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: satellite_accumulation_area|90_day_accumulation_area|permitted_storage_tank|container_storage_building|drip_pad|containment_building|less_than_90_day_area — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  ventilation_system_type varchar [note: 'Type of ventilation system installed to control vapor accumulation and maintain air quality in the storage area. Required for volatile hazardous waste storage.']

  Note: 'Master record for permitted hazardous waste storage units at generator sites or TSDFs. Captures unit ID, unit type (satellite accumulation area, 90-day accumulation area, permitted storage tank, container storage building, drip pad, containment building), physical location, maximum permitted capacity (gallons/lbs), secondary containment type, inspection frequency requirement, permit or exemption basis, current inventory quantity, and unit status (active, closed, under inspection). Supports RCRA storage time limit tracking.'
}

Table hazmat.transporter_registration {
  transporter_registration_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the hazardous waste transporter registration record. Primary key.']
  epa_id_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hazmat.epa_id_registration. Business justification: Hazardous waste transporters must be EPA-registered. The epa_id_number string duplicates epa_id_registration data. Adding FK normalizes this relationship and provides a third inbound link to epa_id_re']
  hauling_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.hauling_agreement. Business justification: Registered hazmat transporters operate under hauling agreements defining routes, rates, insurance requirements, DOT compliance. Transporter assignment validates active agreement, insurance coverage, a']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the transporter was approved for use by Waste Management for hazardous waste transportation services.']
  approved_for_manifest_use boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this transporter is currently approved for use on hazardous waste manifests. False indicates transporter cannot be selected for new shipments.']
  authorized_dot_hazard_classes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of DOT hazard classes (1-9) that the transporter is authorized to transport based on DOT operating authority and vehicle placarding.']
  authorized_vehicle_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of vehicle types the transporter is authorized and equipped to operate for hazardous waste transport (e.g., tanker truck, box truck, flatbed, roll-off, vacuum truck).']
  authorized_waste_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of EPA hazardous waste codes (D, F, K, P, U series) that the transporter is authorized and equipped to handle based on permits, equipment, and training.']
  cargo_insurance_carrier varchar [note: 'Name of the insurance company providing cargo insurance coverage for hazardous waste in transit.']
  cargo_insurance_coverage_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cargo insurance coverage amount in USD for hazardous waste shipments in transit.']
  cargo_insurance_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the current cargo insurance policy expires. Transporter must maintain continuous coverage to remain approved.']
  cargo_insurance_policy_number varchar [note: 'Policy number for the cargo insurance coverage protecting hazardous waste shipments in transit.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the transporter company headquarters or principal place of business is located.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the transporter company headquarters. Typically USA for domestic transporters.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this transporter registration record was first created in the system.']
  dot_operating_authority_number varchar [note: 'DOT motor carrier operating authority number (MC, FF, or MX number) required for interstate hazardous materials transportation. Format: MC-XXXXXX or FF-XXXXXX.']
  emergency_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the 24/7 emergency response contact at the transporter company for incident response and HAZWOPER coordination.']
  emergency_contact_phone varchar [note: '24/7 emergency phone number for incident response and spill coordination. Must be monitored at all times during transport operations.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the transporter registration expires and must be renewed. Null for indefinite registrations subject to periodic review.']
  hazmat_employee_training_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the transporters HAZMAT employee training program per DOT HMR requirements. All drivers and handlers must receive training every 3 years.']
  hazmat_training_last_verified_date date [note: 'Date when Waste Management last verified that the transporter maintains compliant HAZMAT employee training records and certifications.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance and performance review conducted for this transporter.']
  liability_insurance_carrier varchar [note: 'Name of the insurance company providing hazardous waste transporter liability coverage for this transporter.']
  liability_insurance_coverage_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total liability insurance coverage amount in USD. Minimum coverage requirements vary by waste type and quantity transported.']
  liability_insurance_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the current liability insurance policy became effective.']
  liability_insurance_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the current liability insurance policy expires. Transporter must maintain continuous coverage to remain approved.']
  liability_insurance_policy_number varchar [note: 'Policy number for the transporter liability insurance coverage. Required for RCRA compliance and vendor approval.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this transporter registration record was last updated or modified.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Date when the next periodic compliance and performance review is scheduled for this transporter.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing special handling requirements, restrictions, preferred use cases, or other relevant information about the transporter.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'ZIP code or ZIP+4 code for the transporter company headquarters or principal place of business.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Business email address for the primary contact at the transporter company.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary business contact at the transporter company for coordination and communication.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Business phone number for the primary contact at the transporter company.']
  primary_contact_title varchar [note: 'Job title or role of the primary business contact at the transporter company.']
  registration_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the transporter registration. Active transporters are approved for use on manifests. Suspended or revoked transporters cannot be used.']
  safety_rating varchar [note: 'Current FMCSA safety rating assigned to the motor carrier based on compliance reviews and safety performance. Unsatisfactory rating may disqualify transporter.']
  state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code where the transporter company headquarters or principal place of business is located.']
  state_transporter_permit_numbers varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of state-specific hazardous waste transporter permit or license numbers. Some states require additional permits beyond EPA ID.']
  street_address varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the transporter company headquarters or principal place of business.']
  transporter_dba_name varchar [note: 'Trade name or doing-business-as name used by the transporter, if different from legal name.']
  transporter_legal_name varchar [note: 'The full legal business name of the hazardous waste transporter company as registered with EPA and state authorities.']
  usdot_number varchar [note: 'Unique USDOT identification number assigned by FMCSA for commercial motor vehicle operations. Required for all interstate carriers.']
  vendor_performance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Internal performance score (0-100) based on on-time performance, compliance incidents, customer feedback, and service quality metrics.']

  Note: 'Master record for EPA-registered hazardous waste transporters authorized to transport waste on behalf of Waste Management. Captures transporter EPA ID, company name, DOT operating authority number, state transporter permits, insurance certificate details, vehicle types authorized, waste types authorized, HAZMAT employee training compliance status, and approval status for use on manifests. Distinct from the fleet domain\'s vehicle records — this is the regulatory transporter entity identity.'
}

Table hazmat.waste_profile_code_listing {
  waste_profile_code_listing_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the waste_profile_code_listing association']
  waste_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the EPA or state hazardous waste code assigned to this profile.']
  waste_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the waste characterization profile to which this waste code applies.']
  basis_for_listing varchar [note: 'The specific reason or measured property that caused this waste code to apply to this waste profile. For characteristic codes (D-codes), this captures the measured property (e.g., flash point, pH, TCLP result). For listed codes (F/K/P/U), this captures the process or source that qualifies the waste. Belongs to the profile-code pairing, not to either entity alone.']
  concentration_threshold_mg_l decimal [note: 'The measured TCLP extract concentration in mg/L for this specific waste stream that triggered a toxicity characteristic waste code (D004–D043). This value is specific to the waste profile and the code pairing — the same waste code may be triggered at different concentrations across different profiles.']
  epa_waste_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of EPA hazardous waste codes (D-codes for characteristic, F-codes for non-specific source, K-codes for specific source, P/U-codes for commercial chemical products) assigned to this waste stream. [Moved from waste_profile: This denormalized STRING field stores a comma-separated list of all applicable waste codes for the profile — the direct symptom of the suppressed M:N relationship. Once the waste_profile_code_listing association table is created, all waste code assignments are normalized into individual records and this field becomes redundant and should be removed.]']
  primary_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this waste code is the primary (most significant) code for the waste profile. Replaces the denormalized primary_waste_code_id FK on waste_profile. Exactly one record per waste_profile should have this set to TRUE.']
  regulatory_citation_override varchar [note: 'State-specific or site-specific regulatory citation that supersedes the standard federal citation for this waste code as applied to this particular waste profile. Null when the standard federal citation from waste_code applies without modification.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Waste Code Listing between waste_profile and waste_code. It captures the regulatory assignment of each applicable EPA or state hazardous waste code to a specific waste characterization profile, as required by RCRA and documented on the EPA Uniform Hazardous Waste Manifest (Box 13). Each record links one waste_profile to one waste_code with attributes that exist only in the context of this specific profile-code pairing, including the measured concentration that triggered the code, the basis for listing, and whether this is the primary code for the profile.. Existence Justification: Under RCRA regulations, a single hazardous waste stream (waste_profile) must be characterized by ALL applicable EPA waste codes simultaneously — a drum of solvent waste may carry D001 (ignitability), D018 (benzene toxicity), and F005 (spent solvent) concurrently. Conversely, a single waste code (e.g., D001) applies to thousands of different waste profiles across different generators. This is a genuine operational M:N: the EPA manifest form (Box 13) explicitly lists multiple waste codes per waste stream, and the business actively manages which codes apply to each profile during the approval process.'
}

Table hazmat.storage_authorization {
  storage_authorization_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the storage_authorization association']
  storage_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the permitted storage unit in which the waste profile is authorized to be stored.']
  waste_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the hazardous waste characterization profile being authorized for storage.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which this waste profile was formally authorized for storage in this specific unit. Sourced from the detection phase relationship data.']
  approval_expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which this storage authorization expires and must be renewed. Sourced from the detection phase relationship data. Null if the authorization has no defined expiry.']
  approved_quantity_limit_lbs decimal [note: 'Maximum quantity in pounds of this waste profile that may be stored in this unit at any one time under this authorization. Belongs to the pairing, not to either entity alone. Sourced from the detection phase relationship data.']
  authorization_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this storage authorization. Tracks the lifecycle of the approval from active use through expiration or revocation.']
  compatibility_verified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal chemical and physical compatibility assessment was completed for this waste profile and storage unit pairing prior to authorization. Sourced from the detection phase relationship data.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Contract between waste_profile and storage_unit. It captures the formal RCRA-required authorization that permits a specific hazardous waste profile to be stored in a specific storage unit. Each record documents that a named waste stream has been evaluated for compatibility with a named unit, approved by the responsible compliance officer, and assigned a quantity limit and expiration date. Records are created during the waste profile approval workflow, updated when permit conditions change, and revoked when incompatibilities are identified or permits lapse.. Existence Justification: In RCRA-regulated hazardous waste operations, a waste profile must be explicitly approved for storage in specific units before waste can be placed there — a single waste profile may be authorized for multiple compatible storage units (e.g., a corrosive liquid approved for both a satellite accumulation area and a permitted storage tank), and a single storage unit may hold multiple approved waste profiles simultaneously. This compatibility authorization is an active, managed operational record: facility managers create it during the profile approval process, update it when permits change, and revoke it when incompatibilities are discovered. The relationship carries its own data (approval date, quantity limits, compatibility verification, expiration) that belongs to neither the profile nor the unit alone.'
}

Table contract.contract {
  contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for contract']
  agreement_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.agreement_type. Business justification: contract has contract_type (STRING) attribute which is semantically equivalent to agreement_type in this domain context. The agreement_type reference table provides classification for all contract and']
  bundle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.bundle. Business justification: Contracts are sold against service bundles (e.g., combined MSW+recycling+organics package). Linking contract to bundle enables bundle-level revenue reporting, renewal analytics, and sales performance ']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer party to this contract. Links to the customer master record.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.facility. Business justification: Contracts specify service delivery at facilities (landfills, MRFs, transfer stations). Required for facility capacity planning, contract performance tracking, regulatory compliance reporting, and reve']
  offering_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.offering. Business justification: Master contracts specify primary service offering being sold (residential collection, commercial recycling, hazmat). Needed for contract template selection, pricing validation, service eligibility che']
  parent_agreement_contract_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the master or umbrella agreement under which this contract is subordinated. Used for multi-site or multi-service contract hierarchies. Nullable for standalone contracts.']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Waste collection/disposal contracts must reference operating permits authorizing contracted services. Contract execution requires permit verification; service scope cannot exceed permitted capacity. E']
  amendment_count varchar [note: 'Number of formal amendments executed against this contract since origination. Used to track contract change history and version control.']
  annual_escalation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Fixed percentage rate increase applied annually to the base service rate. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 3.00 for 3% annual increase). Nullable if escalation is CPI-based or not applicable.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract automatically renews at expiration unless either party provides notice. True for auto-renewing contracts, False for fixed-term agreements requiring explicit renewal.']
  base_service_rate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Primary recurring charge amount for the contracted service. Currency is USD unless otherwise specified in contract terms. Excludes variable charges, surcharges, and taxes.']
  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which invoices are generated under this contract. Monthly for recurring monthly billing, Quarterly for quarterly invoicing, Annual for yearly billing, Per Service for event-based billing.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the contract. Used in customer communications, invoicing, and legal documentation.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the contract. Draft for contracts under negotiation, Pending for signed but not yet effective, Active for in-force agreements, Suspended for temporarily paused contracts, Amended for contracts with modifications, Terminated for ended agreements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this contract record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  early_termination_penalty decimal(15,2) [note: 'Financial penalty amount charged if the customer terminates the contract before the expiration date. May be a fixed amount or calculated as a percentage of remaining contract value.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the contract terms become binding and service obligations commence. Used for billing start date and service level agreement (SLA) enforcement.']
  environmental_compliance_requirement varchar [note: 'Description of environmental regulations and compliance obligations specified in the contract. May reference Resource Conservation and Recovery Act (RCRA), Clean Air Act (CAA), or state-specific environmental requirements.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the contract term ends and obligations cease unless renewed. Nullable for evergreen or indefinite-term contracts.']
  force_majeure_clause varchar [note: 'Description of the circumstances (natural disasters, pandemics, war, government action) that excuse non-performance of contract obligations without penalty.']
  governing_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Legal jurisdiction whose laws govern the contract interpretation and dispute resolution. Typically a state or province code (e.g., TX, CA, ON) or country code for international contracts.']
  indemnification_clause varchar [note: 'Description of the indemnification obligations where one party agrees to protect the other from specified liabilities, claims, or damages arising from contract performance.']
  insurance_requirement varchar [note: 'Description of the insurance coverage types and minimum limits required under the contract. Typically includes general liability, auto liability, workers compensation, and environmental impairment liability.']
  last_amendment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent contract amendment execution. Nullable if no amendments have been executed.']
  late_payment_penalty_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage rate charged on overdue balances per month or per annum as specified in contract terms. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 1.50 for 1.5% monthly).']
  maximum_tonnage_limit decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum volume of waste in tons that the facility or service provider will accept under the contract. Common in landfill disposal agreements. Nullable for unlimited contracts.']
  minimum_tonnage_commitment decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum volume of waste in tons that the customer commits to deliver under the contract term. Common in disposal and hauling agreements. Nullable for contracts without volume commitments.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this contract record was last updated. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and data synchronization.']
  owner_name varchar [note: 'Name of the Waste Management employee responsible for managing this contract relationship. Typically a sales representative, account manager, or municipal services manager.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from invoice date that payment is due. Common values are 15, 30, or 60 days. Used for accounts receivable (AR) aging and collections management.']
  performance_bond_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of the performance bond required under the contract. Nullable if no bond is required.']
  performance_bond_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract requires the service provider to post a performance bond to guarantee service delivery. Common in municipal and franchise agreements.']
  rate_escalation_clause varchar [note: 'Description of the pricing adjustment mechanism over the contract term. May reference Consumer Price Index (CPI), fixed percentage increases, or fuel surcharge formulas.']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days prior to expiration that either party must provide notice to prevent auto-renewal or initiate renewal discussions. Typically 30, 60, or 90 days.']
  salesforce_opportunity_code varchar [note: 'Salesforce CRM opportunity identifier that originated this contract. Links contract to the sales pipeline and opportunity record for revenue attribution and sales performance tracking.']
  sap_contract_number varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA Sales and Distribution (SD) module contract document number. Used for integration with SAP billing, order management, and financial accounting.']
  service_level_agreement varchar [note: 'Description of the performance obligations and service commitments defined in the contract. May include pickup frequency, response times, container delivery standards, and quality metrics.']
  signed_date date [note: 'Date when all parties executed the contract. Used for legal enforceability and contract lifecycle tracking. May differ from effective date.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record that originated this contract data. Salesforce for CRM-originated contracts, SAP for ERP-originated contracts, AMCS for operations platform contracts, Manual for legacy or paper contracts.']
  term_months varchar [note: 'Duration of the contract term expressed in months. Common values are 12, 24, 36, or 60 months. Used for calculating expiration dates and renewal cycles.']
  termination_clause varchar [note: 'Description of the conditions and procedures under which either party may terminate the contract prior to expiration. Includes notice requirements, cause provisions, and early termination penalties.']
  termination_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance notice required for contract termination. Typically 30, 60, or 90 days depending on contract type and jurisdiction.']
  value_total decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total estimated revenue value of the contract over its full term. Calculated from base rates, estimated volumes, and contract duration. Used for revenue forecasting and sales performance metrics.']

  Note: 'Master record for all service agreements and contracts between Waste Management and its customers. Covers MSW municipal contracts, commercial service agreements, hauling contracts, disposal agreements, and franchise agreements. Stores contract number, type, status, effective/expiration dates, auto-renewal flags, governing jurisdiction, and Salesforce CRM opportunity reference. Single source of truth for contract identity and lifecycle stage (origination, active, amended, renewed, terminated). Integrates with Salesforce CRM and SAP SD.'
}

Table contract.agreement_type {
  agreement_type_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the agreement type reference record. Primary key.']
  line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.line. Business justification: Agreement types map to service lines (residential collection agreement type → residential service line; hazardous waste agreement type → hazardous service line). The service_category plain text column']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: Agreement types are configured per customer segment (residential, commercial, hazmat, municipal). Contract setup and eligibility validation require resolving the segment governing an agreement type. a']
  waste_stream_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.waste_stream. Business justification: Agreement types in waste management are categorized by waste stream (MSW collection agreement, recycling processing agreement, hazardous waste agreement). The waste_stream_type plain text column is a ']
  active_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this agreement type is currently active and available for new contract creation. Inactive types are retained for historical reference but not used for new agreements.']
  allows_early_termination boolean [note: 'Indicates whether agreements of this type permit early termination by either party, typically subject to notice periods and potential penalties.']
  amendment_approval_level varchar [note: 'Organizational approval authority level required to amend or modify agreements of this type. Drives contract workflow routing in Salesforce CRM.']
  auto_renewal_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether agreements of this type are eligible for automatic renewal upon expiration, subject to notice periods and customer consent.']
  compliance_framework varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory compliance framework governing agreements of this type (e.g., RCRA for hazardous waste, local franchise ordinances for MSW, CERCLA for remediation contracts).']
  contract_duration_type varchar [note: 'Typical duration classification for agreements of this type. Short-term (under 1 year), long-term (multi-year), evergreen (auto-renewing), project-based (tied to specific project completion), or indefinite (no fixed end date).']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that created this agreement type reference record. Supports audit and accountability.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this agreement type reference record was first created in the system. Audit trail field.']
  default_billing_frequency varchar [note: 'Standard billing cycle frequency for agreements of this type. Drives invoice generation scheduling in Oracle Revenue Management.']
  default_term_months varchar [note: 'Standard initial contract term length in months for agreements of this type. Null if term is project-based or indefinite.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this agreement type was retired or superseded. Null if currently active. Supports temporal validity tracking.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this agreement type became available for use in contract creation. Supports temporal validity tracking.']
  hazmat_classification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether agreements of this type require hazardous material classification and manifest tracking per Resource Conservation and Recovery Act (RCRA) and Department of Transportation (DOT) regulations.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that last modified this agreement type reference record. Supports audit and accountability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this agreement type reference record was last updated. Audit trail field.']
  pricing_model varchar [note: 'Standard pricing structure used for agreements of this type. Flat rate (fixed fee), per ton (weight-based), per pickup (service event-based), tiered (volume-based brackets), cost-plus (cost recovery plus margin), or negotiated (custom pricing).']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'Standard frequency for regulatory or customer reporting required under agreements of this type (e.g., tonnage reports, diversion rate reports, manifest submissions).']
  requires_franchise_approval boolean [note: 'Indicates whether agreements of this type require municipal or local government franchise approval before execution. Critical for Municipal Solid Waste (MSW) franchise agreements.']
  requires_insurance_certificate boolean [note: 'Indicates whether agreements of this type require the service provider to maintain and provide proof of general liability, environmental liability, and workers compensation insurance.']
  requires_performance_bond boolean [note: 'Indicates whether agreements of this type require the service provider to post a performance bond or financial guarantee to ensure contract obligations are met. Common for municipal franchise agreements.']
  requires_permit boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this agreement type requires an active operating permit (e.g., Resource Conservation and Recovery Act (RCRA) permit for hazardous waste, landfill operating permit, Treatment Storage and Disposal Facility (TSDF) permit).']
  sla_template_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether agreements of this type must include a formal Service Level Agreement (SLA) defining performance obligations, response times, and service quality metrics.']
  supports_rate_escalation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether agreements of this type typically include automatic rate escalation clauses tied to Consumer Price Index (CPI), fuel surcharges, or other economic indices.']
  termination_notice_days varchar [note: 'Standard advance notice period in days required for either party to terminate agreements of this type without cause. Null if termination is not permitted or requires cause.']
  type_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the agreement type (e.g., MSW_FRAN, COMM_HAUL, ROLLOFF, HAZ_DISP). Used as business key in operational systems and contract workflows.']
  type_description varchar [note: 'Detailed business description of the agreement type, including scope of services, typical customer segments, and operational characteristics.']
  type_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the agreement type (e.g., Municipal Solid Waste Franchise Agreement, Commercial Hauling Contract, Roll-Off Disposal Agreement, Hazardous Waste Treatment Storage and Disposal Facility Agreement).']

  Note: 'Reference classification of contract and agreement types used across Waste Management\'s commercial and municipal operations. Includes types such as MSW Municipal Franchise, Commercial Hauling, Roll-Off Disposal, Residential Service, Hazardous Waste Disposal, and Intermodal Transfer. Drives contract workflow routing, SLA assignment, and billing rule selection.'
}

Essential for asset deployment tracking, service fulfillment verification, billing reconciliation, and container lifecycle management.']

  container_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.container_type. Business justification: Service commitments specify exact container equipment for delivery and billing. Direct link needed for asset allocation, delivery scheduling, capacity validation, rental billing, and maintenance track']

Direct link required for route optimization, driver scheduling, SLA compliance tracking, and billing verification. Removes d']
  landfill_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to landfill.site. Business justification: Service commitments for disposal services specify destination landfill site for capacity reservation and routing. Required for site capacity planning, contract fulfillment tracking, and customer servi']
  offering_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.offering. Business justification: Service commitments in contracts should reference the service catalog offering to ensure contractual terms align with defined service offerings. This enables validation that contracted services match ']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Service commitments for hazmat/special waste must align with facility permits authorizing specific waste streams at service locations. Operations teams verify permit coverage before activating service']
  pricing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.pricing. Business justification: contract_service_commitment defines specific service obligations with associated charges (monthly_service_charge, per_pickup_charge, overage_charge_rate). These charges are governed by a pricing recor']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Service commitments for hazardous, medical, or special waste streams must reference the regulatory requirement governing the service. This supports compliance verification during service delivery, reg']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the collection route assigned to fulfill this service commitment.']
  service_rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.service_rate_schedule. Business justification: Service commitments are priced against a service rate schedule (defining base rates, fuel surcharges, environmental fees by area/container/frequency). This link enables rate schedule auditing, pricing']

  waste_stream_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.waste_stream. Business justification: Every service commitment is for a specific waste stream (MSW, recycling, organics, hazardous). This is a fundamental operational attribute — waste stream determines handling requirements, regulatory c']
  activated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the service commitment transitioned to active status and service delivery commenced.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service commitment automatically renews at the end of its term.']

  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this service commitment.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the service commitment expires or is scheduled to terminate. Null for open-ended commitments.']
